member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.AppBase.OnInitialize(Sdl3Sharp.Sdl,System.String[])
member.summary: The initial entry point for an 's execution
member.param: The instance that started the execution of this
A further processed collection of arguments passed from or
member.returns:
How to proceed with the operation of this 's execution:
Terminate the execution with an error
Terminate the execution with success
Continue with the execution

remarks.para: This method is called once, at the start of the 's execution. It should initialize whatever is necessary, possibly create windows and open audio devices, etc.
This method should not go into an infinite loop; it should do any one-time setup it requires and then return.

The parameter contains almost the same collection of arguments as the caller passed to or . But notice that SDL does further (sometimes platform depended) processing on those arguments between the calls to those methods and the call to this method. You should treat those arguments as you normally would main entry point arguments.

If this method returns , the 's execution will proceed to normal operation, and will begin receiving repeated calls to and for the execution time of the . If this method returns , will be called immediately and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for program that reports an error to the platform. If it returns , will be called immediately and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for the program that reports success to the platform.
This method is called by SDL on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.AppBase.OnIterate(Sdl3Sharp.Sdl)
member.summary: The iteration entry point for an 's execution
member.param: The instance that started the execution of this
member.returns:
How to proceed with the operation of this 's execution:
Terminate the execution with an error
Terminate the execution with success
Continue with the execution

remarks.para: This method is called repeatedly after returned . The method should operate as a single iteration the 's primary loop; it should update whatever state it needs and draw a new frame of video, usually.

On some platforms, this method will be called at the refresh rate of the display (which might change during the lifetime of your app!). There are no promises made about what frequency this method might run at. You should use the functionality (e.g. ) if you need to see how much time has passed since the last iteration.
There is no need to process events during this method; events will be send to as they arrive, and in most cases the event queue will be empty when this method runs anyhow.
This method should not go into an infinite loop; it should do one iteration of whatever it needs to do and return.

If this method returns , the 's execution will continue normal operation, receiving repeated calls to and for the execution time of the . If this method returns , will be called immediately and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for program that reports an error to the platform. If it returns , will be called immediately and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for the program that reports success to the platform.
This method is called by SDL on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.AppBase.OnEvent(Sdl3Sharp.Sdl,Sdl3Sharp.Events.Event@)
member.summary: The event handling entry point for an 's execution
member.param: The instance that started the execution of this
A reference to the newly arrived event to examine
member.returns:
How to proceed with the operation of this 's execution:
Terminate the execution with an error
Terminate the execution with success
Continue with the execution

remarks.para: This method is called as needed after returned . It is called once for each new event.

There is (currently) no guarantee about what thread this will be called from; whatever thread pushes an event onto SDL's event queue will trigger a call to this method. SDL is responsible for pumping the event queue between each call to , so in normal operation one should only get events in a serial fashion, but be careful if you have a thread that explicitly calls . SDL itself will push events to the queue on the main thread.
s sent to this method by reference are not owned (by you), nor are those references required to be alive after the call to this method; if you need to save the data, you should copy it!
This method should not go into an infinite loop; it should handle the provided event appropriately and return.

If this method returns , the 's execution will continue normal operation, receiving repeated calls to and for the execution time of the . If this method returns , will be called and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for program that reports an error to the platform. If it returns , will be called and terminate the 's execution with a return value that can be used as an exit code for the program that reports success to the platform.
This method may get called concurrently with or for events not pushed from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.AppBase.OnQuit(Sdl3Sharp.Sdl,Sdl3Sharp.AppResult)
member.summary: The deinitialising entry point for an 's execution
member.param: The instance that started the execution of this
The that terminated the execution of this ( or )

remarks.para: This method is called once before terminating the execution of the .
This method will be called no matter what, even if requests termination.
This method should not go into an infinite loop; it should deinitialize any resources necessary, perform whatever shutdown activities, and return.

You do not need to dispose the instance in this method, as that will be done automatically it after this method returns, but it is safe to do so (with an exception regarding concurrent calls to which might receive the disposed instance by then). The instance is disposed after the call to this method no matter what. This is unavoidable!
This method is called by SDL on the main thread, though may get called concurrently with this method if other threads that push events are still active.

member.summary: Sets the SDL error message for the current thread
member.param: The error message to be set
member.returns: (always)

remarks.para: Calling this method will replace any previous error message that was set for this thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Error.Set(System.String,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Object})
member.summary: Sets the SDL error message to a string for the current thread
member.param: The C-style printf format string
The arguments corresponding to the format specifiers in
member.returns: (always)

remarks.para: Calling this method will replace any previous error message that was set for this thread.

The parameter is interpreted as a C-style printf format string, and the parameter supplies the values for its format specifiers. Supported argument types include all integral types up to 64-bit (including and ), floating point types ( and ), pointer types ( and ), and .
For a detailed explanation of C-style printf format strings and their specifiers, see .
Consider using instead when possible, as it may be more efficient. In many cases, you can use C# string interpolation to construct the message before logging.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Error.TryGet(System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to retrieve a message about the last error that occurred on the current thread
member.param: A message with information about the specific error that occurred, or if there hasn't been an error message set since the last call to
member.returns: if there was a non- error ; otherwise

remarks.para: It is possible for multiple errors to occur before calling . Only the last error is returned.

The message is only applicable when an SDL has signaled an error. You must check the return values of SDL calls to determine when to appropriately call . You should not use the result of to decide if an error has occurred! Sometimes SDL will set an error string even when reporting success.
SDL will not clear the error message for successful API calls. You must check for failure cases before you can assume the error message applies.
Error messages are set per-thread, so an error set in a different thread will not interfere with the current thread's operation.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Error.SDL_ClearError
member.summary: Clear any previous error message for this thread
member.returns: Returns true

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Error.SDL_GetError
member.summary: Retrieve a message about the last error that occurred on the current thread
member.returns: Returns a message with information about the specific error that occurred, or an empty string if there hasn't been an error message set since the last call to SDL_ClearError()

remarks.para: It is possible for multiple errors to occur before calling SDL_GetError(). Only the last error is returned.

The message is only applicable when an SDL function has signaled an error. You must check the return values of SDL function calls to determine when to appropriately call SDL_GetError(). You should not use the results of SDL_GetError() to decide if an error has occurred! Sometimes SDL will set an error string even when reporting success.
SDL will not clear the error string for successful API calls. You must check return values for failure cases before you can assume the error string applies.
Error strings are set per-thread, so an error set in a different thread will not interfere with the current thread's operation.

The returned value is a thread-local string which will remain valid until the current thread's error string is changed. The caller should make a copy if the value is needed after the next SDL API call.

member.returns: Returns false

remarks.para: This function does not do any memory allocation

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.Event
member.summary: Represents a general event in SDL

remarks.para: Even though SDL's *Event structures do not use inheritance (as they are all s or s), you may think of as a common base representation of all the other *Event structures.

More specifically: all of the other *Event structures can be represented as an (this is usually achieved through ), while an structure could potentially be represented as one of the *Event structures, depending on it's (this can be achieved through , or copyless through or )

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.EventExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for and other *Event structures

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Events.EventExtensions.SDL_ConvertEventToRenderCoordinates(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Events.Event*)
member.summary: Convert the coordinates in an event to render coordinates
member.param: The rendering context
The event to modify
member.returns: Returns true if the event is converted or doesn't need conversion, or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
This takes into account several states:
The window dimensions
The logical presentation settings (SDL_SetRenderLogicalPresentation)
The scale (SDL_SetRenderScale)
The viewport (SDL_SetRenderViewport)
Various event types are converted with this function: mouse, touch, pen, etc.
Touch coordinates are converted from normalized coordinates in the window to non-normalized rendering coordinates.

Relative mouse coordinates (xrel and yrel event fields) are also converted. Applications that do not want these fields converted should use SDL_RenderCoordinatesFromWindow() on the specific event fields instead of converting the entire event structure.
Once converted, coordinates may be outside the rendering area.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Events.EventExtensions.<G>$BB5DEC109F6739F27EB2438E96CE3D2D`1.TryConvertToRenderCoordinates``1(``0)
member.summary: Tries to convert the coordinates of an event to render coordinates, in place
member.typeparam: The type of renderer to convert the event for
member.param: The renderer to convert the event for
member.returns: , if the conversion was successful and the event was modified in place; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method modifies the event in place. If you want to keep the original event, you should make a copy of it beforehand.

The conversion takes into account several factors:
The dimensions
The settings
The settings
The settings
Various event types can be converted with this method, including , , , , , , etc.
Touch coordinates are converted from normalized coordinates in the window to non-normalized rendering coordinates.

Relative mouse coordinates (e.g. and ) are also converted. Applications that do not want these coordinates to be converted should use the method with the specific property values of the event instead of converting the entire event.
Converted coordinates may be outside of the bound of the current rendering area.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.GamepadSensorEvent.Data
member.summary: Gets or sets the data from the sensor
member.value: The data from the sensor, as a span of three values

remarks.para: Notice that this property always returns a . If you want to modify the data, you can either set the entire event data by setting this property from a different span, or modify the individual , , and properties.

Notice that when setting this property, the given values of the given span will be copied into the event data. That means you can assign spans that are larger than three elements, in which case only the first three elements will be copied. If you try to assign a span that is smaller than three elements, this property will throw an .

member.exception: When setting this property, the length of the given argument is less than 3

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.GamepadSensorEvent.Data0
member.summary: Gets or sets the first data value from the sensor
member.value: The first data value from the sensor

remarks.para: The value of this property is the same as Data[0].
If you want to retrieve the entire event data from the sensor at once, consider using the property instead.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.GamepadSensorEvent.Data1
member.summary: Gets or sets the second data value from the sensor
member.value: The second data value from the sensor

remarks.para: The value of this property is the same as Data[1].
If you want to retrieve the entire event data from the sensor at once, consider using the property instead.

member.summary: Gets or sets the third data value from the sensor
member.value: The third data value from the sensor

remarks.para: The value of this property is the same as Data[2].

member.value: The timestamp of the sensor reading, in nanoseconds

remarks.para: The value of this property is not necessarily synchronized with the system's clock.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.MouseWheelEvent.X
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount scrolled horizontally
member.value: The amount scrolled horizontally

remarks.para: If the value of this property is positive, the mouse wheel was scrolled to the right. If the value is negative, the mouse wheel was scrolled to the left.
Notice that if the value of the property is , the value of this property is inverted. You might want to multiply this value by -1 in that case to change it back.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.MouseWheelEvent.Y
member.summary: Gets or sets the amount scrolled vertically
member.value: The amount scrolled vertically

remarks.para: If the value of this property is positive, the mouse wheel was scrolled away from the user. If the value is negative, the mouse wheel was scrolled towards the user.
Notice that if the value of the property is , the value of this property is inverted. You might want to multiply this value by -1 in that case to change it back.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.MouseWheelEvent.Direction
member.summary: Gets or sets the direction type of the mouse wheel scrolling
member.value: The direction type of the mouse wheel scrolling

remarks.para: Notice that if the value of this property is , the values of the and properties are inverted. You might want to multiply those values by -1 in that case to change them back.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.PenProximityEvent
member.summary: Represents an event that occurs when a pen enters or leaves proximity

remarks.para: When a pen becomes visible to the system (it is close enough to a tablet, etc), SDL will send a event with the new pen's ID. This ID is valid until the pen leaves proximity again (has been removed from the tablet's area, the tablet has been unplugged, etc). If the same pen reenters proximity again, it will be given a new ID.
Note that "proximity" means "close enough for the tablet to know the tool is there." The pen touching and lifting off from the tablet while not leaving the area are handled by and events ().
Notice that not all platforms have a window associated with the pen during proximity events. Some wait until motion/button/etc. events to offer this info.
Associated s:

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.PenProximityEvent.Type

remarks.para: When setting this property, the value must be either or . Otherwise, it will lead the property to throw an !

member.value: The window Id of the with pen focus, if any, or 0

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.PenProximityEvent.PenId
member.summary: Gets or sets the pen instance ID for the associated with the event
member.value: The pen instance ID for the associated with the event

remarks.value: If a pen leaves proximity and later re-enters proximity, it may be assigned a different device ID.

member.summary: Gets or sets the sensor device ID for the being updated

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.SensorEvent.Data
member.summary: Gets or sets the data from the sensor
member.value: The data from the sensor, as a span of six values

remarks.para: Notice that this property always returns a . If you want to modify the data, you can either set the entire event data by setting this property from a different span, or modify the individual , , …, and properties.

Notice that when setting this property, the given values of the given span will be copied into the event data. That means you can assign spans that are larger than six elements, in which case only the first six elements will be copied. If you try to assign a span that is smaller than six elements, this property will throw an .

member.exception: When setting this property, the length of the given argument is less than 6

member.summary: Gets or sets the first data value from the sensor
member.value: The first data value from the sensor

If you want to retrieve the entire event data from the sensor at once, consider using the property instead.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TextEditingEvent.Start
member.summary: Gets or set the starting cursor of the selected editing text
member.value: The starting cursor of the selected editing text, as a position in number of characters, or -1 if not set

remarks.para: The starting cursor is the position, in number of characters, where new typing will be inserted into the editing text.

Reading and writing this property can be very expensive, you should consider caching it's value. If you want to get the , , and simultaneously, consider using the method as it's way more efficient when getting all three quantities at once.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TextEditingEvent.Length
member.summary: Gets or sets the length of the selected editing text
member.value: The length of the selected editing text, as a length in number of characters, or -1 if not set

remarks.para: The length is the number of characters that will be replaced by new typing.

Reading and writing this property can be very expensive, you should consider caching it's value. If you want to get the , , and simultaneously, consider using the method as it's way more efficient when getting all three quantities at once.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TextEditingEvent.GetTextStartAndLength
member.summary: Gets the editing text, the starting cursor of the selected editing text, and the length of the selected editing text simultaneously
member.returns: The editing text, the starting cursor of the selected editing text, as a position in number of characters, or -1 if not set, and length of the selected editing text, as a length in number of characters, or -1 if not set

remarks.para: The starting cursor is the position, in number of characters, where new typing will be inserted into the editing text.
The resulting length is the number of characters that will be replaced by new typing.
Calling this method can be very expensive, you should consider caching it's return values.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent
member.summary: Represents an event that occurs when a finger is placed on, moved on, lifted from, or canceled on a touch device

remarks.para: Associated s:

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.Type

remarks.para: When setting this property, the value must be either , , , or . Otherwise, it will lead the property to throw an !

member.exception: When setting this property, the value was neither , , , nor

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.TouchId
member.summary: Gets or sets the touch device ID for the associated with the event
member.value: The touch device ID for the associated with the event

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.FingerId
member.summary: Gets or sets the finger ID for the associated with the event
member.value: The finger ID for the associated with the event

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.X
member.summary: Gets or sets the X position of the finger on the touch device
member.value: The X position of the finger on the touch device, normalized between 0 and 1, where 0 is the left edge and 1 is the right edge

remarks.para: Note that while the coordinates are normalized, they are not clamped, which means in some circumstances you can get a value outside of this range. For example, a renderer using logical presentation might give a negative value when the touch is in the letterboxing. Some platforms might report a touch outside of the window, which will also be outside of the range.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.Y
member.summary: Gets or sets the Y position of the finger on the touch device
member.value: The Y position of the finger on the touch device, normalized between 0 and 1, where 0 is the top edge and 1 is the bottom edge

remarks.para: Note that while the coordinates are normalized, they are not clamped, which means in some circumstances you can get a value outside of this range. For example, a renderer using logical presentation might give a negative value when the touch is in the letterboxing. Some platforms might report a touch outside of the window, which will also be outside of the range.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Events.TouchFingerEvent.DX
member.summary: Gets or sets the change in X position of the finger on the touch device
member.value: The change in X position of the finger on the touch device, normalized between -1 and 1, where -1 is a tranversal all the way from the right edge to the left edge, and 1 is a transversal all the way from the left edge to the right edge

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.UserEvent
member.summary: Represents an user defined event

remarks.para: An is unique; it is never created by SDL, but only by the application. The event can be pushed onto the event queue using . The contents of the structure members are completely up to the programmer; the only requirement is that it's is a registered user defined event type obtained from or .

If you want to populate the managed user defined data slots, and , you'll need to handle their lifetimes via an instance. You can then use it's method to obtain an where you'll be able to use it's managed user defined data slots, and .
If you don't intend to use non-blittable or managed data, you can always use the raw user defined data slots, and , freely.

Note: You shouldn't access the any of the raw user defined data slots when you use the corresponding managed user defined data slots. E.g., overwriting the value of will lead to a loss of data in and won't be accessable anymore.
Associated s: User defined s registered using or .

member.value: The user defined event code

remarks.para: You should only access this property if the user defined event is backed by a . If it's not, or the is already disposed, accessing this property will lead to a been thrown.
You can freely set this to anything of your liking as long as the is backed by a .
Note: If you intend to use this property, don't overwrite the value of it's associated raw user defined data slot, . If you do, that will lead to a loss of data in and won't be accessable anymore.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Events.UserEventLifetime
member.summary: Represents the lifetime of a , allowing to store managed data associated with it

remarks.para: An allows to create s that are able to participate in SDL's event system, while still being able to store managed data in their and properties.

s restrict the lifetime of managed data associated with s created from them. Keep the instance alive as long as you need to access the managed data from the s created from it. Once you're sure that no more managed data access is needed, you can and should dispose the instance.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AllowAltTabWhileGrabbed
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the behavior of Alt+Tab while the keyboard is grabbed
member.value: A hint specifing the behavior of Alt+Tab while the keyboard is grabbed

remarks.para: By default, SDL emulates Alt+Tab functionality while the keyboard is grabbed and your window is full-screen. This prevents the user from getting stuck in your application if you've enabled keyboard grab.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" SDL will not handle Alt+Tab. Your application is responsible for handling Alt+Tab while the keyboard is grabbed.
"1" SDL will minimize your window when Alt+Tab is pressed (default)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Android
member.summary: SDL_HINT_ANDROID_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Android.AllowRecreateActivity
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether the SDL activity is allowed to be re-created
member.value: A hint to control whether the SDL activity is allowed to be re-created

remarks.para: If this hint is true, the activity can be recreated on demand by the OS, and Java static data and C++ static data remain with their current values. If this hint is false, then SDL will call exit() when you return from your main function and the application will be terminated and then started fresh each time.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" The application starts fresh at each launch (default)
"1" The application activity can be recreated by the OS
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Android.BlockOnPause
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether the event loop will block itself when the app is paused
member.value: A hint to control whether the event loop will block itself when the app is paused

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Non blocking
"1" Blocking (default)
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Android.LowLatencyAudio
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether low latency audio should be enabled
member.value: A hint to control whether low latency audio should be enabled

remarks.para: Some devices have poor quality output when this is enabled, but this is usually an improvement in audio latency.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Low latency audio is not enabled
"1" Low latency audio is enabled (default)
This hint should be set before SDL audio is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Android.TrapBackButton
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether we trap the Android back button to handle it manually
member.value: A hint to control whether we trap the Android back button to handle it manually

remarks.para: This is necessary for the right mouse button to work on some Android devices, or to be able to trap the back button for use in your code reliably. If this hint is true, the back button will show up as an / pair with a keycode of .

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Back button will be handled as usual for system (default)
"1" Back button will be trapped, allowing you to handle the key press manually (This will also let right mouse click work on systems where the right mouse button functions as back)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AppId
member.summary: Gets a hint setting the app ID string
member.value: A hint setting the app ID string

remarks.para: This string is used by desktop compositors to identify and group windows together, as well as match applications with associated desktop settings and icons.
This will override , if set by the application.
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AppName
member.summary: Gets a hint setting the application name
member.value: A hint setting the application name

remarks.para: This hint lets you specify the application name sent to the OS when required. For example, this will often appear in volume control applets for audio streams, and in lists of applications which are inhibiting the screensaver. You should use a string that describes your program ("My Game 2: The Revenge").
This will override , if set by the application.
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AppleTV
member.summary: SDL_HINT_APPLE_TV_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AppleTV.ControllerUIEvents
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether controllers used with the Apple TV generate UI events
member.value: A hint controlling whether controllers used with the Apple TV generate UI events

remarks.para: When UI events are generated by controller input, the app will be backgrounded when the Apple TV remote's menu button is pressed, and when the pause or B buttons on gamepads are pressed.
More information about properly making use of controllers for the Apple TV can be found here: .

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Controller input does not generate UI events (default)
"1" Controller input generates UI events
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AppleTV.RemoteAllowRotation
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the Apple TV remote's joystick axes will automatically match the rotation of the remote
member.value: A hint controlling whether the Apple TV remote's joystick axes will automatically match the rotation of the remote

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Remote orientation does not affect joystick axes (default)
"1" Joystick axes are based on the orientation of the remote
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Assert
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling response to failures
member.value: A hint controlling response to failures

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following case-sensitive values:
"abort" Program terminates immediately
"break" Program triggers a debugger breakpoint
"retry" Program reruns the 's test again
"ignore" Program continues on, ignoring this assertion failure this time
"always_ignore" Program continues on, ignoring this assertion failure for the rest of the run
Note that offers a programmatic means to deal with assertion failures through a callback, and this hint is largely intended to be used via environment variables by end users and automated tools.
This hint should be set before an assertion failure is triggered and can be changed at any time.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio
member.summary: SDL_HINT_AUDIO_*

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Alsa
member.summary: SDL_HINT_AUDIO_ALSA_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultDevice
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the default ALSA audio device name
member.value: A hint specifying the default ALSA audio device name

remarks.para: This hint is a specific audio device to open when the "default" audio device is used.

This hint will be ignored when opening the default playback device if Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultPlaybackDevice is set, or when opening the default recording device if Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultPlaybackDevice is set.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultPlaybackDevice
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the default ALSA audio playback device name
member.value: A hint specifying the default ALSA audio playback device name

remarks.para: This hint is a specific audio device to open for playback, when the "default" audio device is used.
If this hint isn't set, SDL will check Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultDevice before choosing a reasonable default.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultRecordingDevice
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the default ALSA audio recording device name
member.value: A hint specifying the default ALSA audio recording device name

remarks.para: This hint is a specific audio device to open for recording, when the "default" audio device is used.
If this hint isn't set, SDL will check Hint.Audio.Alsa.DefaultDevice before choosing a reasonable default.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Category
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the audio category on iOS and macOS
member.value: A hint controlling the audio category on iOS and macOS

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"ambient" Use the AVAudioSessionCategoryAmbient audio category, will be muted by the phone mute switch (default)
"playback" Use the AVAudioSessionCategoryPlayback category
For more information, see Apple's documentation: .
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Channels
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the default audio channel count
member.value: A hint controlling the default audio channel count

remarks.para: If the application doesn't specify the audio channel count when opening the device, this hint can be used to specify a default channel count that will be used. This defaults to "1" for recording and "2" for playback devices.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Device
member.summary: SDL_HINT_AUDIO_DEVICE_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Device.AppIconName
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying an application icon name for an audio device
member.value: A hint specifying an application icon name for an audio device

remarks.para: Some audio backends (such as Pulseaudio and Pipewire) allow you to set an XDG icon name for your application. Among other things, this icon might show up in a system control panel that lets the user adjust the volume on specific audio streams instead of using one giant master volume slider. Note that this is unrelated to the icon used by the windowing system, which may be set with (or via desktop file on Wayland).
Setting this to "" or leaving it unset will have SDL use a reasonable default, "applications-games", which is likely to be installed. See and for the relevant XDG icon specs.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Device.SampleFrames
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling device buffer size
member.value: A hint controlling device buffer size

remarks.para: This hint is an integer > 0, that represents the size of the device's buffer in sample frames (stereo audio data in 16-bit format is 4 bytes per sample frame, for example).

SDL3 generally decides this value on behalf of the app, but if for some reason the app needs to dictate this (because they want either lower latency or higher throughput AND ARE WILLING TO DEAL WITH what that might require of the app), they can specify it.
SDL will try to accommodate this value, but there is no promise you'll get the buffer size requested. Many platforms won't honor this request at all, or might adjust it.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Device.StreamName
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying an audio stream name for an audio device
member.value: A hint specifying an audio stream name for an audio device

remarks.para: Some audio backends (such as PulseAudio) allow you to describe your audio stream. Among other things, this description might show up in a system control panel that lets the user adjust the volume on specific audio streams instead of using one giant master volume slider.

This hints lets you transmit that information to the OS. The contents of this hint are used while opening an audio device. You should use a string that describes your what your program is playing ("audio stream" is probably sufficient in many cases, but this could be useful for something like "team chat" if you have a headset playing VoIP audio separately).
Setting this to "" or leaving it unset will have SDL use a reasonable default: "audio stream" or something similar.
Note that while this talks about audio streams, this is an OS-level concept, so it applies to a physical audio device in this case, and not an , nor an SDL logical audio device.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Device.StreamRole
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying an application role for an audio device
member.value: A hint specifying an application role for an audio device

remarks.para: Some audio backends (such as Pipewire) allow you to describe the role of your audio stream. Among other things, this description might show up in a system control panel or software for displaying and manipulating media playback/recording graphs.

This hints lets you transmit that information to the OS. The contents of this hint are used while opening an audio device. You should use a string that describes your what your program is playing (Game, Music, Movie, etc...).
Setting this to "" or leaving it unset will have SDL use a reasonable default: "Game" or something similar.
Note that while this talks about audio streams, this is an OS-level concept, so it applies to a physical audio device in this case, and not an , nor an SDL logical audio device.
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Disk
member.summary: SDL_HINT_AUDIO_DISK_*

member.value: A hint specifying the output file when playing audio using the disk audio driver

remarks.para: This defaults to "sdlaudio.raw".

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Disk.Timescale
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the audio rate when using the disk audio driver
member.value: A hint controlling the audio rate when using the disk audio driver

remarks.para: The disk audio driver normally simulates real-time for the audio rate that was specified, but you can use this hint to adjust this rate higher or lower down to 0. The default value is "1.0".
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Driver
member.summary: Gets a hint that specifies an audio backend to use
member.value: A hint that specifies an audio backend to use

remarks.para: By default, SDL will try all available audio backends in a reasonable order until it finds one that can work, but this hint allows the app or user to force a specific driver, such as "pipewire" if, say, you are on PulseAudio but want to try talking to the lower level instead.
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Dummy
member.summary: SDL_HINT_AUDIO_DUMMY_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Dummy.Timescale
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the audio rate when using the dummy audio driver
member.value: A hint controlling the audio rate when using the dummy audio driver

remarks.para: The dummy audio driver normally simulates real-time for the audio rate that was specified, but you can use this hint to adjust this rate higher or lower down to 0. The default value is "1.0".
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Format
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the default audio format
member.value: A hint controlling the default audio format

remarks.para: If the application doesn't specify the audio format when opening the device, this hint can be used to specify a default format that will be used.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"U8" Unsigned 8-bit audio
"S8" Signed 8-bit audio
"S16LE" Signed 16-bit little-endian audio
"S16BE" Signed 16-bit big-endian audio
"S16" Signed 16-bit native-endian audio (default)
"S32LE" Signed 32-bit little-endian audio
"S32BE" Signed 32-bit big-endian audio
"S32" Signed 32-bit native-endian audio
"F32LE" Floating point little-endian audio
"F32BE" Floating point big-endian audio
"F32" Floating point native-endian audio
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.Frequency
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the default audio frequency
member.value: A hint controlling the default audio frequency

remarks.para: If the application doesn't specify the audio frequency when opening the device, this hint can be used to specify a default frequency that will be used. This defaults to "44100".
This hint should be set before an audio device is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Audio.IncludeMonitors
member.summary: Gets a hint that causes SDL to not ignore audio "monitors"
member.value: A hint that causes SDL to not ignore audio "monitors"

remarks.para: This is currently only used by the PulseAudio driver.

By default, SDL ignores audio devices that aren't associated with physical hardware. Changing this hint to "1" will expose anything SDL sees that appears to be an audio source or sink. This will add "devices" to the list that the user probably doesn't want or need, but it can be useful in scenarios where you want to hook up SDL to some sort of virtual device, etc.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Audio monitor devices will be ignored (default)
"1" Audio monitor devices will show up in the device list
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AutoUpdateJoysticks
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether SDL updates joystick state when getting input events
member.value: A hint controlling whether SDL updates joystick state when getting input events

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" You'll call () manually
"1" SDL will automatically call () (default)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.AutoUpdateSensors
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether SDL updates sensor state when getting input events
member.value: A hint controlling whether SDL updates sensor state when getting input events

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" You'll call () manually
"1" SDL will automatically call () (default)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.BmpSaveLegacyFormat
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether or not to prevent SDL from using version 4 of the bitmap header when saving BMPs
member.value: A hint to control whether or not to prevent SDL from using version 4 of the bitmap header when saving BMPs

remarks.para: The bitmap header version 4 is required for proper alpha channel support and SDL will use it when required. Should this not be desired, this hint can force the use of the 40 byte header version which is supported everywhere.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Surfaces with a colorkey or an alpha channel are saved to a 32-bit BMP file with an alpha mask. SDL will use the bitmap header version 4 and set the alpha mask accordingly. (default)
"1" Surfaces with a colorkey or an alpha channel are saved to a 32-bit BMP file without an alpha mask. The alpha channel data will be in the file, but applications are going to ignore it.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Camera
member.summary: SDL_HINT_CAMERA_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Camera.Driver
member.summary: Get a hint that decides what camera backend to use
member.value: A hint that decides what camera backend to use

remarks.para: By default, SDL will try all available camera backends in a reasonable order until it finds one that can work, but this hint allows the app or user to force a specific target, such as "directshow" if, say, you are on Windows Media Foundations but want to try DirectShow instead.
The default value is unset, in which case SDL will try to figure out the best camera backend on your behalf. This hint needs to be set before the instance is created to be useful.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.CpuFeatureMask
member.summary: Gets a hint that limits what CPU features are available
member.value: A hint that limits what CPU features are available

remarks.para: By default, SDL marks all features the current CPU supports as available. This hint allows to limit these to a subset.
When the hint is unset, or empty, SDL will enable all detected CPU features.

The hint can be set to a comma separated list containing the following items:
"all"
"altivec"
"sse"
"sse2"
"sse3"
"sse41"
"sse42"
"avx"
"avx2"
"avx512f"
"arm-simd"
"neon"
"lsx"
"lasx"
The items can be prefixed by '+'/'-' to add/remove features.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Display
member.summary: SDL_HINT_DISPLAY_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Display.UsableBounds
member.summary: Gets a hint serving as an override for ()
member.value: A hint serving as an override for ()

remarks.para: If set, this hint will override the expected results for () for display index 0. Generally you don't want to do this, but this allows an embedded system to request that some of the screen be reserved for other uses when paired with a well-behaved application.
The contents of this hint must be 4 comma-separated integers, the first is the bounds x, then y, width and height, in that order.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.EGL
member.summary: SDL_HINT_EGL_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.EGL.Library
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the EGL library to load
member.value: A hint specifying the EGL library to load
member.remarks: This hint should be set before creating an OpenGL window or creating an OpenGL context. This hint is only considered if SDL is using EGL to manage OpenGL contexts. If this hint isn't set, SDL will choose a reasonable default.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Emscripten
member.summary: SDL_HINT_EMSCRIPTEN_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Emscripten.Asyncify
member.summary: Gets a hint to disable giving back control to the browser automatically when running with asyncify
member.value: A hint to disable giving back control to the browser automatically when running with asyncify

remarks.para: With -s ASYNCIFY, SDL calls emscripten_sleep during operations such as refreshing the screen or polling events.
This hint only applies to the emscripten platform.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Disable emscripten_sleep calls (if you give back browser control manually or use asyncify for other purposes)
"1" Enable emscripten_sleep calls (default)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Emscripten.CanvasSelector
member.summary: Gets a hint specifying the CSS selector used for the "default" window/canvas
member.value: A hint specifying the CSS selector used for the "default" window/canvas

remarks.para: This hint only applies to the emscripten platform.
The default value is "#canvas".
This hint should be set before creating a window.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Emscripten.KeyboardElement
member.summary: Gets a hint serving as an override for the binding element for keyboard inputs for Emscripten builds
member.value: A hint serving as an override for the binding element for keyboard inputs for Emscripten builds

remarks.para: This hint only applies to the emscripten platform.

The hint can be one of:
"#window" The javascript window object (default)
"#document" The javascript document object
#screen" The javascript window.screen object
"#canvas" The WebGL canvas element
"#none" Don't bind anything at all
Any other string without a leading # sign The element on the page with that ID
This hint should be set before creating a window.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.EnableScreenKeyboard
member.summary: Gets a hint that controls whether the on-screen keyboard should be shown when text input is active
member.value: A hint that controls whether the on-screen keyboard should be shown when text input is active

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"auto" The on-screen keyboard will be shown if there is no physical keyboard attached (default)
"0" Do not show the on-screen keyboard
"1" Show the on-screen keyboard, if available
This hint must be set before () is called.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.EvDevDevices
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of evdev devices to use if udev is not available
member.value: A hint containing a list of evdev devices to use if udev is not available

remarks.para: The list of devices is in the form: deviceclass:path[,deviceclass:path[,...]], where deviceclass is an integer representing the and path is the full path to the event device.
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.EventLogging
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling verbosity of the logging of SDL events pushed onto the internal queue
member.value: A hint controlling verbosity of the logging of SDL events pushed onto the internal queue

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values, from least to most verbose:
"0" Don't log any events. (default)
"1" Log most events (other than the really spammy ones)
"2" Include mouse and finger motion events

This is generally meant to be used to debug SDL itself, but can be useful for application developers that need better visibility into what is going on in the event queue. Logged events are sent through , which means by default they appear on stdout on most platforms or maybe OutputDebugString() on Windows (when is ), and can be funneled by the app with the event, etc.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.FileDialogDriver
member.summary: Gets a hint that specifies a dialog backend to use
member.value: A hint that specifies a dialog backend to use

remarks.para: By default, SDL will try all available dialog backends in a reasonable order until it finds one that can work, but this hint allows the app or user to force a specific target.
If the specified target does not exist or is not available, the dialog-related calls will fail.

This hint currently only applies to platforms using the generic "Unix" dialog implementation, but may be extended to more platforms in the future. Note that some Unix and Unix-like platforms have their own implementation, such as macOS and Haiku.

The hint can be set to the following values:
Select automatically (default, all platforms)
"portal" Use XDG Portals through DBus (Unix only)
"zenity" Use the Zenity program (Unix only)
More options may be added in the future.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.ForceRaiseWindow
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether raising the window should be done more forcefully
member.value: A hint controlling whether raising the window should be done more forcefully

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Honor the OS policy for raising windows (default)
"1" Force the window to be raised, overriding any OS policy
At present, this is only an issue under MS Windows, which makes it nearly impossible to programmatically move a window to the foreground, for "security" reasons. See for a discussion.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.FrameBufferAcceleration
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling how 3D acceleration is used to accelerate the SDL screen surface
member.value: A hint controlling how 3D acceleration is used to accelerate the SDL screen surface

remarks.para: SDL can try to accelerate the SDL screen surface by using streaming textures with a 3D rendering engine. This hint controls whether and how this is done.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Disable 3D acceleration
"1" Enable 3D acceleration, using the default renderer (default)
Any other string
Enable 3D acceleration, using the specified valid rendering drivers (e.g. "direct3d", "opengl", etc.). You can use the value of any of the predefined rendering drivers to set this hint (e.g. ).
This hint should be set before accessing a .

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.GameController.IgnoreDevices
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of devices to skip when scanning for game controllers
member.value: A hint containing a list of devices to skip when scanning for game controllers

remarks.para: The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.GameController.IgnoreDevicesExcept
member.summary: Gets a hint that if set, controls to skip all devices when scanning for game controllers except for the ones listed in this hint
member.value: A hint that if set, controls to skip all devices when scanning for game controllers except for the ones listed in this hint

remarks.para: The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.GameController.SensorFusion
member.summary: Gets a hint that controls whether the device's built-in accelerometer and gyro should be used as sensors for gamepads
member.value: A hint that controls whether the device's built-in accelerometer and gyro should be used as sensors for gamepads

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Sensor fusion is disabled
"1" Sensor fusion is enabled for all controllers that lack sensors
Or the hint can be a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.
This hint should be set before a gamepad is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.GameController.Type
member.summary: Gets a hint that overrides the automatic controller type detection
member.value: A hint that overrides the automatic controller type detection

remarks.para: The hint should be comma separated entries, in the form: "VID/PID=type", where the VID and PID should be hexadecimal with exactly 4 digits, e.g. 0x00fd.
This hint affects what low level protocol is used with the HIDAPI driver.

The type can be set to the following values:
Xbox360
XboxOne
PS3
PS4
PS5
SwitchPro
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.value: A hint that specifies a GPU backend to use

remarks.para: By default, SDL will try all available GPU backends in a reasonable order until it finds one that can work, but this hint allows the app or user to force a specific target, such as "direct3d11" if, say, your hardware supports D3D12 but want to try using D3D11 instead.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi
member.summary: SDL_HINT_HIDAPI_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi.EnumerateOnlyControllers
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether () enumerates all HID devices or only controllers
member.value: A hint to control whether () enumerates all HID devices or only controllers

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" () will enumerate all HID devices
"1" () will only enumerate controllers (default)
By default SDL will only enumerate controllers, to reduce risk of hanging or crashing on devices with bad drivers and avoiding macOS keyboard capture permission prompts.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi.IgnoreDevices
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of devices to ignore in ()
member.value: A hint containing a list of devices to ignore in ()

remarks.para: The format of the hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
For example, to ignore the Shanwan DS3 controller and any Valve controller, you might use the string "0x2563/0x0523,0x28de/0x0000".
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi.LibUsb
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether HIDAPI uses libusb for device access
member.value: A hint to control whether HIDAPI uses libusb for device access

remarks.para: By default libusb will only be used for a few devices that require direct USB access, and this can be controlled with .

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" HIDAPI will not use libusb for device access
"1" HIDAPI will use libusb for device access if available (default)
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi.LibUsbWhitelist
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether HIDAPI uses libusb only for whitelisted devices
member.value: A hint to control whether HIDAPI uses libusb only for whitelisted devices

remarks.para: By default libusb will only be used for a few devices that require direct USB access.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" HIDAPI will use libusb for all device access
"1" HIDAPI will use libusb only for whitelisted devices (default)
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.HidApi.UDev
member.summary: Gets a hint to control whether HIDAPI uses udev for device detection
member.value: A hint to control whether HIDAPI uses udev for device detection

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" HIDAPI will poll for device changes
"1" HIDAPI will use udev for device detection (default)
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.ImeImplementedUI
member.summary: Gets a hint describing what IME UI elements the application can display
member.value: A hint describing what IME UI elements the application can display

remarks.para: By default IME UI is handled using native components by the OS where possible, however this can interfere with or not be visible when exclusive fullscreen mode is used.

The hint can be set to a comma separated list containing the following items:
"none" or "0" The application can't render any IME elements, and native UI should be used (default)
"composition" The application handles events and can render the composition text
"candidates" The application handles and can render the candidate list
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.IOS
member.summary: SDL_HINT_IOS_*

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.IOS.HideHomeIndicator
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the home indicator bar on iPhone X should be hidden
member.value: A hint controlling whether the home indicator bar on iPhone X should be hidden

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" The indicator bar is not hidden (default for windowed applications)
"1" The indicator bar is hidden and is shown when the screen is touched (useful for movie playback applications)
"2" The indicator bar is dim and the first swipe makes it visible and the second swipe performs the "home" action (default for fullscreen applications)
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.DirectInput
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether DirectInput should be used for controllers
member.value: A hint controlling whether DirectInput should be used for controllers

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Disable DirectInput detection

This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.EnhancedReports
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether enhanced reports should be used for controllers when using the HIDAPI driver
member.value: A hint controlling whether enhanced reports should be used for controllers when using the HIDAPI driver

remarks.para: Enhanced reports allow rumble and effects on Bluetooth PlayStation controllers and gyro on Nintendo Switch controllers, but break Windows DirectInput for other applications that don't use SDL.
Once enhanced reports are enabled, they can't be disabled on PlayStation controllers without power cycling the controller.

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Enhanced reports are not enabled
"1" Enhanced reports are enabled (default)
"auto" Enhanced features are advertised to the application, but SDL doesn't change the controller report mode unless the application uses them
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.FlightStick

member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of flightstick style controllers
member.value: A hint containing a list of flightstick style controllers

remarks.para: The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.FlightStick.DevicesExcluded
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of devices that are not flightstick style controllers
member.value: A hint containing a list of devices that are not flightstick style controllers
member.remarks:
This hint will override and the built in device list.
The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of devices known not to have a GameCube form factor
member.value: A hint containing a list of devices known not to have a GameCube form factor
member.remarks:
This hint will override and the built in device list.
The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.GameInput
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether GameInput should be used for controller handling on Windows
member.value: A hint controlling whether GameInput should be used for controller handling on Windows

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" GameInput is not used
"1" GameInput is used
The default is "1" on GDK platforms, and "0" otherwise.
This hint should be set before SDL is initialized.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HapticAxes
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of devices and their desired number of haptic (force feedback) enabled axis
member.value: A hint containing a list of devices and their desired number of haptic (force feedback) enabled axis

remarks.para: The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form plus the number of desired axes, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB/1,0xCCCC/0xDDDD/3".
This hint supports a "wildcard" device that will set the number of haptic axes on all initialized haptic devices which were not defined explicitly in this hint: "0xFFFF/0xFFFF/1".
This hint should be set before a controller is opened. The number of haptic axes won't exceed the number of real axes found on the device.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.GameCube.RumbleBrake
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether rumble is used to implement the GameCube controller's 3 rumble modes (Stop(0), Rumble(1), and StopHard(2))
member.value: A hint controlling whether rumble is used to implement the GameCube controller's 3 rumble modes (Stop(0), Rumble(1), and StopHard(2))

remarks.para:
This is useful for applications that need full compatibility for things like ADSR envelopes.
Stop is implemented by setting to 0 and > 0
Rumble is both at any arbitrary value
StopHard is implemented by setting both and to 0

The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Normal rumble behavior is behavior is used (default)
"1" Proper GameCube controller rumble behavior is used
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.GameCube.UseDriver
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the HIDAPI driver for Nintendo GameCube controllers should be used
member.value: A hint controlling whether the HIDAPI driver for Nintendo GameCube controllers should be used

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" HIDAPI driver is not used
"1" HIDAPI driver is used
The default value for this hint is the value of .
This hint should be set before initializing joysticks and gamepads (either by initializing SDL or by initializing them on their own).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.Gip.ResetForMetadata
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the new HIDAPI driver for wired Xbox One (GIP) controllers should reset the controller if it can't get the metadata from the controller
member.value: A hint controlling whether the new HIDAPI driver for wired Xbox One (GIP) controllers should reset the controller if it can't get the metadata from the controller

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Assume this is a generic controller
"1" Reset the controller to get metadata
By default the controller is not reset.
This hint should be set before initializing joysticks and gamepads (either by initializing SDL or by initializing them on their own).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.JoyCons.Combine
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether Nintendo Switch Joy-Con controllers will be combined into a single Pro-like controller when using the HIDAPI driver
member.value: A hint controlling whether Nintendo Switch Joy-Con controllers will be combined into a single Pro-like controller when using the HIDAPI driver

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Left and right Joy-Con controllers will not be combined and each will be a mini-gamepad
"1" Left and right Joy-Con controllers will be combined into a single controller (default)
This hint should be set before initializing joysticks and gamepads (either by initializing SDL or by initializing them on their own).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.JoyCons.HomeLed
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the Home button LED should be turned on when a Nintendo Switch Joy-Con controller is opened
member.value: A hint controlling whether the Home button LED should be turned on when a Nintendo Switch Joy-Con controller is opened

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Home button LED is turned off
"1" Home button LED is turned on
By default the Home button LED state is not changed. This hint can also be set to a floating point value between 0.0 and 1.0 which controls the brightness of the Home button LED.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.PS4.ReportInterval
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling the update rate of the PS4 controller over Bluetooth when using the HIDAPI driver
member.value: A hint controlling the update rate of the PS4 controller over Bluetooth when using the HIDAPI driver

remarks.para: This defaults to 4 ms, to match the behavior over USB, and to be more friendly to other Bluetooth devices and older Bluetooth hardware on the computer. It can be set to "1" (1000 Hz), "2" (500 Hz), and "4" (250 Hz).
This hint can be set anytime, but only takes effect when extended input reports are enabled.

The default is "0" on Windows, otherwise the value of .
This hint should be set before initializing joysticks and gamepads (either by initializing SDL or by initializing them on their own).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.HidApi.XBoxOne.HomeLed
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the Home button LED should be turned on when an Xbox One controller is opened
member.value: A hint controlling whether the Home button LED should be turned on when an Xbox One controller is opened

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Home button LED is turned off
"1" Home button LED is turned on

By default the Home button LED state is not changed. This hint can also be set to a floating point value between 0.0 and 1.0 which controls the brightness of the Home button LED. The default brightness is 0.4.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.Linux.Classic
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether to use the classic /dev/input/js* joystick interface or the newer /dev/input/event* joystick interface on Linux
member.value: A hint controlling whether to use the classic /dev/input/js* joystick interface or the newer /dev/input/event* joystick interface on Linux

The hint can be set to the following values:

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.Linux.Deadzones
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether joysticks on Linux adhere to their HID-defined deadzones or return unfiltered values
member.value: A hint controlling whether joysticks on Linux adhere to their HID-defined deadzones or return unfiltered values

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Return unfiltered joystick axis values (default)
"1" Return axis values with deadzones taken into account
This hint should be set before a controller is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.Linux.DigitalHats
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether joysticks on Linux will always treat 'hat' axis inputs (ABS_HAT0X - ABS_HAT3Y) as 8-way digital hats without checking whether they may be analog
member.value: A hint controlling whether joysticks on Linux will always treat 'hat' axis inputs (ABS_HAT0X - ABS_HAT3Y) as 8-way digital hats without checking whether they may be analog

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Only map hat axis inputs to digital hat outputs if the input axes appear to actually be digital (default)
"1" Always handle the input axes numbered ABS_HAT0X to ABS_HAT3Y as digital hats
This hint should be set before a controller is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.Linux.HatDeadzones
member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether digital hats on Linux will apply deadzones to their underlying input axes or use unfiltered values
member.value: A hint controlling whether digital hats on Linux will apply deadzones to their underlying input axes or use unfiltered values

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" Return digital hat values based on unfiltered input axis values
"1" Return digital hat values with deadzones on the input axes taken into account (default)
This hint should be set before a controller is opened.

member.summary: Gets a hint controlling whether the RAWINPUT driver should pull correlated data from XInput
member.value: A hint controlling whether the RAWINPUT driver should pull correlated data from XInput

remarks.para:
The hint can be set to the following values:
"0" RAWINPUT driver will only use data from raw input APIs
"1" RAWINPUT driver will also pull data from XInput and Windows.Gaming.Input, providing better trigger axes, guide button presses, and rumble support for Xbox controllers (default)
This hint should be set before a controller is opened.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.Joystick.Throttle.Devices
member.summary: Gets a hint containing a list of throttle style controllers
member.value: A hint containing a list of throttle style controllers

remarks.para: The format of this hint is a comma separated list of USB VID/PID pairs in hexadecimal form, e.g. "0xAAAA/0xBBBB,0xCCCC/0xDDDD".
The hint can also take the form of "@file", in which case the named file will be loaded and interpreted as the value of the hint.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.KeycodeOptions
member.summary: Gets a hint that controls keycode representation in keyboard events
member.value: A hint that controls keycode representation in keyboard events

remarks.para:
The hint is a comma separated set of options for translating keycodes in events:
"none" Keycode options are cleared, this overrides other options
"hide_numpad" The numpad keysyms will be translated into their non-numpad versions based on the current NumLock state. For example, would become if is set in the event modifiers, and if it is unset.
"french_numbers" The number row on French keyboards is inverted, so pressing the 1 key would yield the keycode , or '1', instead of , or '&'
"latin_letters" For keyboards using non-Latin letters, such as Russian or Thai, the letter keys generate keycodes as though it had an en_US layout. E.g. pressing the key associated with on a Russian keyboard would yield 'a' instead of a Cyrillic letter.
The default value for this hint is "french_numbers,latin_letters"
Some platforms like Emscripten only provide modified keycodes and the options are not used.
These options do not affect the return value of () or (), they just apply to the keycode included in key events.
This hint can be set anytime.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Hint.MainCallbackRate
member.summary: Gets a hint that request to be called at a specific rate
member.value: A hint that request to be called at a specific rate

remarks.para: If this hint is set to a number, it represents Hz, so "60" means try to iterate 60 times per second. "0" means to iterate as fast as possible. Negative values are illegal, but reserved, in case they are useful in a future revision of SDL.

There are other string values that have special meaning. If set to "waitevent", will not be called until new event(s) have arrived (and been processed by ). This can be useful for apps that are completely idle except in response to input.
This hint defaults to 0, and specifying for the hint's value will restore the default.
This hint can be set anytime and is allowed to be changed at anytime.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Input.KeycodeExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.KeycodeExtensions.SDL_GetKeyFromName(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get a key code from a human-readable name
member.param: The human-readable key name
member.returns: Returns key code, or SDLK_UNKNOWN if the name wasn't recognized; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.KeycodeExtensions.SDL_GetKeyFromScancode(Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode,Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Get the key code corresponding to the given scancode according to the current keyboard layout
member.param: The desired SDL_Scancode to query
The modifier state to use when translating the scancode to a keycode
true if the keycode will be used in key events
member.returns: Returns the SDL_Keycode that corresponds to the given SDL_Scancode

remarks.para: If you want to get the keycode as it would be delivered in key events, including options specified in SDL_HINT_KEYCODE_OPTIONS, then you should pass as true. Otherwise this function simply translates the scancode based on the given modifier state.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.KeycodeExtensions.SDL_GetKeyName(Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keycode)
member.summary: Get a human-readable name for a key
member.param: The desired SDL_Keycode to query
member.returns: Returns a UTF-8 encoded string of the key name

remarks.para: If the key doesn't have a name, this function returns an empty string ("").
Letters will be presented in their uppercase form, if applicable.
This function is not thread safe.
