member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod
member.summary: Represents keyboard modifier keys

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.None
member.summary: No modifier key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.LeftShift
member.summary: The left ⇧ (shift) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.RightShift
member.summary: The right ⇧ (shift) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.Level5Shift
member.summary: The "Level 5 Shift" key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.LeftControl
member.summary: The left CTRL (left control) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.RightControl
member.summary: The right CTRL (right control) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.LeftAlt
member.summary: The left ALT (left alt) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.RightAlt
member.summary: The right ALT (right alt) key, or sometimes called ALT GR (alt gr.) key, is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.LeftGui
member.summary: The left ⊞ (left Windows) key on PCs, left ⌘ (left command) key on Macs, or sometimes called left meta key, is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.RightGui
member.summary: The right ⊞ (right Windows) key on PCs, right ⌘ (right command) key on Macs, or sometimes called right meta key, is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.NumLock
member.summary: The NUM (num lock), may be located on an extended keypad, is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.CapsLock
member.summary: The ⇪ (caps lock) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.ScrollLock
member.summary: The SCROLL (scroll lock) key is down

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.Control
member.summary: Both CTRL (control) keys combined

remarks.para: This is effectively | .

member.example:
Use this to determine if any of the CTRL (control) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // any 'CTRL' key is down in 'modifier'
Use this to determine if all of the CTRL (control) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // all 'CTRL' keys are down in 'modifier'

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.Shift
member.summary: Both ⇧ (shift) keys combined

remarks.para: This is effectively | .

member.example:
Use this to determine if any of the ⇧ (shift) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when any 'SHIFT' key is down in 'modifier'
Use this to determine if all of the ⇧ (shift) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when all 'SHIFT' keys are down in 'modifier'

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.Alt
member.summary: Both ALT (alt) keys combined

remarks.para: This is effectively | .

member.example:
Use this to determine if any of the ALT (alt) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when any 'ALT' key is down in 'modifier'
Use this to determine if all of the ALT (alt) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when all 'ALT' keys are down in 'modifier'

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod.Gui
member.summary: Both ⊞ (Windows) key on PCs, ⌘ (command) keys on Macs, or sometimes called meta keys, combined

remarks.para: This is effectively | .

member.example:
Use this to determine if any of the ⊞ (Windows) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when any 'WINDOWS' key is down in 'modifier'
Use this to determine if all of the ⊞ (Windows) keys are down:
(modifier & ) // 'true' when all 'WINDOWS' keys are down in 'modifier'

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis
member.summary: Represents the various axes of pen input

remarks.para: The values defined here are the valid values for the property in the . s of these axes are either normalised to the range from 0 to 1 or report a (positive or negative) angle in degrees, with 0 representing the centre. Not all pens/backends support all axes. Values of unsupported axes are always zero.

To convert angles for tilt and rotation into vector representation, use the following formula for values (x) of the respective axis: sin(x ⋅ π / 180°) where x is the value of either the , the , or the axis.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.Pressure
member.summary: The pressure applied by the pen

remarks.para: Values are unidirectional, in the range from 0 to 1 (no pressure to maximum pressure).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.XTilt
member.summary: The pen's horizontal tilt angle

remarks.para: Values are bidirectional, in the range from -90° to 90° (full left tilt to full right tilt).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.YTilt
member.summary: The pen's vertical tilt angle

remarks.para: Values are bidirectional, in the range from -90° to 90° (full up tilt to full down tilt).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.Distance
member.summary: The pen's distance to the drawing surface

remarks.para: Values are unidirectional, in the range from 0 to 1 (no distance to maximum distance).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.Rotation
member.summary: The pen's barrel rotation angle

remarks.para: Values are bidirectional, in the range from -180° to 179.9° (clockwise, 0 is facing up, -180° is facing down).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.Slider
member.summary: The pen's finger wheel or slider position (e.g. for Airbrush pens)

remarks.para: Values are unidirectional, in the range from 0 to 1.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.PenAxis.TangentialPressure
member.summary: The pressure applied from squeezing the pen ("barrel pressure")

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.Backslash
member.summary: The for the \ key in US layouts

remarks.para: This key Located at the lower left of the ⏎ (return / enter) key on ISO keyboards and at the right end of the QWERTY row on ANSI keyboards.

This key produces:
'\' (back slash) and '|' (vertical line) in a US layout and in a UK Mac layout
'#' (number sign) and '~' (tilde) in a UK Windows layout
'$' (dollar sign) and '£' (pound sign) in a Swiss German layout
'#' (number sign) and ''' (apostrophe) in a German layout
'`' (accent grave) and '£' (pound sign) in a French Mac layout
'*' (asterisk) and 'µ' (micro sign) in a French Windows layout

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.NonUsHash
member.summary: The alternative that ISO USB keyboards use for

remarks.para: All relevant operating systems treat this and identically.
You should not generate this , nor should you rely on this getting generated, because SDL will never do that with most keyboards.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.Semicolon
member.summary: The for the ; key

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.Apostrophe
member.summary: The for the ' key

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.Grave
member.summary: The for the ` key in US layouts

remarks.para: This key Located at the top left corner.

This key produces:
'`' (accent grave) and '~' (tilde) in a US Windows layout and in US and UK Mac layouts on ASNI keyboards
'`' (accent grave) and '¬' (not sign) in a UK Windows layout
'§' (section sign) and '±' (plus-minus sign) in US and UK Mac layouts on ISO keyboards
'§' (section sign) and '°' (degree sign) in a Swiss German layout (for Macs only on ISO keyboards)
'^' (accent circumflex) and '°' (degree sign) in a German layout (for Macs only on ISO keyboards)
'²' (superscript two) and '~' (tilde) in a French Windows layout
'@' (commercial at sign) and '#' (number sign) in a French Mac layout on ISO keyboards
'<' (less than sign) and '>' (greater than sign) in a Swiss German, German, or French Mac layout on ANSI keyboards

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode.NonUsBackslash
member.summary: The for the additional key, located between the left ⇧ (shift) key and the Y key, on ISO keyboards

remarks.para: This is the additional key that ISO keyboards have over ANSI ones, located between the left ⇧ (shift) key and the Y key.

This key produces:
'`' (accent grave) and '~' (tilde) in a US or UK Mac layout
'\' (back slash) and '|' (vertical line) in a US or UK Windows layout
'<' (less than sign) and '>' (greater than sign) in a Swiss German, German, or French layout

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Input.ScancodeExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.ScancodeExtensions.SDL_GetScancodeFromKey(Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keycode,Sdl3Sharp.Input.Keymod*)
member.summary: Get the scancode corresponding to the given key code according to the current keyboard layout
member.param: The desired SDL_Keycode to query.
A pointer to the modifier state that would be used when the scancode generates this key, may be NULL
member.returns: Returns the SDL_Scancode that corresponds to the given SDL_Keycode

remarks.para: Note that there may be multiple scancode+modifier states that can generate this keycode, this will just return the first one found.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.ScancodeExtensions.SDL_GetScancodeFromName(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get a scancode from a human-readable name
member.param: The human-readable scancode name
member.returns: Returns the SDL_Scancode, or SDL_SCANCODE_UNKNOWN if the name wasn't recognized; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.ScancodeExtensions.SDL_GetScancodeName(Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode)
member.summary: Get a human-readable name for a scancode
member.param: The desired SDL_Scancode to query
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the name for the scancode. If the scancode doesn't have a name this function returns an empty string ("")

remarks.para: Warning: The returned name is by design not stable across platforms, e.g. the name for SDL_SCANCODE_LGUI is "Left GUI" under Linux but "Left Windows" under Microsoft Windows, and some scancodes like SDL_SCANCODE_NONUSBACKSLASH don't have any name at all. There are even scancodes that share names, e.g. SDL_SCANCODE_RETURN and SDL_SCANCODE_RETURN2 (both called "Return"). This function is therefore unsuitable for creating a stable cross-platform two-way mapping between strings and scancodes.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Input.ScancodeExtensions.SDL_SetScancodeName(Sdl3Sharp.Input.Scancode,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Set a human-readable name for a scancode
member.param: The desired SDL_Scancode
The name to use for the scancode, encoded as UTF-8. The string is not copied, so the pointer given to this function must stay valid while SDL is being used.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function is not thread safe.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Input.SensorType
member.summary: Represents various types of sensors

remarks.para: Additional sensors may be available, using platform dependent semantics. These are additionally available Android sensors:

Notes on accelerometers: The accelerometer returns the current acceleration in SI meters per second squared. This measurement includes the force of gravity, so a device at rest will have an value of away from the center of the earth, which is a positive Y value.
values[0] Acceleration on the X axis
values[1] Acceleration on the Y axis
values[2] Acceleration on the Z axis
For phones and tablets held in natural orientation and game controllers held in front of you, the axes are defined as follows:
-X … +X Left … Right
-Y … +Y Bottom … Top
-Z … +Z Farther … Closer
The accelerometer axis data is not changed when the device is rotated.

Notes on gyroscopes: The gyroscope returns the current rate of rotation in radians per second. The rotation is positive in the counter-clockwise direction. That is, an observer looking from a positive location on one of the axes would see positive rotation on that axis when it appeared to be rotating counter-clockwise.
values[0] Angular speed around the X axis (pitch)
values[1] Angular speed around the Y axis (yaw)
values[2] Angular speed around the Z axis (roll)
For phones and tablets held in natural orientation and game controllers held in front of you, the axes are defined as follows:
-X … +X Left … Right
-Y … +Y Bottom … Top
-Z … +Z Farther … Closer
The gyroscope axis data is not changed when the device is rotated.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.#ctor(System.String,System.String,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new instance for the specified file name and mode string
member.param: The name of the file to open
The mode string to use when opening the file

An to use if the newly created should be automatically closed when disposed. Set to to disable automatic closing behavior. This parameter is reflected by the property. Changing the value of the property after construction also changes the automatic closing behavior.

A value indicating whether to flush the when automatically closing it. Has no effect if is . This parameter is reflected by the property. Changing the value of the property after construction also changes the automatic closing behavior.

remarks.para: Opening a file is not an asynchronous operation under the assumption that doing so is generally a fast operation. Future reading and writing operations to the will be asynchronous though.

The mode strings used by this method are roughly the same as the ones used by the C standard library's fopen function with some exceptions. You can use to construct valid mode strings. There's no support for binary/text modes ("b"/"t"), as all asynchronous file I/O is performed in binary mode, and there's no support for appending mode ("a"), since you specify offsets explicitly when reading and writing.

remarks.para: Opening a file is not an asynchronous operation under the assumption that doing so is generally a fast operation. Future reading and writing operations to the will be asynchronous though.
There's no support for specifying a value, as all asynchronous file I/O is performed in binary mode, and there's no support for , since you specify offsets explicitly when reading and writing.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes the and potentially closes it asynchronously

remarks.para: If the property is set to a non- value, the will be automatically closed when this method is called. The automatic closing behavior of the (if any) will always just attempt a single try to initiate a closing operation.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.TryClose(System.Boolean,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue,System.Object)
member.summary: Tries to asynchronously close the
member.param: A value indicating whether to flush the before closing it
The to add the asynchronous closing operation to
User-defined data to associate with the asynchronous closing operation. This will be provided as the property's value of the returned by the operation.
member.returns: , if the asynchronous closing operation was initiated successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: Closing an is also an asynchronous operation.

Closing a file that has been written to does not guarantee the data has made it to physical media; it may remain in the operating system's file cache, for later writing to disk. This means that a successfully-closed file can be lost if the system crashes or loses power in this small window. To prevent this, call this method with the argument set to . This will make the operation take longer, and perhaps increase system load in general, but a successful result guarantees that the data has made it to physical storage. Set the argument to for temporary files, caches, and unimportant data, and definitely set it to for crucial irreplaceable files, like game saves.

This method guarantees that the closing operation will happen after any other pending tasks to the . So it's safe to open a file, start several operations, close the immediately, then check for all results later. This method will not block until the tasks have completed.

Once this method returns , the is no longer valid, regardless of any future outcomes. Any completed operation might still point to this in their , in case it's used to track information, but it should not be used again.

If this method returns , the closing operation wasn't initiated at all, and it's safe to attempt to close again later. The automatic closing behavior of the (if any) will always just attempt a single try to initiate a closing operation.
The newly created asynchronous closing operation will be added to the specified .

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.TryGetModeString(Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileAccess,Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileMode,System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to get the mode string for the specified access and mode
member.param: The value representing the access mode
The value representing the file mode
The resulting mode string, when this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the mode string was successfully retrieved; otherwise,

remarks.para: A mode string constructed by this method is roughly the same as the ones used by the C standard library's fopen function with some exceptions. There's no support for appending mode ("a"), since you specify offsets explicitly when reading and writing in asynchronous file I/O.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.TryRead(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory,System.UInt64,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue,System.Object)
member.summary: Tries to asynchronously read data from the
member.param: The memory buffer to read data into
The offset within underlying data stream to start reading from
The to add the asynchronous reading operation to
User-defined data to associate with the asynchronous reading operation. This will be provided as the property's value of the returned by the operation.
member.returns: , if the reading operation was initiated successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The asynchronous reading operation might read less bytes than requested by 's property.

This method returns as quickly as possible; it does not wait for the reading operation to complete. On a successful return, this work will continue in the background. If the work begins, even a failure is asynchronous: a failing return value from this method only means the reading operation couldn't get initiated.
This method will pin the specified until the of the reading operation is disposed.
The newly created asynchronous reading operation will be added to the specified .

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_AsyncIOFromFile(System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Use this function to create a new SDL_AsyncIO object for reading from and/or writing to a named file
member.param: A UTF-8 string representing the filename to open
An ASCII string representing the mode to be used for opening the file
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the SDL_AsyncIO structure that is created or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
The string understands the following values:
"r" Open a file for reading only. It must exist.
"w" Open a file for writing only. It will create missing files or truncate existing ones.
"r+" Open a file for update both reading and writing. The file must exist.
"w+" Create an empty file for both reading and writing. If a file with the same name already exists its content is erased and the file is treated as a new empty file.
There is no "b" mode, as there is only "binary" style I/O, and no "a" mode for appending, since you specify the position when starting a task.
This function supports Unicode filenames, but they must be encoded in UTF-8 format, regardless of the underlying operating system.

This call is not asynchronous; it will open the file before returning, under the assumption that doing so is generally a fast operation. Future reads and writes to the opened file will be async, however.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_CloseAsyncIO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_AsyncIO*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*,System.Void*)
member.summary: Close and free any allocated resources for an async I/O object
member.param: A pointer to an SDL_AsyncIO structure to close
True if data should sync to disk before the task completes
A queue to add the new SDL_AsyncIO to
An app-defined pointer that will be provided with the task results
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Closing a file is also an asynchronous task! If a write failure were to happen during the closing process, for example, the task results will report it as usual.

Closing a file that has been written to does not guarantee the data has made it to physical media; it may remain in the operating system's file cache, for later writing to disk. This means that a successfully-closed file can be lost if the system crashes or loses power in this small window. To prevent this, call this function with the flush parameter set to true. This will make the operation take longer, and perhaps increase system load in general, but a successful result guarantees that the data has made it to physical storage. Don't use this for temporary files, caches, and unimportant data, and definitely use it for crucial irreplaceable files, like game saves.

This function guarantees that the close will happen after any other pending tasks to , so it's safe to open a file, start several operations, close the file immediately, then check for all results later. This function will not block until the tasks have completed.

Once this function returns true, is no longer valid, regardless of any future outcomes. Any completed tasks might still contain this pointer in their SDL_AsyncIOOutcome data, in case the app was using this value to track information, but it should not be used again.
If this function returns false, the close wasn't started at all, and it's safe to attempt to close again later.
An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes its work.
It is safe to call this function from any thread, but two threads should not attempt to close the same object.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_GetAsyncIOSize(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_AsyncIO*)
member.summary: Use this function to get the size of the data stream in an SDL_AsyncIO
member.param: The SDL_AsyncIO to get the size of the data stream from
member.returns: Returns the size of the data stream in the SDL_IOStream on success or a negative error code on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This call is not asynchronous; it assumes that obtaining this info is a non-blocking operation in most reasonable cases.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_ReadAsyncIO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIO.SDL_AsyncIO*,System.Void*,System.UInt64,System.UInt64,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*,System.Void*)
member.summary: Start an async read
member.param: A pointer to an SDL_AsyncIO structure
A pointer to a buffer to read data into
The position to start reading in the data source
The number of bytes to read from the data source
A queue to add the new SDL_AsyncIO to
An app-defined pointer that will be provided with the task results
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function reads up to bytes from position in the data source to the area pointed at by . This function may read less bytes than requested.

This function returns as quickly as possible; it does not wait for the read to complete. On a successful return, this work will continue in the background. If the work begins, even failure is asynchronous: a failing return value from this function only means the work couldn't start at all.

must remain available until the work is done, and may be accessed by the system at any time until then. Do not allocate it on the stack, as this might take longer than the life of the calling function to complete!
An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes its work.

remarks.para: This function writes bytes from position in the data source to the area pointed at by .

This function returns as quickly as possible; it does not wait for the write to complete. On a successful return, this work will continue in the background. If the work begins, even failure is asynchronous: a failing return value from this function only means the work couldn't start at all.

must remain available until the work is done, and may be accessed by the system at any time until then. Do not allocate it on the stack, as this might take longer than the life of the calling function to complete!
An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes its work.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue
member.summary: Represents a queue for asynchronous I/O operations

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.#ctor
member.summary: Creates a new
member.exception: The could not be created

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.Signal
member.summary: Wakes up any threads waiting for an asynchronous I/O outcome () from this queue

remarks.para: This method will unblock any threads that are sleeping in a call to for this queue, and cause them to return from that method.

This method can be useful when disposing a queue to make sure nothing is waiting on it indefinitely. In this case, once the call to this method completes, the caller should take measures to make sure any previously-blocked threads have returned from their wait and will not touch the queue again.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.TryGetOutcome(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOOutcome@)
member.summary: Tries to get an asynchronous I/O outcome () from this queue without blocking
member.param: The representing the result of a finished asynchronous I/O operation, when this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if there was a finished asynchronous I/O operation in the queue; otherwise,

remarks.para: If an asynchronous I/O operation assigned to this queue has finished, this method will return and fill in with the details of result of that operation. If no asynchronous I/O operation in the queue has finished, this method will return immediately. This method does not block.
If an asynchronous I/O operation has finished, this method will clean up its internal resources, and then the operation will be removed from the queue.
It is safe for multiple threads to call this method on the same instance at once; a finished asynchronous I/O operation will only be removed once and it's will only be returned to one of the threads.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.TryWaitForOutcome(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOOutcome@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to wait for an asynchronous I/O outcome () from this queue
member.param: The representing the result of a finished asynchronous I/O operation, when this method returns ; otherwise,
The maximum time to wait for an outcome, in milliseconds, or -1 to wait indefinitely
member.returns: , if there was a finished asynchronous I/O operation in the queue, or if an asynchronous I/O operation finishes before the queue was signaled or timed out; otherwise,

remarks.para: This method puts the caller thread to sleep until there an asynchronous I/O operation assigned to the queue that has finished.

If an asynchronous I/O operation assigned to this queue has finished, this method will return and fill in with the details of result of that operation. If no asynchronous I/O operation in the queue has finished until the queue was signaled or timed out, this method will return .
If an asynchronous I/O operation has finished, this method will clean up its internal resources, and then the operation will be removed from the queue.
It is safe for multiple threads to call this method on the same instance at once; a finished asynchronous I/O operation will only be removed once and it's will only be returned to one of the threads.

Please note that by the nature of some platforms, more than one waiting thread may wake to handle to outcome of a single asynchronous I/O operation, but only one thread will obtain it and it's call to this method will return , while the return value of the other awoken threads will be . So is a maximum wait time, and this method may return sooner.

This method may return sooner if there was a system error, the OS inadvertently awoke multiple threads, or if was called on the instance to wake up all waiting threads without a finished asynchronous I/O operation.
A can be used to specify a maximum wait time, but rather than polling, it is possible to have a value of -1 (its default value) to wait indefinitely, and use to wake up the waiting threads later.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_CreateAsyncIOQueue
member.summary: Create a task queue for tracking multiple I/O operations
member.returns: Returns a new task queue object or NULL if there was an error; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Async I/O operations are assigned to a queue when started. The queue can be checked for completed tasks thereafter.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_DestroyAsyncIOQueue(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*)
member.summary: Destroy a previously-created async I/O task queue
member.param: The task queue to destroy

remarks.para: If there are still tasks pending for this queue, this call will block until those tasks are finished. All those tasks will be deallocated. Their results will be lost to the app.
Any pending reads from SDL_LoadFileAsync() that are still in this queue will have their buffers deallocated by this function, to prevent a memory leak.
Once this function is called, the queue is no longer valid and should not be used, including by other threads that might access it while destruction is blocking on pending tasks.

Do not destroy a queue that still has threads waiting on it through SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult(). You can call SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue() first to unblock those threads, and take measures (such as SDL_WaitThread()) to make sure they have finished their wait and won't wait on the queue again.
It is safe to call this function from any thread, so long as no other thread is waiting on the queue with SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_GetAsyncIOResult(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOOutcome.SDL_AsyncIOOutcome*)
member.summary: Query an async I/O task queue for completed tasks
member.param: The async I/O task queue to query
Details of a finished task will be written here. May not be NULL.
member.returns: Returns true if a task has completed, false otherwise

remarks.para: If a task assigned to this queue has finished, this will return true and fill in with the details of the task. If no task in the queue has finished, this function will return false. This function does not block.
If a task has completed, this function will free its resources and the task pointer will no longer be valid. The task will be removed from the queue.
It is safe for multiple threads to call this function on the same queue at once; a completed task will only go to one of the threads.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*)
member.summary: Wake up any threads that are blocking in SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult()
member.param: The async I/O task queue to signal

remarks.para: This will unblock any threads that are sleeping in a call to SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult for the specified queue, and cause them to return from that function.

This can be useful when destroying a queue to make sure nothing is touching it indefinitely. In this case, once this call completes, the caller should take measures to make sure any previously-blocked threads have returned from their wait and will not touch the queue again (perhaps by setting a flag to tell the threads to terminate and then using SDL_WaitThread() to make sure they've done so).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_WaitAsyncIOResult(Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOOutcome.SDL_AsyncIOOutcome*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Block until an async I/O task queue has a completed task
member.param: The async I/O task queue to wait on
Details of a finished task will be written here. May not be NULL.
The maximum time to wait, in milliseconds, or -1 to wait indefinitely
member.returns: Returns true if task has completed, false otherwise

remarks.para: This function puts the calling thread to sleep until there a task assigned to the queue that has finished.
If a task assigned to the queue has finished, this will return true and fill in with the details of the task. If no task in the queue has finished, this function will return false.
If a task has completed, this function will free its resources and the task pointer will no longer be valid. The task will be removed from the queue.
It is safe for multiple threads to call this function on the same queue at once; a completed task will only go to one of the threads.

Note that by the nature of various platforms, more than one waiting thread may wake to handle a single task, but only one will obtain it, so is a maximum wait time, and this function may return false sooner.

This function may return false if there was a system error, the OS inadvertently awoke multiple threads, or if SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue() was called to wake up all waiting threads without a finished task.

A timeout can be used to specify a maximum wait time, but rather than polling, it is possible to have a timeout of -1 to wait forever, and use SDL_SignalAsyncIOQueue() to wake up the waiting threads later.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.DynamicMemoryStream.SDL_IOFromDynamicMem
member.summary: Use this function to create an SDL_IOStream that is backed by dynamically allocated memory
member.returns: Returns a pointer to a new SDL_IOStream structure or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
This supports the following properties to provide access to the memory and control over allocations:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_DYNAMIC_MEMORY_POINTER
A pointer to the internal memory of the stream. This can be set to NULL to transfer ownership of the memory to the application, which should free the memory with SDL_free(). If this is done, the next operation on the stream must be SDL_CloseIO().

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.IO.EnumerateDirectoryCallback

member.param: The directory that is being enumerated. Can be the empty string when enumerating the root of a container.
The name of the entry being enumerated
member.returns:
How to proceed with the enumeration:
Terminate the enumeration with an error
Terminate the enumeration with success
Continue with the enumeration

remarks.para: Enumeration of directory entries will continue until either all entries have been enumerated, or the enumeration was requested to terminate.

is guaranteed to end with a directory separator character ('\\' on Windows; '/' on most other platforms) if it's not the empty string. It can be the empty string ("") when enumerating the root of a container.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.File.SDL_LoadFileAsync(System.Byte*,Sdl3Sharp.IO.AsyncIOQueue.SDL_AsyncIOQueue*,System.Void*)
member.summary: Load all the data from a file path, asynchronously
member.param: The path to read all available data from
A queue to add the new SDL_AsyncIO to
An app-defined pointer that will be provided with the task results
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function returns as quickly as possible; it does not wait for the read to complete. On a successful return, this work will continue in the background. If the work begins, even failure is asynchronous: a failing return value from this function only means the work couldn't start at all.
The data is allocated with a zero byte at the end (null terminated) for convenience. This extra byte is not included in SDL_AsyncIOOutcome's bytes_transferred value.
This function will allocate the buffer to contain the file. It must be deallocated by calling SDL_free() on SDL_AsyncIOOutcome's buffer field after completion.
An SDL_AsyncIOQueue must be specified. The newly-created task will be added to it when it completes its work.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileStream.SDL_IOFromFile(System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Use this function to create a new SDL_IOStream structure for reading from and/or writing to a named file
member.param: A UTF-8 string representing the filename to open
An ASCII string representing the mode to be used for opening the file
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the SDL_IOStream structure that is created or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The string is treated roughly the same as in a call to the C library's fopen(), even if SDL doesn't happen to use fopen() behind the scenes.

Available strings:
"r" Open a file for reading. The file must exist.
"w"
Create an empty file for writing. If a file with the same name already exists its content is erased and the file is treated as a new empty file.
"a" Append to a file. Writing operations append data at the end of the file. The file is created if it does not exist.
"r+" Open a file for update both reading and writing. The file must exist.
"w+"
Create an empty file for both reading and writing. If a file with the same name already exists its content is erased and the file is treated as a new empty file.
"a+"
Open a file for reading and appending. All writing operations are performed at the end of the file, protecting the previous content to be overwritten. You can reposition (fseek, rewind) the internal pointer to anywhere in the file for reading, but writing operations will move it back to the end of file. The file is created if it does not exist.
NOTE: In order to open a file as a binary file, a "b" character has to be included in the mode string. This additional "b" character can either be appended at the end of the string (thus making the following compound modes: "rb", "wb", "ab", "r+b", "w+b", "a+b") or be inserted between the letter and the "+" sign for the mixed modes ("rb+", "wb+", "ab+"). Additional characters may follow the sequence, although they should have no effect. For example, "t" is sometimes appended to make explicit the file is a text file.
This function supports Unicode filenames, but they must be encoded in UTF-8 format, regardless of the underlying operating system.
In Android, SDL_IOFromFile() can be used to open content:// URIs. As a fallback, SDL_IOFromFile() will transparently open a matching filename in the app's assets.
Closing the SDL_IOStream will close SDL's internal file handle.

The following properties may be set at creation time by SDL:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_WINDOWS_HANDLE_POINTER
A pointer, that can be cast to a win32 HANDLE, that this SDL_IOStream is using to access the filesystem. If the program isn't running on Windows, or SDL used some other method to access the filesystem, this property will not be set.
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_STDIO_FILE_POINTER
A pointer, that can be cast to a stdio FILE *, that this SDL_IOStream is using to access the filesystem. If SDL used some other method to access the filesystem, this property will not be set. PLEASE NOTE that if SDL is using a different C runtime than your app, trying to use this pointer will almost certainly result in a crash! This is mostly a problem on Windows; make sure you build SDL and your app with the same compiler and settings to avoid it.
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_FILE_DESCRIPTOR_NUMBER A file descriptor that this SDL_IOStream is using to access the filesystem
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_ANDROID_AASSET_POINTER
A pointer, that can be cast to an Android NDK AAsset *, that this SDL_IOStream is using to access the filesystem. If SDL used some other method to access the filesystem, this property will not be set.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem
member.summary: Provides static methods and properties to access file system related functionality

remarks.para: SDL offers an APIs for examining and manipulating the system's file system. This covers most things one would need to do with directories, except for actual file I/O (which is covered by , , , and partially by instead).

There are the members to answer necessary path questions:
Where is my app's data?
Where can I safely write files?
Where are paths like Downloads, Desktop, Music?
What is this thing at this location?
What items live in this folder? or
What items live in this folder by wildcard?
What is my current working directory?
SDL'S APIs also offers functionality to manipulate the directory tree: renaming, removing, copying files.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.BasePath
member.summary: Gets the path where the application was run from
member.value: The path where the application was run from, or on error or when the plaform does not support this functionality (check for more information)

remarks.para: While SDL caches the value of this property internally, the first access to this property is not necessarily fast, so plan accordingly.

description.para: If the application is in a ".app" bundle, the value of this property will be the resource directory (e.g. "MyApp.app/Contents/Resources/"). This behaviour can be overridden by adding a property to the Info.plist file. Adding a string key with the name SDL_FILESYSTEM_BASE_DIR_TYPE with a supported value will change the behaviour.

Supported values for the SDL_FILESYSTEM_BASE_DIR_TYPE property are:
resource The bundle's resource directory. For example: "/Applications/SDLApp/MyApp.app/". This is the default.
bundle The bundle directory. For example: "/Applications/SDLApp/MyApp.app/".
parent The containing directory of the bundle. For example: "/Applications/SDLApp/".
The value of this property will be "romfs" directory of the application, as it is uncommon to store resources outside the executable. As such it is not a writable directory.

remarks.para: The value of this property is guaranteed to end with a directory separator character ('\\' on Windows; '/' on most other platforms).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.CurrentDirectory
member.summary: Gets what the system considers the "current working directory"
member.value: The current working directory, or on error

remarks.para: For systems without a concept of a current working directory, this will still attempt to provide something reasonable.
SDL does not provide the means to change the current working directory (for platforms without this concept). Please use the provided .NET APIs to change the current working directory if needed.
The value of this property is guaranteed to end with a directory separator character ('\\' on Windows; '/' on most other platforms).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.TryCopyFile(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Tries to copy a file from one path to another
member.param: The original file path
The new file path
member.returns: , if the file was copied successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: If the file at already exists, it will be overwritten with the contents of the file at .

This method will block until the copy is complete, which might be a significant time for large files on slow disks. On some platforms, the copy can be handed off to the OS itself, but on others SDL might just open both paths, and read from one and write to the other.

Note: this is not an atomic operation! If something tries to read from while the copy is in progress, it will see an incomplete copy of the data, and if the calling thread terminates (or the power goes out) during the copy, 's previous contents will be gone, replaced with an incomplete copy of the data. To avoid this risk, it is recommended that you copy to a temporary file in the same directory as first, and if the copy is successful, use to replace with the temporary file. This will ensure that reads of will either see a complete copy of the data, or it will see the pre-copy state of the file at .

This method attempts to synchronize the newly-copied data to disk before returning, if the platform allows it, so that the renaming trick will not have a problem in a system crash or power failure, where the file could be renamed but the contents never made it from the system file cache to the physical disk.

If the copy operation fails for any reason, the state of the file system entry at is undefined. It might be half a copy, it might be the untouched data of what was already there, or it might be a zero-byte file, etc.
While it's safe to call this method from any thread, the operation itself is not atomic, so you'll might need to protect access to specific paths from other threads if appropriate.

If any parent directories in the specified path do not exist, they will be created as well. Please note, that if the operation fails at any point, parent directories that were already created will not be removed.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.TryGetPreferencesPath(System.String,System.String,System.String@)
member.summary: Tries to get the user-and-application-specific path where files can be written to
member.param: The name of your organization
The name of your application
The path to the preferences directory, when this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the preferences path was retrieved successfully; otherwise,

remarks.para: The "preferences path" is a directory, that is meant to store the user's personal files (preferences and save games, etc) that are specific to your application. This directory is unique per user, per application.
This method will decide the appropriate location in the native filesystem, create the directory if necessary, and result in the absolute path to that directory.

Examples for "preferences paths" are:
On Windows "C:\\Users\\bob\\AppData\\Roaming\\My Company\\My Program Name\\"
On Linux "/home/bob/.local/share/My Program Name/"
On macOS "/Users/bob/Library/Application Support/My Program Name/"

You should assume that the resulting path of a call to this method is the only safe place to write files to (and that , while it might be writable, or even the parent of the resulting path, isn't where you should be writing things).

Both the and may become part of a directory name, so please follow these rules:
Try to use the same value for (including case-sensitivity) for all your applications that use this functionality.
Always use an unique value for for each individual application, and make sure it never changes for an application once you've decided on it.
Unicode characters are legal, but you should only use letters, numbers, and spaces. Avoid punctuation like "Game Name 2: Bad Guy's Revenge!", "Game Name 2" is sufficient.

Due to historical mistakes, is allowed to be or the empty string (""). In such cases, SDL will omit the subdirectory for , including on platforms where it shouldn't, and including on platforms where this would make your app fail certification for an app store. Newly created application should definitely specify a non-, non-empty value for !
The resulting is guaranteed to end with a directory separator character ('\\' on Windows; '/' on most other platforms).

This method is specifically for user folders, which are meant for the user to access and manage. For application-specific folders, meant to hold data for the application to manage, see and .

member.summary: Copy a file
member.param: The old path
The new path
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the file at already exists, it will be overwritten with the contents of the file at .

This function will block until the copy is complete, which might be a significant time for large files on slow disks. On some platforms, the copy can be handed off to the OS itself, but on others SDL might just open both paths, and read from one and write to the other.

Note that this is not an atomic operation! If something tries to read from while the copy is in progress, it will see an incomplete copy of the data, and if the calling thread terminates (or the power goes out) during the copy, 's previous contents will be gone, replaced with an incomplete copy of the data. To avoid this risk, it is recommended that the app copy to a temporary file in the same directory as , and if the copy is successful, use SDL_RenamePath() to replace with the temporary file. This will ensure that reads of will either see a complete copy of the data, or it will see the pre-copy state of .

This function attempts to synchronize the newly-copied data to disk before returning, if the platform allows it, so that the renaming trick will not have a problem in a system crash or power failure, where the file could be renamed but the contents never made it from the system file cache to the physical disk.
If the copy fails for any reason, the state of is undefined. It might be half a copy, it might be the untouched data of what was already there, or it might be a zero-byte file, etc.
It is safe to call this function from any thread, but this operation is not atomic, so the app might need to protect access to specific paths from other threads if appropriate.

member.summary: Create a directory, and any missing parent directories
member.param: The path of the directory to create

If parent directories are missing, it will also create them. Note that if this fails, it will not remove any parent directories it already made.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_EnumerateDirectory(System.Byte*,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Enumerate a directory through a callback function
member.param: The path of the directory to enumerate
A function that is called for each entry in the directory
A pointer that is passed to
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function provides every directory entry through an app-provided callback, called once for each directory entry, until all results have been provided or the callback returns either SDL_ENUM_SUCCESS or SDL_ENUM_FAILURE.

This will return false if there was a system problem in general, or if a callback returns SDL_ENUM_FAILURE. A successful return means a callback returned SDL_ENUM_SUCCESS to halt enumeration, or all directory entries were enumerated.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_GetBasePath
member.summary: Get the directory where the application was run from
member.returns: Returns an absolute path in UTF-8 encoding to the application data directory. NULL will be returned on error or when the platform doesn't implement this functionality, call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: SDL caches the result of this call internally, but the first call to this function is not necessarily fast, so plan accordingly.

macOS and iOS Specific Functionality: If the application is in a ".app" bundle, this function returns the Resource directory (e.g. MyApp.app/Contents/Resources/). This behaviour can be overridden by adding a property to the Info.plist file. Adding a string key with the name SDL_FILESYSTEM_BASE_DIR_TYPE with a supported value will change the behaviour.

Supported values for the SDL_FILESYSTEM_BASE_DIR_TYPE property (Given an application in /Applications/SDLApp/MyApp.app):
resource Bundle resource directory (the default). For example: /Applications/SDLApp/MyApp.app/Contents/Resources
bundle The Bundle directory. For example: /Applications/SDLApp/MyApp.app/
parent The containing directory of the bundle. For example: /Applications/SDLApp/
Nintendo 3DS Specific Functionality: This function returns "romfs" directory of the application as it is uncommon to store resources outside the executable. As such it is not a writable directory.
The returned path is guaranteed to end with a path separator ('\' on Windows, '/' on most other platforms).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_GetCurrentDirectory
member.summary: Get what the system believes is the "current working directory"
member.returns: Returns a UTF-8 string of the current working directory in platform-dependent notation. NULL if there's a problem. This should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

remarks.para: For systems without a concept of a current working directory, this will still attempt to provide something reasonable.
SDL does not provide a means to change the current working directory; for platforms without this concept, this would cause surprises with file access outside of SDL.
The returned path is guaranteed to end with a path separator ('\' on Windows, '/' on most other platforms).

member.summary: Get information about a filesystem path
member.param: The path to query
A pointer filled in with information about the path, or NULL to check for the existence of a file
member.returns: Returns true on success or false if the file doesn't exist, or another failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_GetPrefPath(System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get the user-and-app-specific path where files can be written.
member.param: The name of your organization
The name of your application
member.returns: Returns a UTF-8 string of the user directory in platform-dependent notation. NULL if there's a problem (creating directory failed, etc.). This should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

remarks.para: Get the "pref dir". This is meant to be where users can write personal files (preferences and save games, etc) that are specific to your application. This directory is unique per user, per application.
This function will decide the appropriate location in the native filesystem, create the directory if necessary, and return a string of the absolute path to the directory in UTF-8 encoding.
On Windows, the string might look like: C:\Users\bob\AppData\Roaming\My Company\My Program Name\
On Linux, the string might look like: /home/bob/.local/share/My Program Name/
On macOS, the string might look like: /Users/bob/Library/Application Support/My Program Name/

You should assume the path returned by this function is the only safe place to write files (and that SDL_GetBasePath(), while it might be writable, or even the parent of the returned path, isn't where you should be writing things).

Both the org and app strings may become part of a directory name, so please follow these rules:
Try to use the same org string (including case-sensitivity) for all your applications that use this function
Always use a unique app string for each one, and make sure it never changes for an app once you've decided on it
Unicode characters are legal, as long as they are UTF-8 encoded, but...
...only use letters, numbers, and spaces. Avoid punctuation like "Game Name 2: Bad Guy's Revenge!" ... "Game Name 2" is sufficient

Due to historical mistakes, is allowed to be NULL or "". In such cases, SDL will omit the org subdirectory, including on platforms where it shouldn't, and including on platforms where this would make your app fail certification for an app store. New apps should definitely specify a real string for .
The returned path is guaranteed to end with a path separator ('\' on Windows, '/' on most other platforms).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_GetUserFolder(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Folder)
member.summary: Finds the most suitable user folder for a specific purpose
member.param: The type of folder to find
member.returns: Returns either a null-terminated C string containing the full path to the folder, or NULL if an error happened

remarks.para: Many OSes provide certain standard folders for certain purposes, such as storing pictures, music or videos for a certain user. This function gives the path for many of those special locations.

This function is specifically for user folders, which are meant for the user to access and manage. For application-specific folders, meant to hold data for the application to manage, see SDL_GetBasePath() and SDL_GetPrefPath().
The returned path is guaranteed to end with a path separator ('\' on Windows, '/' on most other platforms).
If NULL is returned, the error may be obtained with SDL_GetError().

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_GlobDirectory(System.Byte*,System.Byte*,Sdl3Sharp.IO.GlobFlags,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Enumerate a directory tree, filtered by pattern, and return a list
member.param: The path of the directory to enumerate
The pattern that files in the directory must match. Can be NULL.
SDL_GLOB_* bitflags that affect this search
On return, will be set to the number of items in the returned array. Can be NULL.
member.returns: Returns an array of strings on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

remarks.para: Files are filtered out if they don't match the string in , which may contain wildcard characters * (match everything) and ? (match one character). If pattern is NULL, no filtering is done and all results are returned. Subdirectories are permitted, and are specified with a path separator of /. Wildcard characters * and ? never match a path separator.
may be set to SDL_GLOB_CASEINSENSITIVE to make the pattern matching case-insensitive.
The returned array is always NULL-terminated, for your iterating convenience, but if is non-NULL, on return it will contain the number of items in the array, not counting the NULL terminator.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_RemovePath(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Remove a file or an empty directory
member.param: The path to remove from the filesystem
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Directories that are not empty will fail; this function will not recursely delete directory trees.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.FileSystem.SDL_RenamePath(System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Rename a file or directory
member.param: The old path
The new path
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the file at already exists, it will be replaced.
Note that this will not copy files across filesystems/drives/volumes, as that is a much more complicated (and possibly time-consuming) operation.

Which is to say, if this function fails, SDL_CopyFile() to a temporary file in the same directory as newpath, then SDL_RenamePath() from the temporary file to and SDL_RemovePath() on might work for files. Renaming a non-empty directory across filesystems is dramatically more complex, however.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.MemoryStream.SDL_IOFromMem(System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Use this function to prepare a read-write memory buffer for use with SDL_IOStream
member.param: A pointer to a buffer to feed an SDL_IOStream stream
The buffer size, in bytes
member.returns: Returns a pointer to a new SDL_IOStream structure or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function sets up an SDL_IOStream struct based on a memory area of a certain size, for both read and write access.
This memory buffer is not copied by the SDL_IOStream; the pointer you provide must remain valid until you close the stream.
If you need to make sure the SDL_IOStream never writes to the memory buffer, you should use SDL_IOFromConstMem() with a read-only buffer of memory instead.

The following properties will be set at creation time by SDL:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_POINTER This will be the parameter that was passed to this function
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_SIZE_NUMBER This will be the parameter that was passed to this function
Additionally, the following properties are recognized:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_FREE_FUNC_POINTER
If this property is set to a non-NULL value it will be interpreted as a function of SDL_free_func type and called with the passed pointer when closing the stream. By default it is unset, i.e., the memory will not be freed.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.ReadOnlyMemoryStream.SDL_IOFromConstMem(System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Use this function to prepare a read-only memory buffer for use with SDL_IOStream
member.param: A pointer to a read-only buffer to feed an SDL_IOStream stream
The buffer size, in bytes.
member.returns: Returns a pointer to a new SDL_IOStream structure or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function sets up an SDL_IOStream struct based on a memory area of a certain size. It assumes the memory area is not writable.
Attempting to write to this SDL_IOStream stream will report an error without writing to the memory buffer.
This memory buffer is not copied by the SDL_IOStream; the pointer you provide must remain valid until you close the stream.
If you need to write to a memory buffer, you should use SDL_IOFromMem() with a writable buffer of memory instead.

The following properties will be set at creation time by SDL:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_POINTER This will be the parameter that was passed to this function
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_SIZE_NUMBER This will be the parameter that was passed to this function
Additionally, the following properties are recognized:
SDL_PROP_IOSTREAM_MEMORY_FREE_FUNC_POINTER
If this property is set to a non-NULL value it will be interpreted as a function of SDL_free_func type and called with the passed pointer when closing the stream. By default it is unset, i.e., the memory will not be freed.

remarks.para: This constructor does its best to detect the capabilities of the provided SDL . But in some cases it might not be possible to determine whether the stream can be read from, seeked or written to, or the detection might be wrong. In those cases, you can use the optional , and parameters to explicitly specify the capabilities of the stream. Note that, for example specifying for for an inherently read-only stream will not magically make it writable, but it will make this wrapper report that it is writable.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage
member.summary: A base storage container for abstracting file storage operations

remarks.para: All paths in the Storage API use Unix-style path separators ('/'). Using a different path separator will not work, even if the underlying platform would otherwise accept it. This is to keep code using the Storage API portable between platforms and implementations and simplify app code.
Paths with relative directories ("." and "..") are forbidden by the Storage API.

All valid Unicode strings (discounting the '\0' character and the '/' path separator character) are usable for filenames, however, an underlying implementation may not support particularly strange sequences and refuse to create files with those names.
This constructor does neiter nor fail otherwise

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the storage container is ready to use
member.value: A value indicating whether the storage container is ready to use

remarks.para: The value of this property should be examined in regular intervals until it becomes . However, it is not recommended to spin-wait on examining this property, as the backend may depend on a synchronous message loop. You might instead poll the value of this property in your app's main loop while processing events and drawing some kind of loading indicator.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.TryGlobDirectory(System.String,System.String,Sdl3Sharp.IO.GlobFlags,System.String[]@)
member.summary: Tries to enumerate entries in a directory within the storage container, filtered by a pattern
member.param: The path to the directory to enumerate, or or the empty string ("") to request the root of the storage container
The pattern that entries must match. Can be to not filter at all.
Flags to modify the pattern matching behavior
The matching entries, when this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the directory was successfully enumerated and entries were filtered; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: Entries are filtered out if they don't match the , which may contain wildcard characters '*' (match everything) and '?' (match one character). If is , no filtering is done and all results are returned. Subdirectories are permitted, and are specified with a path separator of '/'. Wildcard characters '*' and '?' never match a path separator.
may be set to to make the pattern matching case-insensitive.
If is or the empty string (""), this is treated as a request to enumerate the root of the storage container.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_EnumerateStorageDirectory(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_Storage*,System.Byte*,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Enumerate a directory in a storage container through a callback function
member.param: A storage container
The path of the directory to enumerate, or NULL for the root
A function that is called for each entry in the directory
A pointer that is passed to
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function provides every directory entry through an app-provided callback, called once for each directory entry, until all results have been provided or the callback returns either SDL_ENUM_SUCCESS or SDL_ENUM_FAILURE.

This will return false if there was a system problem in general, or if a callback returns SDL_ENUM_FAILURE. A successful return means a callback returned SDL_ENUM_SUCCESS to halt enumeration, or all directory entries were enumerated.
If is NULL, this is treated as a request to enumerate the root of the storage container's tree. An empty string also works for this.

SDL_GLOB_* bitflags that affect this search.
On return, will be set to the number of items in the returned array. Can be NULL.
member.returns: Returns an array of strings on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information. The caller should pass the returned pointer to SDL_free when done with it. This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

remarks.para: Files are filtered out if they don't match the string in , which may contain wildcard characters * (match everything) and ? (match one character). If pattern is NULL, no filtering is done and all results are returned. Subdirectories are permitted, and are specified with a path separator of '/'. Wildcard characters * and ? never match a path separator.
may be set to SDL_GLOB_CASEINSENSITIVE to make the pattern matching case-insensitive.
The returned array is always NULL-terminated, for your iterating convenience, but if is non-NULL, on return it will contain the number of items in the array, not counting the NULL terminator.
If is NULL, this is treated as a request to enumerate the root of the storage container's tree. An empty string also works for this.
It is safe to call this function from any thread, assuming the object is thread-safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_OpenStorage(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_StorageInterface*,System.Void*)
member.summary: Opens up a container using a client-provided storage interface
member.param: The interface that implements this storage, initialized using SDL_INIT_INTERFACE()
The pointer that will be passed to the interface functions
member.returns: Returns a storage container on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Applications do not need to use this function unless they are providing their own SDL_Storage implementation. If you just need an SDL_Storage, you should use the built-in implementations in SDL, like SDL_OpenTitleStorage() or SDL_OpenUserStorage().
This function makes a copy of and the caller does not need to keep it around after this call.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_ReadStorageFile(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_Storage*,System.Byte*,System.Void*,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Synchronously read a file from a storage container into a client-provided buffer
member.param: A storage container to read from
The relative path of the file to read
A client-provided buffer to read the file into
The length of the destination buffer
member.returns: Returns true if the file was read or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The value of must match the length of the file exactly; call SDL_GetStorageFileSize() to get this value. This behavior may be relaxed in a future release.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_StorageReady(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Storage.SDL_Storage*)
member.summary: Checks if the storage container is ready to use
member.param: A storage container to query
member.returns: Returns true if the container is ready, false otherwise

remarks.para: This function should be called in regular intervals until it returns true - however, it is not recommended to spinwait on this call, as the backend may depend on a synchronous message loop. You might instead poll this in your game's main loop while processing events and drawing a loading screen.

remarks.para: Inspecting the status of the stream can be useful to determine if a short read or write operation was due to an error, due to reaching the end of the stream, or due to a non-blocking operation that hasn't finished.
The value of this property is only expected to change after read or write operations on the stream. Don't expect it to change spontaneously.
This property is not threadsafe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.TryClose
member.summary: Tries to close the stream and releases associated resources
member.returns: if the stream was closed without failure; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: Note: this method returns if the stream couldn't be flushed successfully before closing. The stream is still invalid when this method returns.

A call to this method flushes any buffered writes to the operating system, but there are no guarantees that those writes have gone to physical media; they might be in the OS's file cache, waiting to go to disk later. If it's absolutely crucial that writes go to disk immediately, so they are definitely stored even if the power fails before the file cache would have caught up, one should call before closing. Note that flushing takes time and makes the system and your app operate less efficiently, so do so sparingly.
This method is not threadsafe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.TryFlush
member.summary: Tries to flush any buffered data in the stream
member.returns: if the flush was successful; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: A call to this method makes sure that any buffered data is written to the stream. Normally this isn't necessary but if the stream is a pipe or socket it guarantees that any pending data is sent.
This method is not threadsafe.

A value indicating whether the stream should be closed before this method returns (even in case of an error)
member.returns: , if all available data from the stream was succesfully read; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The resulting should be disposed if the memory it's managing is no longer needed. That also frees the allocated memory.
This method is not threadsafe.

member.returns: if the 32-bit unsigned integer value was successfully read; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The value is expected to be stored in the stream in little-endian byte order. The resulting will be in the endianness of the current platform.

This method will return , if the end of the stream was reached before completely reading the 32-bit unsigned integer value ( will be ). If this method returns and the is not , an error occured and you should check for more information.
This method is not threadsafe.

The number of bytes written to the stream

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.TryWrite(System.String,System.UIntPtr@,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Object})
member.summary: Tries to write a formatted string into the stream
member.param: The C-style printf format string to be written into the stream
The number of bytes written to the stream
The arguments corresponding to the format specifiers in
member.returns: if the formatted string was successfully written into the stream; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The parameter is interpreted as a C-style printf format string, and the parameter supplies the values for its format specifiers. Supported argument types include all integral types up to 64-bit (including and ), floating point types ( and ), pointer types ( and ), and .
For a detailed explanation of C-style printf format strings and their specifiers, see .
Consider using instead when possible, as it may be more efficient. In many cases, you can use C# string interpolation to construct the string before writing.
This method is not threadsafe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_CloseIO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*)
member.summary: Close and free an allocated SDL_IOStream structure
member.param: SDL_IOStream structure to close
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: SDL_CloseIO() closes and cleans up the SDL_IOStream stream. It releases any resources used by the stream and frees the SDL_IOStream itself. This returns true on success, or false if the stream failed to flush to its output (e.g. to disk).
Note that if this fails to flush the stream for any reason, this function reports an error, but the SDL_IOStream is still invalid once this function returns.

This call flushes any buffered writes to the operating system, but there are no guarantees that those writes have gone to physical media; they might be in the OS's file cache, waiting to go to disk later. If it's absolutely crucial that writes go to disk immediately, so they are definitely stored even if the power fails before the file cache would have caught up, one should call SDL_FlushIO() before closing. Note that flushing takes time and makes the system and your app operate less efficiently, so do so sparingly.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_FlushIO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*)
member.summary: Flush any buffered data in the stream
member.param: SDL_IOStream structure to flush
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function makes sure that any buffered data is written to the stream. Normally this isn't necessary but if the stream is a pipe or socket it guarantees that any pending data is sent.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_GetIOProperties(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*)
member.summary: Get the properties associated with an SDL_IOStream
member.param: A pointer to an SDL_IOStream structure
member.returns: Returns a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_GetIOSize(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*)
member.summary: Use this function to get the size of the data stream in an SDL_IOStream
member.param: The SDL_IOStream to get the size of the data stream from
member.returns: Returns the size of the data stream in the SDL_IOStream on success or a negative error code on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_GetIOStatus(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*)
member.summary: Query the stream status of an SDL_IOStream
member.param: The SDL_IOStream to query
member.returns: Returns an SDL_IOStatus enum with the current state

remarks.para: This information can be useful to decide if a short read or write was due to an error, an EOF, or a non-blocking operation that isn't yet ready to complete.
An SDL_IOStream's status is only expected to change after a SDL_ReadIO or SDL_WriteIO call; don't expect it to change if you just call this query function in a tight loop.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOprintf(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Print to an SDL_IOStream data stream
member.param: A pointer to an SDL_IOStream structure
A printf() style format string
member.returns: Returns the number of bytes written or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function does formatted printing to the stream.
This function is not thread safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_LoadFile_IO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*,System.UIntPtr*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Load all the data from an SDL data stream
member.param: The SDL_IOStream to read all available data from
A pointer filled in with the number of bytes read, may be NULL
if true, calls SDL_CloseIO() on src before returning, even in the case of an error
member.returns: Returns the data or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: The data is allocated with a zero byte at the end (null terminated) for convenience. This extra byte is not included in the value reported via .
The data should be freed with SDL_free().
This function is not thread safe.

member.summary: Create a custom SDL_IOStream
member.param: The interface that implements this SDL_IOStream, initialized using SDL_INIT_INTERFACE()
The pointer that will be passed to the interface functions
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the allocated memory on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Applications do not need to use this function unless they are providing their own SDL_IOStream implementation. If you just need an SDL_IOStream to read/write a common data source, you should use the built-in implementations in SDL, like SDL_IOFromFile() or SDL_IOFromMem(), etc.
This function makes a copy of and the caller does not need to keep it around after this call.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_ReadIO(Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*,System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Read from a data source
member.param: A pointer to an SDL_IOStream structure
A pointer to a buffer to read data into
The number of bytes to read from the data source
member.returns: Returns the number of bytes read, or 0 on end of file or other failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function reads up bytes from the data source to the area pointed at by . This function may read less bytes than requested.

This function will return zero when the data stream is completely read, and SDL_GetIOStatus() will return SDL_IO_STATUS_EOF. If zero is returned and the stream is not at EOF, SDL_GetIOStatus() will return a different error value and SDL_GetError() will offer a human-readable message.
This function is not thread safe.

member.param: The stream from which to read data
A pointer filled in with the data read
member.returns: Returns true on successful read or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: SDL byteswaps the data only if necessary, so the data returned will be in the native byte order.

This function will return false when the data stream is completely read, and SDL_GetIOStatus() will return SDL_IO_STATUS_EOF. If false is returned and the stream is not at EOF, SDL_GetIOStatus() will return a different error value and SDL_GetError() will offer a human-readable message.
This function is not thread safe.
