member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_CreateThreadRuntime(,System.Byte*,System.Void*,,)
member.summary: Create a new thread with a default stack size
member.param: the SDL_ThreadFunction function to call in the new thread
the name of the thread
a pointer that is passed to fn
set to NULL
set to NULL
member.returns: Returns an opaque pointer to the new thread object on success, NULL if the new thread could not be created; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
This is a convenience function, equivalent to calling SDL_CreateThreadWithProperties with the following properties set:
DL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_ENTRY_FUNCTION_POINTER fn
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_NAME_STRING name
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_USERDATA_POINTER data

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_CreateThreadWithPropertiesRuntime(System.UInt32,,)
member.summary: Create a new thread with with the specified properties
member.param: the properties to use
set to NULL
set to NULL
member.returns: Returns an opaque pointer to the new thread object on success, NULL if the new thread could not be created; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
These are the supported properties:
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_ENTRY_FUNCTION_POINTER an SDL_ThreadFunction value that will be called at the start of the new thread's life. Required.
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_NAME_STRING the name of the new thread, which might be available to debuggers. Optional, defaults to NULL.
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_USERDATA_POINTER an arbitrary app-defined pointer, which is passed to the entry function on the new thread, as its only parameter. Optional, defaults to NULL.
SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_STACKSIZE_NUMBER the size, in bytes, of the new thread's stack. Optional, defaults to 0 (system-defined default).
SDL makes an attempt to report SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_NAME_STRING to the system, so that debuggers can display it. Not all platforms support this.
Thread naming is a little complicated: Most systems have very small limits for the string length (Haiku has 32 bytes, Linux currently has 16, Visual C++ 6.0 has nine!), and possibly other arbitrary rules. You'll have to see what happens with your system's debugger. The name should be UTF-8 (but using the naming limits of C identifiers is a better bet). There are no requirements for thread naming conventions, so long as the string is null-terminated UTF-8, but these guidelines are helpful in choosing a name:
If a system imposes requirements, SDL will try to munge the string for it (truncate, etc), but the original string contents will be available from SDL_GetThreadName().
The size (in bytes) of the new stack can be specified with SDL_PROP_THREAD_CREATE_STACKSIZE_NUMBER. Zero means "use the system default" which might be wildly different between platforms. x86 Linux generally defaults to eight megabytes, an embedded device might be a few kilobytes instead. You generally need to specify a stack that is a multiple of the system's page size (in many cases, this is 4 kilobytes, but check your system documentation).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Delay(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Wait a specified number of milliseconds before returning
member.param: the number of milliseconds to delay
member.remarks: This function waits a specified number of milliseconds before returning. It waits at least the specified time, but possibly longer due to OS scheduling.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_DelayNS(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Wait a specified number of nanoseconds before returning
member.param: the number of nanoseconds to delay
member.remarks: This function waits a specified number of nanoseconds before returning. It waits at least the specified time, but possibly longer due to OS scheduling.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_DelayPrecise(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Wait a specified number of nanoseconds before returning
member.param: the number of nanoseconds to delay
member.remarks: This function waits a specified number of nanoseconds before returning. It will attempt to wait as close to the requested time as possible, busy waiting if necessary, but could return later due to OS scheduling.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_DetachThread(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Thread*)
member.summary: Let a thread clean up on exit without intervention
member.param: the SDL_Thread pointer that was returned from the SDL_CreateThread() call that started this thread
member.remarks:
A thread may be "detached" to signify that it should not remain until another thread has called SDL_WaitThread() on it. Detaching a thread is useful for long-running threads that nothing needs to synchronize with or further manage. When a detached thread is done, it simply goes away.
There is no way to recover the return code of a detached thread. If you need this, don't detach the thread and instead use SDL_WaitThread().
Once a thread is detached, you should usually assume the SDL_Thread isn't safe to reference again, as it will become invalid immediately upon the detached thread's exit, instead of remaining until someone has called SDL_WaitThread() to finally clean it up. As such, don't detach the same thread more than once.
If a thread has already exited when passed to SDL_DetachThread(), it will stop waiting for a call to SDL_WaitThread() and clean up immediately. It is not safe to detach a thread that might be used with SDL_WaitThread().
You may not call SDL_WaitThread() on a thread that has been detached. Use either that function or this one, but not both, or behavior is undefined.
It is safe to pass NULL to this function; it is a no-op.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_GetCurrentThreadID
member.summary: Get the thread identifier for the current thread
member.returns: Returns the ID of the current thread
member.remarks:
This thread identifier is as reported by the underlying operating system. If SDL is running on a platform that does not support threads the return value will always be zero.
This function also returns a valid thread ID when called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_GetThreadID(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Thread*)
member.summary: Get the thread identifier for the specified thread
member.param: the thread to query
member.returns: Returns the ID of the specified thread, or the ID of the current thread if thread is NULL
member.remarks: This thread identifier is as reported by the underlying operating system. If SDL is running on a platform that does not support threads the return value will always be zero.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_GetThreadName(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Thread*)
member.summary: Get the thread name as it was specified in SDL_CreateThread()
member.param: the thread to query
member.returns: Returns a pointer to a UTF-8 string that names the specified thread, or NULL if it doesn't have a name

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_GetThreadState(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Thread*)
member.summary: Get the current state of a thread
member.param: the thread to query
member.returns: Returns the current state of a thread, or SDL_THREAD_UNKNOWN if the thread isn't valid

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_IsMainThread
member.summary: Return whether this is the main thread
member.returns: Returns true if this thread is the main thread, or false otherwise
member.remarks: On Apple platforms, the main thread is the thread that runs your program's main() entry point. On other platforms, the main thread is the one that calls SDL_Init(SDL_INIT_VIDEO), which should usually be the one that runs your program's main() entry point. If you are using the main callbacks, SDL_AppInit(), SDL_AppIterate(), and SDL_AppQuit() are all called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_RunOnMainThread(,System.Void*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Call a function on the main thread during event processing
member.param: the callback to call on the main thread
a pointer that is passed to callback
true to wait for the callback to complete, false to return immediately
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
If this is called on the main thread, the callback is executed immediately. If this is called on another thread, this callback is queued for execution on the main thread during event processing.
Be careful of deadlocks when using this functionality. You should not have the main thread wait for the current thread while this function is being called with wait_complete true.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_SetCurrentThreadPriority(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.ThreadPriority)
member.summary: Set the priority for the current thread
member.param: the SDL_ThreadPriority to set
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks: Note that some platforms will not let you alter the priority (or at least, promote the thread to a higher priority) at all, and some require you to be an administrator account. Be prepared for this to fail.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_WaitThread(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.Thread.SDL_Thread*,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Wait for a thread to finish
member.param: the SDL_Thread pointer that was returned from the SDL_CreateThread() call that started this thread
a pointer filled in with the value returned from the thread function by its 'return', or -1 if the thread has been detached or isn't valid, may be NULL
member.remarks:
Threads that haven't been detached will remain until this function cleans them up. Not doing so is a resource leak.
Once a thread has been cleaned up through this function, the SDL_Thread that references it becomes invalid and should not be referenced again. As such, only one thread may call SDL_WaitThread() on another.
The return code from the thread function is placed in the area pointed to by status, if status is not NULL.
You may not wait on a thread that has been used in a call to SDL_DetachThread(). Use either that function or this one, but not both, or behavior is undefined.
It is safe to pass a NULL thread to this function; it is a no-op.
Note that the thread pointer is freed by this function and is not valid afterward.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.ThreadLocal.SDL_CleanupTLS
member.summary: Cleanup all TLS data for this thread
member.remarks: If you are creating your threads outside of SDL and then calling SDL functions, you should call this function before your thread exits, to properly clean up SDL memory

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.ThreadLocal.SDL_GetTLS(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.AtomicInt32*)
member.summary: Get the current thread's value associated with a thread local storage ID
member.param: a pointer to the thread local storage ID, may not be NULL
member.returns: Returns the value associated with the ID for the current thread or NULL if no value has been set; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Threading.ThreadLocal.SDL_SetTLS(Sdl3Sharp.Threading.AtomicInt32*,System.Void*,)
member.summary: Set the current thread's value associated with a thread local storage ID
member.param: a pointer to the thread local storage ID, may not be NULL
the value to associate with the ID for the current thread
a function called when the thread exits, to free the value, may be NULL
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
If the thread local storage ID is not initialized (the value is 0), a new ID will be created in a thread-safe way, so all calls using a pointer to the same ID will refer to the same local storage.
Note that replacing a value from a previous call to this function on the same thread does not call the previous value's destructor!
destructor can be NULL; it is assumed that value does not need to be cleaned up if so.

member.summary: Represents a SDL thread priority

remarks.para: SDL will make system changes as necessary in order to apply the thread priority. Code which attempts to control thread state related to priority should be aware that calling may alter such state. can be used to control aspects of this behavior.
This explicitly does not represent a Unix nice level!

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Timing.Timer.SDL_AddTimer(System.UInt32,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Call a callback function at a future time
member.param: the timer delay, in milliseconds, passed to callback
the SDL_TimerCallback function to call when the specified interval elapses
a pointer that is passed to callback
member.returns: Returns a timer ID or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
The callback function is passed the current timer interval and the user supplied parameter from the SDL_AddTimer() call and should return the next timer interval. If the value returned from the callback is 0, the timer is canceled and will be removed.
The callback is run on a separate thread, and for short timeouts can potentially be called before this function returns.
Timers take into account the amount of time it took to execute the callback. For example, if the callback took 250 ms to execute and returned 1000 (ms), the timer would only wait another 750 ms before its next iteration.
Timing may be inexact due to OS scheduling. Be sure to note the current time with SDL_GetTicksNS() or SDL_GetPerformanceCounter() in case your callback needs to adjust for variances.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Timing.Timer.SDL_AddTimerNS(System.UInt64,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Call a callback function at a future time
member.param: the timer delay, in nanoseconds, passed to callback
the SDL_TimerCallback function to call when the specified interval elapses
a pointer that is passed to callback
member.returns: Returns a timer ID or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
The callback function is passed the current timer interval and the user supplied parameter from the SDL_AddTimerNS() call and should return the next timer interval. If the value returned from the callback is 0, the timer is canceled and will be removed.
The callback is run on a separate thread, and for short timeouts can potentially be called before this function returns.
Timers take into account the amount of time it took to execute the callback. For example, if the callback took 250 ns to execute and returned 1000 (ns), the timer would only wait another 750 ns before its next iteration.
Timing may be inexact due to OS scheduling. Be sure to note the current time with SDL_GetTicksNS() or SDL_GetPerformanceCounter() in case your callback needs to adjust for variances.

remarks.para: The list of items () must be previously sorted (e.g. by using ) or else the result will be unrelyable.

Note: The result of this method is somewhat different to SDL's native equivalent. member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.#ctor(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new set of environment variables
member.param: Indicates whether the newly created should be initialized with the environment variables from the C runtime environment

remarks.para: If is set to (its default value), it is safe to call this constructor from any thread, otherwise it is only safe to call, if there are no other threads that are calling or .

In contrast to most of the remaining API which uses the Try-method pattern, this constructor intentionally fails by throwing an exception. If you want to handle failures wrap the call to this constructor in a -block, and check for more information when ing a .

member.remarks: In contrast to most of the remaining API which uses the Try-method pattern, this method intentionally fails by throwing an exception. If you want to handle failures wrap the call to this method in a -block, and check for more information when ing a .

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_CreateEnvironment(Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Create a set of environment variables
member.param: true to initialize it from the C runtime environment, false to create an empty environment
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the new environment or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks: If is false, it is safe to call this function from any thread, otherwise it is safe if no other threads are calling setenv() or unsetenv()

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_DestroyEnvironment(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_Environment*)
member.summary: Destroy a set of environment variables
member.param: the environment to destroy
member.remarks: It is safe to call this function from any thread, as long as the environment is no longer in use.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_GetEnvironment
member.summary: Get the process environment
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the environment for the process or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks: This is initialized at application start and is not affected by setenv() and unsetenv() calls after that point. Use SDL_SetEnvironmentVariable() and SDL_UnsetEnvironmentVariable() if you want to modify this environment, or SDL_setenv_unsafe() or SDL_unsetenv_unsafe() if you want changes to persist in the C runtime environment after SDL_Quit().

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_GetEnvironmentVariable(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_Environment*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get the value of a variable in the environment
member.param: the environment to query
the name of the variable to get
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the value of the variable or NULL if it can't be found

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_GetEnvironmentVariables(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_Environment*)
member.summary: Get all variables in the environment
member.param: the environment to query
member.returns: Returns a NULL terminated array of pointers to environment variables in the form "variable=value" or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_SetEnvironmentVariable(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_Environment*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Set the value of a variable in the environment
member.param: the environment to modify
the name of the variable to set
the value of the variable to set
true to overwrite the variable if it exists, false to return success without setting the variable if it already exists
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_UnsetEnvironmentVariable(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_Environment*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Clear a variable from the environment
member.param: the environment to modify
the name of the variable to unset
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_getenv(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get the value of a variable in the environment
member.param: the name of the variable to get
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the value of the variable or NULL if it can't be found
member.remarks: This function uses SDL's cached copy of the environment and is thread-safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_getenv_unsafe(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get the value of a variable in the environment
member.param: the name of the variable to get
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the value of the variable or NULL if it can't be found
member.remarks: This function bypasses SDL's cached copy of the environment and is not thread-safe.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_setenv_unsafe(System.Byte*,System.Byte*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Set the value of a variable in the environment
member.param: the name of the variable to set
the value of the variable to set
1 to overwrite the variable if it exists, 0 to return success without setting the variable if it already exists
member.returns: Returns 0 on success, -1 on error
member.remarks: This function is not thread safe, consider using SDL_SetEnvironmentVariable() instead.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Environment.SDL_unsetenv_unsafe(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Clear a variable from the environment
member.param: the name of the variable to unset
member.returns: Returns 0 on success, -1 on error
member.remarks: This function is not thread safe, consider using SDL_UnsetEnvironmentVariable() instead.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.FileDialog.SDL_ShowFileDialogWithProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.FileDialogType,,System.Void*,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Create and launch a file dialog with the specified properties
member.param: The type of file dialog
A function pointer to be invoked when the user selects a file and accepts, or cancels the dialog, or an error occurs
An optional pointer to pass extra data to the callback when it will be invoked
The properties to use

remarks.para:
These are the supported properties:
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_FILTERS_POINTER
A pointer to a list of SDL_DialogFileFilter structs, which will be used as filters for file-based selections. Ignored if the dialog is an "Open Folder" dialog. If non-NULL, the array of filters must remain valid at least until the callback is invoked.
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_NFILTERS_NUMBER The number of filters in the array of filters, if it exists
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_WINDOW_POINTER The window that the dialog should be modal for
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_LOCATION_STRING The default folder or file to start the dialog at
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_MANY_BOOLEAN true to allow the user to select more than one entry
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_TITLE_STRING The title for the dialog
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_ACCEPT_STRING The label that the accept button should have
SDL_PROP_FILE_DIALOG_CANCEL_STRING The label that the cancel button should have
Note that each platform may or may not support any of the properties.
This function should be called only from the main thread. The callback may be invoked from the same thread or from a different one, depending on the OS's constraints.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.FileDialog.SDL_ShowOpenFileDialog(,System.Void*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.FileDialog.SDL_DialogFileFilter*,System.Int32,System.Byte*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Displays a dialog that lets the user select a file on their filesystem
member.param: A function pointer to be invoked when the user selects a file and accepts, or cancels the dialog, or an error occurs
An optional pointer to pass extra data to the callback when it will be invoked
The window that the dialog should be modal for, may be NULL. Not all platforms support this option.

A list of filters, may be NULL. Not all platforms support this option, and platforms that do support it may allow the user to ignore the filters. If non-NULL, it must remain valid at least until the callback is invoked.
The number of filters. Ignored if filters is NULL.
The default folder or file to start the dialog at, may be NULL. Not all platforms support this option.
If non-zero, the user will be allowed to select multiple entries. Not all platforms support this option.

remarks.para: This is an asynchronous function; it will return immediately, and the result will be passed to the callback.
The callback will be invoked with a null-terminated list of files the user chose. The list will be empty if the user canceled the dialog, and it will be NULL if an error occurred.
Note that the callback may be called from a different thread than the one the function was invoked on.
Depending on the platform, the user may be allowed to input paths that don't yet exist.
On Linux, dialogs may require XDG Portals, which requires DBus, which requires an event-handling loop. Apps that do not use SDL to handle events should add a call to in their main loop.
This function should be called only from the main thread. The callback may be invoked from the same thread or from a different one, depending on the OS's constraints.

This function should be called only from the main thread. The callback may be invoked from the same thread or from a different one, depending on the OS's constraints.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash
member.summary: Provides methods to calculate checksums and hash values for data blocks

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.Crc16(System.UInt16,System.Memory{System.Byte})
member.summary: Calculates and accumulates a CRC-16 checksum hash value
member.param: The current checksum for the data set, or 0 for a new data set
The new block of data to be added to the checksum
member.returns: A CRC-16 checksum hash value of all blocks in the data set
member.remarks: Calls to this method can be chained by passing in the previous return value as the argument for the parameter to add a new block of data to the checksum. This allows for the data set to be streamed. If you want to start a new data set, you can simply pass in 0 as the argument for the parameter.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.Crc32(System.UInt32,System.Memory{System.Byte})
member.summary: Calculates and accumulates a CRC-32 checksum hash value
member.param: The current checksum for the data set, or 0 for a new data set
The new block of data to be added to the checksum
member.returns: A CRC-32 checksum hash value of all blocks in the data set
member.remarks: Calls to this method can be chained by passing in the previous return value as the argument for the parameter to add a new block of data to the checksum. This allows for the data set to be streamed. If you want to start a new data set, you can simply pass in 0 as the argument for the parameter.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.Murmur3_32(System.Memory{System.Byte},System.UInt32)
member.summary: Calculates a 32-bit MurmurHash3 hash value for a block of data
member.param: The block of data to be hashed
A value that alters the resulting hash value
member.returns: A MurmurHash3 hash value for the given

remarks.para: If you aren't sure what to provide for the value, 0 is fine.
MurmurHash3 is not cryptographically secure, so it shouldn't be used for hashing top-secret data.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.SDL_crc16(System.UInt16,System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Calculate a CRC-16 value
member.param: The current checksum for this data set, or 0 for a new data set
A new block of data to add to the checksum
The size, in bytes, of the new block of data.
member.returns: Returns a CRC-16 checksum value of all blocks in the data set

remarks.para: This function can be called multiple times, to stream data to be checksummed in blocks. Each call must provide the previous CRC-16 return value to be updated with the next block. The first call to this function for a set of blocks should pass in a zero CRC value.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.SDL_crc32(System.UInt32,System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Calculate a CRC-32 value
member.param: The current checksum for this data set, or 0 for a new data set
A new block of data to add to the checksum
The size, in bytes, of the new block of data.
member.returns: Returns a CRC-32 checksum value of all blocks in the data set

remarks.para: This function can be called multiple times, to stream data to be checksummed in blocks. Each call must provide the previous CRC-32 return value to be updated with the next block. The first call to this function for a set of blocks should pass in a zero CRC value.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Hash.SDL_murmur3_32(System.Void*,System.UIntPtr,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Calculate a 32-bit MurmurHash3 value for a block of data
member.param: The data to be hashed
The size of data, in bytes
A value that alters the final hash value
member.returns: Returns a Murmur3 32-bit hash value

remarks.para: If you aren't sure what to provide for a seed, zero is fine. Murmur3 is not cryptographically secure, so it shouldn't be used for hashing top-secret data.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Math.SDL_acos(System.Double)
member.summary: Compute the arc cosine of
member.param: A floating point value
member.returns: Returns arc cosine of , in radians

remarks.para: The definition of y = acos(x) is x = cos(y).
Domain: -1 <= x <= 1
Range: 0 <= y <= Pi
This function operates on double-precision floating point values, use SDL_acosf for single-precision floats.

This function may use a different approximation across different versions, platforms and configurations. i.e, it can return a different value given the same input on different machines or operating systems, or if SDL is updated.

member.summary: Compute the arc tangent of
member.param: A floating point value
member.returns: Returns arc tangent of in radians, or 0 if x = 0

remarks.para: The definition of y = atan(x) is x = tan(y).
Domain: -INF <= x <= INF
Range: -Pi/2 <= y <= Pi/2
This function operates on double-precision floating point values, use SDL_atanf for single-precision floats.
To calculate the arc tangent of y / x, use SDL_atan2.

A floating point value of the denominator (x coordinate)

remarks.para: The definition of z = atan2(x, y) is y = x tan(z).
Domain: -INF <= x <= INF, -INF <= y <= INF
Range: -Pi <= z <= Pi
This function operates on double-precision floating point values, use SDL_atan2f for single-precision floats.
To calculate the arc tangent of y / x, use SDL_atan.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.MessageBox.SDL_ShowMessageBox(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.MessageBox.SDL_MessageBoxData*,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Create a modal message box
member.param: The SDL_MessageBoxData structure with title, text and other options
The pointer to which user id of hit button should be copied
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: If your needs aren't complex, it might be easier to use SDL_ShowSimpleMessageBox.

This function should be called on the thread that created the parent window, or on the main thread if the messagebox has no parent. It will block execution of that thread until the user clicks a button or closes the messagebox.
This function may be called at any time, even before SDL_Init(). This makes it useful for reporting errors like a failure to create a renderer or OpenGL context.
On X11, SDL rolls its own dialog box with X11 primitives instead of a formal toolkit like GTK+ or Qt.

Note that if SDL_Init() would fail because there isn't any available video target, this function is likely to fail for the same reasons. If this is a concern, check the return value from this function and fall back to writing to stderr if you can.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.MessageBox.SDL_ShowSimpleMessageBox(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.MessageBoxFlags,System.Byte*,System.Byte*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Display a simple modal message box
member.param: An SDL_MessageBoxFlags value
UTF-8 title text
UTF-8 message text
The parent window, or NULL for no parent
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If your needs aren't complex, this function is preferred over SDL_ShowMessageBox.

may be any of the following:
SDL_MESSAGEBOX_ERROR error dialog
SDL_MESSAGEBOX_WARNING error dialog
SDL_MESSAGEBOX_INFORMATION error dialog

This function should be called on the thread that created the parent window, or on the main thread if the messagebox has no parent. It will block execution of that thread until the user clicks a button or closes the messagebox.
This function may be called at any time, even before SDL_Init(). This makes it useful for reporting errors like a failure to create a renderer or OpenGL context.
On X11, SDL rolls its own dialog box with X11 primitives instead of a formal toolkit like GTK+ or Qt.

Note that if SDL_Init() would fail because there isn't any available video target, this function is likely to fail for the same reasons. If this is a concern, check the return value from this function and fall back to writing to stderr if you can.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory
member.summary: Represents an allocated native memory buffer of unspecified type. Also provides methods for managing native memory allocations, including managing SDL's internal allocators.

remarks.para: Note: Some of the static methods in this class require the caller to manually free the allocated memory using the appropriate free method (e.g., or ). Failure to do so may result in memory leaks.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_GetMemoryFunctions(*,*,*,*)
member.summary: Get the current set of SDL memory functions
member.param: filled with malloc function
filled with calloc function
filled with realloc function
filled with free function
member.remarks: This does not hold a lock, so do not call this in the unlikely event of a background thread calling SDL_SetMemoryFunctions simultaneously.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_GetNumAllocations
member.summary: Get the number of outstanding (unfreed) allocations
member.returns: Returns the number of allocations or -1 if allocation counting is disabled

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_GetOriginalMemoryFunctions(*,*,*,*)
member.summary: Get the original set of SDL memory functions
member.param: filled with malloc function
filled with calloc function
filled with realloc function
filled with free function
member.remarks: This is what SDL_malloc and friends will use by default, if there has been no call to SDL_SetMemoryFunctions. This is not necessarily using the C runtime's malloc functions behind the scenes! Different platforms and build configurations might do any number of unexpected things.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_SetMemoryFunctions(,,,)
member.summary: Replace SDL's memory allocation functions with a custom set
member.param: custom malloc function
custom calloc function
custom realloc function
custom free function
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information
member.remarks:
It is not safe to call this function once any allocations have been made, as future calls to SDL_free will use the new allocator, even if they came from an SDL_malloc made with the old one!
If used, usually this needs to be the first call made into the SDL library, if not the very first thing done at program startup time.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_aligned_alloc(System.UIntPtr,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Allocate memory aligned to a specific alignment
member.param: the alignment of the memory
the size to allocate
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the aligned memory, or NULL if allocation failed
member.remarks:
The memory returned by this function must be freed with SDL_aligned_free(), not SDL_free().
If is less than the size of void *, it will be increased to match that.
The returned memory address will be a multiple of the alignment value, and the size of the memory allocated will be a multiple of the alignment value.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_aligned_free(System.Void*)
member.summary: Free memory allocated by SDL_aligned_alloc()
member.param: a pointer previously returned by SDL_aligned_alloc(), or NULL
member.remarks:
The pointer is no longer valid after this call and cannot be dereferenced anymore.
If is NULL, this function does nothing.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_calloc(System.UIntPtr,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Allocate a zero-initialized array
member.param: the number of elements in the array
the size of each element of the array
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the allocated array, or NULL if allocation failed
member.remarks:
The memory returned by this function must be freed with SDL_free().
If either of or is 0, they will both be set to 1.
If the allocation is successful, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the fundamental alignment (alignof(max_align_t) in C11 and later) or 2 * sizeof(void *), whichever is smaller.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_free(System.Void*)
member.summary: Free allocated memory
member.param: a pointer to allocated memory, or NULL
member.remarks:
The pointer is no longer valid after this call and cannot be dereferenced anymore.
If is NULL, this function does nothing.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_malloc(System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Allocate uninitialized memory
member.param: the size to allocate
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the allocated memory, or NULL if allocation failed
member.remarks:
The allocated memory returned by this function must be freed with SDL_free().
If is 0, it will be set to 1.
If the allocation is successful, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the fundamental alignment (alignof(max_align_t) in C11 and later) or 2 * sizeof(void *), whichever is smaller. Use SDL_aligned_alloc() if you need to allocate memory aligned to an alignment greater than this guarantee.

The length, in bytes, to set in
member.returns: Returns

Despite being an int instead of a char, this only operates on bytes; must be a value between 0 and 255, inclusive.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_memset4(System.Void*,System.UInt32,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Initialize all 32-bit words of buffer of memory to a specific value

Unlike SDL_memset, this sets 32-bit values, not bytes, so it's not limited to a range of 0-255.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.SDL_realloc(System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Change the size of allocated memor
member.param: a pointer to allocated memory to reallocate, or NULL
the new size of the memory
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the newly allocated memory, or NULL if allocation failed
member.remarks:
The memory returned by this function must be freed with SDL_free().
If is 0, it will be set to 1. Note that this is unlike some other C runtime realloc implementations, which may treat realloc(, 0) the same way as free().
If is NULL, the behavior of this function is equivalent to SDL_malloc(). Otherwise, the function can have one of three possible outcomes:
If it returns the same pointer as , it means that was resized in place without freeing.
If it returns a different non-NULL pointer, it means that was freed and cannot be dereferenced anymore.
If it returns NULL (indicating failure), then will remain valid and must still be freed with SDL_free().
If the allocation is successfully resized, the returned pointer is guaranteed to be aligned to either the fundamental alignment (alignof(max_align_t) in C11 and later) or 2 * sizeof(void *), whichever is smaller.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.AllocationCount

member.value: The number of outstanding (unfreed) allocations, or -1 if allocation counting is disabled

The size in bytes to allocate
member.returns: A pointer to the allocated uninitialized aligned memory, or if the allocation failed

remarks.para: The memory returned by this method must be freed with , not .
If the is less than (*), it will be increased to match that instead.
The returned memory address will be a multiple of the , and the actual size of the memory allocated will be a multiple of the too.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.Realloc(System.Void*,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Changes the size of allocated memory
member.param: A pointer to allocated memory to reallocate, or
The new size in bytes of the memory
member.returns: A pointer to the newly allocated memory, or if the allocation failed

remarks.para: The memory returned by this method must be freed with .
If is 0, it will be set to 1 instead. Note that this is unlike some other C runtime realloc implementations, which may treat Realloc(, 0) the same way as Free().

If the pointer is , the behavior of this method is equivalent to Malloc(). Otherwise, the result of this method can have one of three possible outcomes:
If it returns the same pointer as , it means that the was resized in place without freeing
If it returns a different non- pointer, it means that the original was freed and cannot be dereferenced there anymore
If it returns (indicating failure), then the will remain valid and must still be freed with
The returned memory is guaranteed to be aligned to either the fundamental alignment (alignof(max_align_t) in C11 and later) or 2 * (*), whichever is smaller.

The value of the resulting 's property will be a multiple of the , and the actual size of the memory allocated (not necessarily identical to ) will be a multiple of the too.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.TryRealloc(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemoryManager@,System.UIntPtr)
member.summary: Tries to change the size of allocated native memory that is managed by a
member.param: A reference to that manages the native memory to reallocate, or a reference to . If this reference is referencing a value, it might be referencing an actual when this method returns .
The new size in bytes of the memory
member.returns: , if the memory could be successfull reallocated; otherwise,

remarks.para: The resulting should be still disposed when the memory it's managing is no longer needed. That also frees the allocated memory.
The referenced cannot be pinned. Trying to reallocated a pinned memory buffer results in failure.
If is 0, it will be set to 1 instead. Note that this is unlike some other C runtime realloc implementations, which may treat TryRealloc( , 0) the same way as .Dispose().

If the reference to is referencing a value, the behavior of this method is equivalent to TryMalloc(, ). Otherwise, a call to this method can have one of three possible outcomes:
If the result of the call to this method is and the values of 's , before and after this operation, are the same, it means that the original memory was resized in place without freeing
If the result of the call to this method is and the values of 's , before and after this operation, are different, it means that the original memory was freed and all active references to it (e.g. lasting s) are invalid and cannot be dereferenced anymore ( and instances are safe though)
The result of the call to this method is (indicating failure), then the given will remain valid (if it was non-) and should be still disposed when it's no longer needed
The value of the resulting 's property is guaranteed to be aligned to either the fundamental alignment (alignof(max_align_t) in C11 and later) or 2 * (*), whichever is smaller.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemory.TrySetMemoryFunctions(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.INativeMemoryFunctions)
member.summary: Tries to replace SDL's native memory functions with a custom set
member.param: The set of custom native memory functions to replace SDL's current one
member.returns: , if the set of SDL's current native memory functions was successfully replaced by the given custom one; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: It is not safe to call this method once any allocations have been made, as future calls to will use the new allocator, even if those allocations were allocated by an older set of native memory functions!
If this method is used, usually it needs to be the very first call made using the SDL library, if not the very first thing to do at the program's startup time.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemoryManagerBase.AddPin(System.UInt64,System.UInt64)
member.summary: Adds a "pin" to this
member.param: The pin counter before it was increased
The current pin counter after it was increased

remarks.para: This method is called each time the pin counter was successfully increased. You can override this method to implement custom logic that should be executed each time a "pin" is added to this .
If you want a more general pinning logic that only triggers when the first pin is added, you can check whether is 0 and greater than 0 in your custom implementation.
When implementing this method, remember that it must work in conjunction with .

There's no guarantee about when this method is called in relation to other threads pinning or unpinning this . Custom implementations must take care of thread-safetiness themselves if needed, especially in regards to the ordering of and operations.
The only guarantee given is that this method is only ever called immediately after the pin counter was successfully increased by a single pinning operation.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.NativeMemoryManagerBase.DecreasePinCounter(System.UInt64)
member.summary: Decreases the pin counter
member.param: The current pin counter
member.returns: The new pin counter

remarks.para: Override this method to implement custom pin counter decreasing step logic. You can even just ignore pinning altogether and always return the given .

Notice that custom implementations of this method should ensure that the returned value is less than or equal to the given . Also, when implementing this method, remember that the pin counter stepping must work in conjunction with . Furthermore, there's no underflow preventing logic in the consumers of this method, so custom implementations should ensure that underflow doesn't happen.
All of the and instances, that are subsequently depending on this , will become invalid of this operation.
Note: Trying to dipose a pinned will throw! Make sure that the is not pinned anymore before trying to dispose it.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform
member.summary: Provides methods and properties to identify the current executing platform as well as to interact with it on a lower level in a more platform-specific way.

remarks.para: Most application can make do without using these methods, but they can be useful for integrating with other parts of a specific system, adding platform-specific polish to an applicaiton, or solving problems that only affect one target.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.GetAndroidSDKVersion
member.summary: Gets the Android API level of the current device
member.returns: The Android API level of the current device

remarks.para:
This method returns the Android API level of the current device, not the Android version. The following is an incomplete mapping from Android API levels to Android versions:
API level 35 Android 15 (VANILLA_ICE_CREAM)
API level 34 Android 14 (UPSIDE_DOWN_CAKE)
API level 33 Android 13 (TIRAMISU)
API level 32 Android 12L (S_V2)
API level 31 Android 12 (S)
API level 30 Android 11 (R)
API level 29 Android 10 (Q)
API level 28 Android 9 (P)
API level 27 Android 8.1 (O_MR1)
API level 26 Android 8.0 (O)
API level 25 Android 7.1 (N_MR1)
API level 24 Android 7.0 (N)
API level 23 Android 6.0 (M)
API level 22 Android 5.1 (LOLLIPOP_MR1)
API level 21 Android 5.0 (LOLLIPOP, L)
API level 20 Android 4.4W (KITKAT_WATCH)
API level 19 Android 4.4 (KITKAT)
API level 18 Android 4.3 (JELLY_BEAN_MR2)
API level 17 Android 4.2 (JELLY_BEAN_MR1)
API level 16 Android 4.1 (JELLY_BEAN)
API level 15 Android 4.0.3 (ICE_CREAM_SANDWICH_MR1)
API level 14 Android 4.0 (ICE_CREAM_SANDWICH)
API level 13 Android 3.2 (HONEYCOMB_MR2)
API level 12 Android 3.1 (HONEYCOMB_MR1)
API level 11 Android 3.0 (HONEYCOMB)
API level 10 Android 2.3.3 (GINGERBREAD_MR1)
Note: This method is only available on Android and will throw a on all other platforms!

member.exception: The executing platform is not Android

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.OnApplicationDidChangeStatusBarOrientation
member.summary: Report onApplicationDidChangeStatusBarOrientation on Apple iOS when using external event handling

remarks.para: This method allows apps that have their own event handling to hook into SDL to generate SDL events. It maps directly to the iOS-specific event onApplicationDidChangeStatusBarOrientation.
Most apps do not need to use this method directly; SDL's internal event code will handle all this for windows created by !

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.TryRequestAndroidPermission(System.String,Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.AndroidRequestPermissionCallback)
member.summary: Tries to request an Android permission at runtime, asynchronously
member.param: The permission to request. Should be one of those values: .
A that will be invoked when the request has a response
member.returns: if the request was succesfully submitted; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: Note: The returned value of a call to this method just indicates if the request was succesfully submitted, not if the permission was granted. Only the given will receive a response the request.

You do not need to call this for built-in functionality of SDL. When using standard SDL APIs (e.g. recording from a microphone or reading images from a camera), SDL will manage permission requests for you.

This method never blocks. Instead, the given will be invoked when a decision has been made. That may get invoked on a different thread, and possibly much later, as it might wait on a user to respond to a system dialog. If permission has already been granted for a specific entitlement, the will still be invoked, probably on the current thread and before this method returns.
Note: This method is only available on Android and will throw a on all other platforms!

member.exception: The executing platform is not Android

member.summary: Retrieve the Java instance of the Android activity class

The jobject returned by the function is a local reference and must be released by the caller. See the PushLocalFrame() and PopLocalFrame() or DeleteLocalRef() functions of the Java native interface:

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_GetAndroidCachePath
member.summary: Get the path used for caching data for this Android application
member.returns: Returns the path used for caches for this application on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This path is unique to your application, but is public and can be written to by other applications.
Your cache path is typically: /data/data/your.app.package/cache/.
This is a C wrapper over android.content.Context.getCacheDir():

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_GetAndroidExternalStoragePath

remarks.para: This path is unique to your application, but is public and can be written to by other applications.
Your external storage path is typically: /storage/sdcard0/Android/data/your.app.package/files.
This is a C wrapper over android.content.Context.getExternalFilesDir():

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_GetAndroidExternalStorageState

remarks.para: The current state of external storage, a bitmask of these values: SDL_ANDROID_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_READ, SDL_ANDROID_EXTERNAL_STORAGE_WRITE.
If external storage is currently unavailable, this will return 0.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_GetAndroidInternalStoragePath

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_GetAndroidJNIEnv
member.summary: Get the Android Java Native Interface Environment of the current thread
member.returns: Returns a pointer to Java native interface object (JNIEnv) to which the current thread is attached, or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This is the JNIEnv one needs to access the Java virtual machine from native code, and is needed for many Android APIs to be usable from C.

The prototype of the function in SDL's code actually declare a void* return type, even if the implementation returns a pointer to a JNIEnv. The rationale being that the SDL headers can avoid including jni.h.

member.summary: Let iOS apps with external event handling report onApplicationDidChangeStatusBarOrientation

remarks.para: This functions allows iOS apps that have their own event handling to hook into SDL to generate SDL events. This maps directly to an iOS-specific event, but since it doesn't do anything iOS-specific internally, it is available on all platforms, in case it might be useful for some specific paradigm. Most apps do not need to use this directly; SDL's internal event code will handle all this for windows created by SDL_CreateWindow!

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Platform.SDL_RequestAndroidPermission(System.Byte*,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Request permissions at runtime, asynchronously
member.param: The permission to request
The callback to trigger when the request has a response
An app-controlled pointer that is passed to the callback
member.returns: Returns true if the request was submitted, false if there was an error submitting. The result of the request is only ever reported through the callback, not this return value.

remarks.para: You do not need to call this for built-in functionality of SDL; recording from a microphone or reading images from a camera, using standard SDL APIs, will manage permission requests for you.

This function never blocks. Instead, the app-supplied callback will be called when a decision has been made. This callback may happen on a different thread, and possibly much later, as it might wait on a user to respond to a system dialog. If permission has already been granted for a specific entitlement, the callback will still fire, probably on the current thread and before this function returns.

If the request submission fails, this function returns -1 and the callback will NOT be called, but this should only happen in catastrophic conditions, like memory running out. Normally there will be a yes or no to the request through the callback.
For the parameter, choose a value from here: ///

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.PowerStateExtensions.SDL_GetPowerInfo(System.Int32*,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Get the current power supply details
member.param: A pointer filled in with the seconds of battery life left, or NULL to ignore. This will be filled in with -1 if we can't determine a value or there is no battery
A pointer filled in with the percentage of battery life left, between 0 and 100, or NULL to ignore. This will be filled in with -1 we can't determine a value or there is no battery
member.returns: Returns the current battery state or SDL_POWERSTATE_ERROR on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: You should never take a battery status as absolute truth. Batteries (especially failing batteries) are delicate hardware, and the values reported here are best estimates based on what that hardware reports. It's not uncommon for older batteries to lose stored power much faster than it reports, or completely drain when reporting it has 20 percent left, etc.
Battery status can change at any time; if you are concerned with power state, you should call this function frequently, and perhaps ignore changes until they seem to be stable for a few seconds.
It's possible a platform can only report battery percentage or time left but not both.
On some platforms, retrieving power supply details might be expensive. If you want to display continuous status you could call this function every minute or so.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.PowerStateExtensions.<G>$735528E0CF14CD6FFB3804683BA73639.GetInfo(System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Gets the current power supply details
member.param: The time, in seconds, of battery life left, or -1 if the value couldn't be determined or there's no battery
The percentage of battery life left (in the range of 0 through 100), or -1 if the value couldn't be determined or there's no battery
member.returns: The current battery's including if the value couldn't be determined or when there's no battery, or on failure (check for more information)

remarks.para: You should never take a battery status as absolute truth. Batteries (especially failing batteries) are delicate hardware, and the values reported here are best estimates based on what that hardware reports. It's not uncommon for older batteries to lose stored power much faster than it reports, or completely drain when reporting it has 20 percent left, etc.
The battery's status can change at any time; if you are concerned with power state, you should call this method frequently, and perhaps ignore changes until they seem to be stable for a few seconds.
It's possible for some platforms to only report the battery life time left () or the battery percentage left () but not both.
On some platforms, retrieving power supply details might be expensive. If you want to display continuous status you could call this method every minute or so.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Process.SDL_CreateProcess(System.Byte**,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Create a new process
member.param: The path and arguments for the new process
true to create pipes to the process's standard input and from the process's standard output, false for the process to have no input and inherit the application's standard output
member.returns: Returns the newly created and running process, or NULL if the process couldn't be created

remarks.para: Setting pipe_stdio to true is equivalent to setting SDL_PROP_PROCESS_CREATE_STDIN_NUMBER and SDL_PROP_PROCESS_CREATE_STDOUT_NUMBER to SDL_PROCESS_STDIO_APP, and will allow the use of SDL_ReadProcess() or SDL_GetProcessInput() and SDL_GetProcessOutput().
See SDL_CreateProcessWithProperties() for more details.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random
member.summary: Provides methods to generate and seed pseudo-random numbers

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random.Next(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a pseudo-random integer number less than a specified positive integer
member.param: The number of possible outcomes. Should be positive.
member.returns: A pseudo-random value in the range from inclusive 0 to inclusive -1, if is greater than 0; otherwise 0

remarks.para: This method should be faster and of better quality than C's rand() % n. Odds are roughly 99.9% even for = 1 million. Evenness is better for smaller , and much worse as gets bigger.
If you want to generate a pseudo-random number in the full 32-bit integer range, use (see the examples given).
If you want reproducible output, be sure to initialize the pseudo-random number generator with a seed first ().

There are no guarantees as to the quality of the random sequence produced, and this should not be used for security (cryptography, passwords) or where money is on the line (loot-boxes, casinos). There are many alternatives with different characteristics available to meet more serious needs.
All calls to , , , or (the "global" APIs) should be made from the same single thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random.Next(System.UInt64@,System.Int32)
member.summary: Generates a pseudo-random integer number less than a specified positive integer
member.param: The current random number generator state
The number of possible outcomes. Should be positive.
member.returns: A pseudo-random value in the range from inclusive 0 to inclusive -1, if is greater than 0; otherwise 0

remarks.para: Instead of using a shared "global" state like , you can use a "local" here. This enables you to have multiple random number generates with different s at the same time.
This method should be faster and of better quality than C's rand() % n. Odds are roughly 99.9% even for = 1 million. Evenness is better for smaller , and much worse as gets bigger.
If you want to generate a pseudo-random number in the full 32-bit integer range, use (see the examples given).

There are no guarantees as to the quality of the random sequence produced, and this should not be used for security (cryptography, passwords) or where money is on the line (loot-boxes, casinos). There are many alternatives with different characteristics available to meet more serious needs.
This method is thread-safe, as long as the used is only shared in a thread-safe manner and no parallel concurrent calls to either , , or are made.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random.SDL_rand(System.Int32)
member.summary: Generate a pseudo-random number less than n for positive n
member.param: The number of possible outcomes. n must be positive.
member.returns: Returns a random value in the range of [0 .. n-1]

remarks.para: The method used is faster and of better quality than rand() % n. Odds are roughly 99.9% even for n = 1 million. Evenness is better for smaller n, and much worse as n gets bigger.
Example: to simulate a d6 use SDL_rand(6) + 1 The +1 converts 0..5 to 1..6
If you want to generate a pseudo-random number in the full range of Sint32, you should use: (Sint32)SDL_rand_bits()
If you want reproducible output, be sure to initialize with SDL_srand() first.

There are no guarantees as to the quality of the random sequence produced, and this should not be used for security (cryptography, passwords) or where money is on the line (loot-boxes, casinos). There are many random number libraries available with different characteristics and you should pick one of those to meet any serious needs.
All calls should be made from a single thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random.SDL_rand_bits
member.summary: Generate 32 pseudo-random bits.
member.returns: Returns a random value in the range of [0-SDL_MAX_UINT32]

remarks.para: You likely want to use SDL_rand() to get a pseudo-random number instead.

There are no guarantees as to the quality of the random sequence produced, and this should not be used for security (cryptography, passwords) or where money is on the line (loot-boxes, casinos). There are many random number libraries available with different characteristics and you should pick one of those to meet any serious needs.
All calls should be made from a single thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.Random.SDL_rand_bits_r(System.UInt64*)
member.summary: Generate 32 pseudo-random bits
member.param: A pointer to the current random number state, this may not be NULL
member.returns: Returns a random value in the range of [0-SDL_MAX_UINT32]

remarks.para: You likely want to use SDL_rand_r() to get a pseudo-random number instead.

There are no guarantees as to the quality of the random sequence produced, and this should not be used for security (cryptography, passwords) or where money is on the line (loot-boxes, casinos). There are many random number libraries available with different characteristics and you should pick one of those to meet any serious needs.
This function is thread-safe, as long as the state pointer isn't shared between threads.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject
member.summary: Represents a system-dependent code library (shared object) that is programmatically loadable at runtime

remarks.para: Windows calls these "DLLs", Linux calls them "shared libraries", etc.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Create and loads a new from a specified file
member.param: The system-dependent name of the shared object file. Could be a file path or some kind of special name.

remarks.para: Creating a new instance also loads the specified shared object file through system-specific manners.

In contrast to most of the remaining API which uses the Try-method pattern, this constructor intentionally fails by throwing an exception. If you want to handle failures wrap the call to this constructor in a -block, and check for more information when ing a .

member.exception: Couldn't load the specified shared object file (check for more information)

remarks.para: Note: after disposing a , all of the resulting symbol handles from calls to during its lifetime are now invalid! Do not try to dereference such a handle after disposing the respective !
Note: after disposing a , all of the resulting symbol handles from calls to during its lifetime are now invalid! Do not try to dereference such a handle after disposing the respective !

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject.TryLoadSymbol(System.String,System.IntPtr@)
member.summary: Tries to look up the handle of a named symbol in the
member.param: The name of the symbol to look up
The handle the symbol with the specified when this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: if a symbol with the specified was found and succesfully loaded; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method can only look up exported C symbol names. Other languages may have name mangling and intrinsic language support that varies from compiler to compiler.

If you want to call a resulting as a function, make sure you declare the function pointer signature with the same calling convention as the actual library function. Your code will crash mysteriously if you do not do this.
Note: Resulting handles are no longer valid after disposing the respective !

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject.SDL_LoadObject(System.Byte*)
member.summary: Dynamically load a shared object
member.param: A system-dependent name of the object file
member.returns: Returns an opaque pointer to the object handle or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject.SDL_LoadFunction(Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SharedObject.SDL_SharedObject*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Look up the address of the named function in a shared object
member.param: A valid shared object handle returned by SDL_LoadObject()
The name of the function to look up
member.returns: Returns a pointer to the function or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: This function pointer is no longer valid after calling SDL_UnloadObject().
This function can only look up C function names. Other languages may have name mangling and intrinsic language support that varies from compiler to compiler.
Make sure you declare your function pointers with the same calling convention as the actual library function. Your code will crash mysteriously if you do not do this.
If the requested function doesn't exist, NULL is returned.

member.summary: Unload a shared object from memory
member.param: A valid shared object handle returned by SDL_LoadObject()
member.remarks: Note that any pointers from this object looked up through SDL_LoadFunction() will no longer be valid.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo

remarks.para: These properties are largely concerned with indicating if the system has access to various SIMD instruction sets, but also has other important info to share, such as the system's RAM size or the number of logical CPU cores.

member.value: A value indicating whether the CPU has AltiVec features

remarks.para: The value of this property will always be on CPUs that aren't using PowerPC instruction sets.

member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the CPU has ARM SIMD (ARMv6) features

remarks.para: ARM SIMD (ARMv6) features are different from ARM NEON features, which are a different instruction set.
The value of this property will always be on CPUs that aren't using ARM instruction sets.

member.value: A value indicating whether the CPU has AVX-512F (foundation) features

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo.RamSize
member.summary: Gets the amount of RAM configured in the system in MiB
member.value: The amount of RAM configured in the system in MiB

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo.SimdAlignment
member.summary: Gets the alignment this system needs for available and known SIMD instructions in bytes
member.value: The alignment this system needs for available and known SIMD instructions in bytes

remarks.para: The value of this property is the minimum number of bytes to which a pointer must be aligned to be compatible with SIMD instructions on the current machine. For example, if the machine supports SSE only, it will be 16, but if it supports AVX-512F, it will be 64 (etc).

The values of this property are only for instruction sets SDL knows about, so if your SDL build doesn't have , then it might be 16 for the SSE support it sees and not 64 for the AVX-512 instructions that exist but SDL doesn't know about. Plan accordingly.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo.SDL_GetCPUCacheLineSize
member.summary: Determine the L1 cache line size of the CPU
member.returns: Returns the L1 cache line size of the CPU, in bytes

remarks.para: This is useful for determining multi-threaded structure padding or SIMD prefetch sizes.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo.SDL_GetNumLogicalCPUCores
member.summary: Get the number of logical CPU cores available
member.returns: Returns the total number of logical CPU cores. On CPUs that include technologies such as hyperthreading, the number of logical cores may be more than the number of physical cores.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.SystemInfo.SDL_GetSIMDAlignment
member.summary: Report the alignment this system needs for SIMD allocations
member.returns: Returns the alignment in bytes needed for available, known SIMD instructions

remarks.para: This will return the minimum number of bytes to which a pointer must be aligned to be compatible with SIMD instructions on the current machine. For example, if the machine supports SSE only, it will return 16, but if it supports AVX-512F, it'll return 64 (etc). This only reports values for instruction sets SDL knows about, so if your SDL build doesn't have SDL_HasAVX512F(), then it might return 16 for the SSE support it sees and not 64 for the AVX-512 instructions that exist but SDL doesn't know about. Plan accordingly.

member.returns: Returns the amount of RAM configured in the system in MiB

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.WindowsMessageHook
member.summary: Represents a method to handle messages directly from the Windows event loop
member.param: A pointer to a Win32 event structure to process
member.returns: to let the event continue on; otherwise, to drop it

remarks.para: This callback may modify the message, and should return if the message should continue to be processed, or to prevent further processing.
As this is processing a message directly from the Windows event loop, this callback should do the minimum required work and return quickly.
This may only be called (by SDL) from the thread handling the Windows event loop.

remarks.para: This callback may modify the event, and should return if the event should continue to be processed, or to prevent further processing.
As this is processing an event directly from the X11 event loop, this callback should do the minimum required work and return quickly.
This may only be called (by SDL) from the thread handling the X11 event loop.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Blending.BlendModeExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for
Compose a custom blend mode for renderers
member.param: The SDL_BlendFactor applied to the red, green, and blue components of the source pixels
The SDL_BlendFactor applied to the red, green, and blue components of the destination pixels
The SDL_BlendOperation used to combine the red, green, and blue components of the source and destination pixels
The SDL_BlendFactor applied to the alpha component of the source pixels
The SDL_BlendFactor applied to the alpha component of the destination pixels
The SDL_BlendOperation used to combine the alpha component of the source and destination pixels
member.returns: Returns an SDL_BlendMode that represents the chosen factors and operations

remarks.para: The functions SDL_SetRenderDrawBlendMode and SDL_SetTextureBlendMode accept the SDL_BlendMode returned by this function if the renderer supports it.

A blend mode controls how the pixels from a drawing operation (source) get combined with the pixels from the render target (destination). First, the components of the source and destination pixels get multiplied with their blend factors. Then, the blend operation takes the two products and calculates the result that will get stored in the render target.

Expressed in pseudocode, it would look like this:
dstRGB = colorOperation(srcRGB * srcColorFactor, dstRGB * dstColorFactor); dstA = alphaOperation(srcA* srcAlphaFactor, dstA* dstAlphaFactor);
Where the functions colorOperation(src, dst) and alphaOperation(src, dst) can return one of the following:
src + dst
src - dst
dst - src
min(src, dst)
max(src, dst)
The red, green, and blue components are always multiplied with the first, second, and third components of the SDL_BlendFactor, respectively. The fourth component is not used.
The alpha component is always multiplied with the fourth component of the SDL_BlendFactor. The other components are not used in the alpha calculation.

Support for these blend modes varies for each renderer. To check if a specific SDL_BlendMode is supported, create a renderer and pass it to either SDL_SetRenderDrawBlendMode or SDL_SetTextureBlendMode. They will return with an error if the blend mode is not supported.

This list describes the support of custom blend modes for each renderer. All renderers support the four blend modes listed in the SDL_BlendMode enumeration.
direct3d
Supports all operations with all factors. However, some factors produce unexpected results with SDL_BLENDOPERATION_MINIMUM and SDL_BLENDOPERATION_MAXIMUM.
direct3d11 Same as Direct3D 9.
opengl
Supports the SDL_BLENDOPERATION_ADD operation with all factors. OpenGL versions 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3 do not work correctly here.
opengles2
Supports the SDL_BLENDOPERATION_ADD, SDL_BLENDOPERATION_SUBTRACT, SDL_BLENDOPERATION_REV_SUBTRACT operations with all factors.
psp No custom blend mode support.
software No custom blend mode support.
Some renderers do not provide an alpha component for the default render target. The SDL_BLENDFACTOR_DST_ALPHA and SDL_BLENDFACTOR_ONE_MINUS_DST_ALPHA factors do not have an effect in this case.

member.summary: Composes a custom for renderers
member.param: The applied to the red, green, and blue components of the source pixels
The applied to the red, green, and blue components of the destination pixels
The used to combine the red, green, and blue components of the source and destination pixels
The applied to the alpha component of the source pixels
The applied to the alpha component of the destination pixels
The used to combine the alpha component of the source and destination pixels
member.returns: A custom that represents the chosen factors and operations

remarks.para: A custom controls how the pixels from a drawing operation (source) get combined with the pixels from the render target (destination). First, the components of the source and destination pixels get multiplied with their blend factors. Then, the blend operation takes the two products and calculates the result that will get stored in the render target.

Expressed in pseudocode, it would look like this:
dstRGB = colorOperation(srcRGB * srcColorFactor, dstRGB * dstColorFactor); dstA = alphaOperation(srcA * srcAlphaFactor, dstA * dstAlphaFactor);
Where the functions colorOperation(src, dst) and alphaOperation(src, dst) can return one of the following:
src + dst ()
src - dst ()
dst - src ()
min(src, dst) ()
max(src, dst) ()
The red, green, and blue components are always multiplied with the respective factors of the . The alpha factor is not used.
The alpha component is always multiplied with the alpha factor of the . The other factors are not used in the alpha calculation.

Support for these custom blend modes varies for each renderer. To check if a specific is supported, create a , use it's property, and then - for a . If there was an exception, check if it's about an unsupported blend mode. Alternatively, you can do the same procedure with a and it's property.

The following list describes the support of custom blend modes for each rendering driver. All rendering drivers support the blend modes defined in the enumeration.
Supports all operations with all factors. However, some factors produce unexpected results with the and operations.
Same as Direct3D 9.
Supports the operation with all factors. OpenGL versions 1.1, 1.2, and 1.3 do not work correctly here.
Supports the , , and operations with all factors.
No custom blend mode support.
No custom blend mode support.
Some renderers do not provide an alpha component for the default render target. The and factors do not have an effect in this case.
The properties and accept the s returned by this method if the renderer supports it.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ChromaLocation
member.summary: Represents the chroma sample location used in s

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ChromaLocation.None
member.summary: No chroma sampling

remarks.para: This is most likely used for RGB s

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ChromaLocation.Left
member.summary: Chroma sampling at the group's left pixel column, vertically center

remarks.para: In MPEG-2, MPEG-4, and AVC, Cb and Cr are taken on midpoint of the left-edge of the 2x2 square. In other words, they have the same horizontal location as the top-left pixel, but is shifted one-half pixel down vertically.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ChromaLocation.Center
member.summary: Chroma sampling at the group's center

remarks.para: In JPEG/JFIF, H.261, and MPEG-1, Cb and Cr are taken at the center of the 2x2 square. In other words, they are offset one-half pixel to the right and one-half pixel down compared to the top-left pixel.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ChromaLocation.TopLeft
member.summary: Chroma sampling at the group's top-left pixel

remarks.para: In HEVC for BT.2020 and BT.2100 content (in particular on Blu-rays), Cb and Cr are sampled at the same location as the group's top-left Y pixel ("co-sited", "co-located").

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Color`1
member.summary: Represents a color with red, green, blue and alpha components
member.typeparam: The type of the color components
member.param: The value of the red component
The value of the green component
The value of the blue component
The value of the alpha component

remarks.para: Most often the type is either for 32-bit pixel values with unsigned integer ranges from 0 to 255 for each component, or for 128-bit pixel values with floating-point ranges from 0 to 1 for each component.
Color<> values can be directly reinterpreted as an integer-packed color which uses the Rgba32 format ( on little-endian systems and on big-endian systems).
Color<> values can be directly reinterpreted as a float-packed color which uses the format.
