member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ColorSpace.Srgb
member.summary: sRGB is a gamma corrected color space, and the default color space for SDL rendering and 8-bit RGB surface

remarks.para: This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_RGB_FULL_G22_NONE_P709 (see ).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ColorSpace.SrgbLinear
member.summary: sRGB linear is a linear colorspace and the default color space for floating point surfaces. On Windows this is the scRGB color space, and on Apple platforms this is kCGColorSpaceExtendedLinearSRGB for EDR content.

remarks.para: This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_RGB_FULL_G10_NONE_P709 (see ).

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ColorSpace.Hdr10
member.summary: HDR10 is a non-linear HDR colorspace and the default colorspace for 10-bit surfaces

remarks.para: This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_RGB_FULL_G2084_NONE_P2020 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_FULL_G22_NONE_P709_X601 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_STUDIO_G22_LEFT_P601 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_STUDIO_G22_LEFT_P601 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_STUDIO_G22_LEFT_P709 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_STUDIO_G22_LEFT_P709 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_STUDIO_G22_LEFT_P2020 (see ).
This is equivalent to the DirectX color space DXGI_COLOR_SPACE_YCBCR_FULL_G22_LEFT_P2020 (see ).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.GetColor(System.UInt32,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette,System.Byte@,System.Byte@,System.Byte@)
member.summary: Gets the RGB component values from an encoded pixel value
member.param: An an encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this
An optional to use for indexed pixel formats
The red component value of the encoded pixel value
The green component value of the encoded pixel value
The blue component value of the encoded pixel value

remarks.para: This method uses the entire 8-bit (0-255) range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8-bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit format would return (, , ) = (0xff, 0xff, 0xff), not (0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8)).

member.param: An an encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this
An optional to use for indexed pixel formats
The red component value of the encoded pixel value
The green component value of the encoded pixel value
The blue component value of the encoded pixel value
The alpha component value of the encoded pixel value

remarks.para: This method uses the entire 8-bit (0-255) range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8-bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit format would return (, , ) = (0xff, 0xff, 0xff), not (0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8)).
If the surface has no alpha component, the lpha component value will be returned as 0xff (fully opaque).

member.param: An an encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this
An optional to use for indexed pixel formats
The Color<> of the encoded pixel value

remarks.para: This method uses the entire 8-bit (0-255) range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8-bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit format would return (, , ) = (0xff, 0xff, 0xff), not (0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8)).
If the surface has no alpha component, the value will be returned as 0xff (fully opaque).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.MapColor(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Maps RGB component values to an opaque encoded pixel value
member.param: An optional to use for indexed pixel formats
The red component value to encode
The green component value to encode
The blue component value to encode
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this

remarks.para: This method maps the RGB color value to the specified pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given RGB color value for the pixel format.
If the pixel format is indexed and uses a the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has an alpha component, the encoded pixel value's alpha component value will be set to be fully opaque.

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.MapColor(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)

member.param: An optional to use for indexed pixel formats
The red component value to encode

The blue component value to encode
The alpha component value to encode
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this

remarks.para: This method maps the RGBA color value to the specified pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given RGBA color value for the pixel format.
If the pixel format is indexed and uses a the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has no alpha component, the given alpha component value will be ignored (as it will be in indexed formats with a ).

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

The Color<> to encoded
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of this

remarks.para: This method maps the Color<> to the specified pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given Color<> for the pixel format.
If the pixel format is indexed and uses a the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has no alpha component, the given alpha component value will be ignored (as it will be in indexed formats with a ).

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_GetRGB(System.UInt32,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_PixelFormatDetails*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get RGB values from a pixel in the specified format
member.param: A pixel value
A pointer to SDL_PixelFormatDetails describing the pixel format
An optional palette for indexed formats, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the red component, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the green component, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the blue component, may be NULL

remarks.para: This function uses the entire 8-bit [0..255] range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8-bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit RGB565 format would return [0xff, 0xff, 0xff] not [0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8]).
It is safe to call this function from any thread, as long as the palette is not modified.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_GetRGBA(System.UInt32,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_PixelFormatDetails*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Get RGBA values from a pixel in the specified format
member.param: A pixel value
A pointer to SDL_PixelFormatDetails describing the pixel format
An optional palette for indexed formats, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the red component, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the green component, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the blue component, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the alpha component, may be NULL

remarks.para: This function uses the entire 8-bit [0..255] range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8-bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit RGB565 format would return [0xff, 0xff, 0xff] not [0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8]).
If the surface has no alpha component, the alpha will be returned as 0xff (100% opaque).
It is safe to call this function from any thread, as long as the palette is not modified.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_MapRGB(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_PixelFormatDetails*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Map an RGB triple to an opaque pixel value for a given pixel format
member.param: A pointer to SDL_PixelFormatDetails describing the pixel format
An optional palette for indexed formats, may be NULL
The red component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The green component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255
member.returns: Returns a pixel value

remarks.para: This function maps the RGB color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGB color value for the given pixel format.
If the format has a palette (8-bit) the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the specified pixel format has an alpha component it will be returned as all 1 bits (fully opaque).

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).
It is safe to call this function from any thread, as long as the palette is not modified.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_MapRGBA(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormatDetails.SDL_PixelFormatDetails*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Map an RGBA quadruple to a pixel value for a given pixel format
member.param: A pointer to SDL_PixelFormatDetails describing the pixel format
An optional palette for indexed formats, may be NULL
The red component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The green component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The alpha component of the pixel in the range 0-255
member.returns: Returns a pixel value

remarks.para: This function maps the RGBA color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGBA color value for the given pixel format.
If the specified pixel format has no alpha component the alpha value will be ignored (as it will be in formats with a palette).
If the format has a palette (8-bit) the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).
It is safe to call this function from any thread, as long as the palette is not modified.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect.TryGetLineIntersection(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Determines the intersecting segment of a line and a rectangle, if any
member.param: The rectangle to intersect with

The x-coordinate of the start point of the line. If this method returns , this value will be updated to the x-coordinate of the nearer intersection point or remain unchanged if the start point is contained within the rectangle.

The y-coordinate of the start point of the line. If this method returns , this value will be updated to the y-coordinate of the nearer intersection point or remain unchanged if the start point is contained within the rectangle.

The x-coordinate of the end point of the line. If this method returns , this value will be updated to the x-coordinate of the farther intersection point or remain unchanged if the end point is contained within the rectangle.

The y-coordinate of the end point of the line. If this method returns , this value will be updated to the y-coordinate of the farther intersection point or remain unchanged if the end point is contained within the rectangle.
member.returns: , if the line intersects with the rectangle; otherwise,

remarks.para: This method is used to clip a line segment to a rectangle. A line segment contained entirely within the rectangle or that does not intersect will remain unchanged. A line segment that crosses the rectangle at either or both ends will be clipped to the boundary of the rectangle and the new coordinates stored in , , , and/or as necessary.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.IRenderingDriver
member.summary: Represents a rendering driver used to specify the rendering backend in and

remarks.para:
There are some pre-defined rendering drivers that SDL comes with. Not all of them are necessarily available in every environment. You can check the property to see which rendering drivers are available in the current environment. The rendering drivers that SDL comes with are:
Direct3D 11 backend (only available on Windows)
Direct3D 12 backend (only available on Windows)
Direct3D 9 backend (only available on Windows)
Metal backend (only available on macOS and iOS)
Nokia N-Gage backend (not supported; experimental only)
OpenGL backend
OpenGL for Embedded Systems 2 backend (primarily only available on mobile and embedded platforms, but may be available on some desktop platforms as well)
Sony PlayStation 2 backend (not supported; experimental only)
Sony PlayStation Portable backend (not supported; experimental only)
Sony PlayStation Vita backend (not supported; experimental only)
Vulkan backend
GPU backend (only available on platforms that support the SDL GPU render API, which are primarly platforms that support either , , or as well)
Software rendering backend (supported almost everywhere; not hardware-accelerated; needed when rendering should be done to a directly instead of to a or to an off-screen render target)
Please note that some of these rendering drivers require platforms that .NET doesn't run on (yet). These drivers are currently unsupported and marked as experimental (e.g. , , , and ).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.IRenderingDriver.AvailableDriverNames
member.summary: Gets the list of names of available rendering drivers in the current environment
member.value: The list of names of available rendering drivers in the current environment

remarks.para: The list of available rendering drivers is only retrieved once and then cached afterwards, so the value of this property won't change during the lifetime of the application.

The list is in the order that rendering drivers are normally initialized by default; the drivers that seem more reasonable to choose first (as far as the SDL developers believe) are earlier in the list.

The names of all pre-defined rendering drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "opengl", "direct3d12" or "metal". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.
You can use the value of the properties of individual rendering drivers types (e.g. ) to check for the availability of a certain rendering driver; alternatively you can check the property for that.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.IRenderingDriver.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the rendering driver
member.value: The name of the rendering driver, or if the driver doesn't have a name (although all pre-defined rendering available to the user do have a non- name)

remarks.para: The names of all pre-defined rendering drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "opengl", "direct3d12" or "metal". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.IRenderingDriver.SDL_GetNumRenderDrivers
member.summary: Get the number of 2D rendering drivers available for the current display
member.returns: Returns the number of built in render drivers

remarks.para: A render driver is a set of code that handles rendering and texture management on a particular display. Normally there is only one, but some drivers may have several available with different capabilities.
There may be none if SDL was compiled without render support.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.IRenderingDriver.SDL_GetRenderDriver(System.Int32)
member.summary: Use this function to get the name of a built in 2D rendering driver
member.param: The index of the rendering driver; the value ranges from 0 to SDL_GetNumRenderDrivers() - 1
member.returns: Returns the name of the rendering driver at the requested index, or NULL if an invalid index was specified

remarks.para: The list of rendering drivers is given in the order that they are normally initialized by default; the drivers that seem more reasonable to choose first (as far as the SDL developers believe) are earlier in the list.
The names of drivers are all simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "opengl", "direct3d12" or "metal". These never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.GpuRenderStateCreateInfo.TryPrepare
member.summary: Tries to prepare this and caches its internal representation
member.returns: , if this is ready to be used for creating a ; otherwise,

remarks.para: This method prepares this 's internal representation, caching it for future use. That means calling this method multiple times without modifying any of the properties is essentially a no-op after the first call.

Calling this method is not strictly necessary, as the method would prepare the itself, if necessary. But it could be useful to call this method in advance to have a more deterministic point where the preparation happens, and to move away the heavy lifting from the constructor call.

This method will return if any of the required properties is not set to a valid value (e.g. is ), or if it fails to allocate or reallocate the native memory needed for the sampler bindings, storage textures, or storage buffers.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer
member.summary: Represents a rendering context (renderer)

remarks.para: This is used to perform driver-specific 2D rendering operations, most commonly to a or an off-screen render target.
You can create new renderers for a using the or instance methods on a instance.

If you create textures using a , please remember to dispose them before disposing the renderer. Do not dispose the associated before disposing the either! Using an after its associated has been disposed can cause undefined behavior, including crashes.
For the most part s are not thread-safe, and most of their properties and methods should only be accessed from the main thread!

s are not driver-agnostic! Most of the time instance of this abstract class are of the concrete type with a specific rendering driver as the type argument. However, the type exists as an abstraction to use common rendering operations.
To specify a concrete renderer type, use with a rendering driver that implements the interface (e.g. Renderer<OpenGL>).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.ClippingRect
member.summary: Gets or sets the clipping rectangle for the current target
member.value: The clipping rectangle for the current target

remarks.para: If the value of this property is equal to the target's bounds, clipping is effectively disabled.
Alternatively, you can use the property to check whether clipping is enabled or not, and the method to reset the clipping rectangle.
Each render target, or (or in the case of a software renderer), has its own clipping rectangle. This property reflects the clipping rectangle of the current target.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.ColorScale
member.summary: Gets or sets the color scale for render operations
member.value: The color scale for render operations

remarks.para: The color scale is an additional scale multiplied into the pixel color value while rendering. This can be used to adjust the brightness of colors during HDR rendering, or changing HDR video brightness when playing on an SDR display.
The color scale does not affect the alpha channel, only the color brightness.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.CurrentOutputSize
member.summary: Gets the current output size for the renderer
member.value: The current output size for the renderer, in pixels

remarks.para: If a rendering target is active, the value of this property will be the size of that target, otherwise it will be equal to the value of the property.
Either way, the resulting size will be adjusted by the current logical presentation state, dictated by the property.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.value: The default scale mode for new textures created by the renderer

remarks.para: The value of this property defaults to for newly created renderers.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.DrawColor
member.summary: Gets or sets the color used for drawing operations
member.value: The color used for drawing operations

remarks.para: Drawing operations that make use of the blend mode defined by this property include, but are not limited to: , , , , , , , , , , and .
In addition to that, also defines the color used for clearing the current render target when using the method.
The specifies how the alpha component of this property is used in drawing operations.
The component values of this property are equivalent to the component values of the property, multiplied by 255 and rounded towards zero.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.IsViewportSet
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether an explicit rectangle was set as the current for the renderer
member.value: A value indicating whether an explicit rectangle was set as the current for the renderer

remarks.para: This is useful if you're saving and restoring the and want to know whether you should restore a specific rectangle or not.
Each render target, or (or in the case of a software renderer), has its own viewport. This property reflects the viewport of the current target.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.LogicalPresentation
member.summary: Gets or sets the device-independent resolution and presentation mode for rendering
member.value: The device-independent resolution and presentation mode for rendering

remarks.para: The value of this property specifies the width and height of the logical rendering output. The renderer will act as if the current target is always the specified size, scaling to the actual resolution as necessary.

This can be useful for games and applications that expect a fixed size, but would like to scale the output to whatever is available, regardless of how a user resizes a window, or if the display is high DPI.

Logical presentation is disabled if the mode is set to , in which case the logical presentation size will be equal to the output size of the current target. It is safe to toggle the logical presentation mode during the rendering of a frame. E.g. you can do most of the rendering to a specified logical resolution, but to make text look sharper, you can temporarily disable logical presentation when rendering text.

It might be useful to draw to a texture that matches the window dimensions with logical presentation enabled, and then draw that texture across the entire window with logical presentation disabled. Be careful not to render both with logical presentation enabled, however, as this could produce double-letterboxing, etc.
Coordinates coming from an event can be converted to rendering coordinates using the method.
Each render target, or (or in the case of a software renderer), has its own logical presentation size and mode. This property reflects the logical presentation size and mode of the current target.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information). A common reason for this to happen is when the associated with this renderer was already disposed, but the renderer itself wasn't disposed yet.

member.value: The maximum texture size supported by the renderer, in pixels

remarks.para: The value of this property is in regards to both width and height of the texture. E.g. if the value is 4096, then the renderer supports textures up to 4096⨯4096 in size.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.Name
member.summary: Gets the name of the rendering driver used by the renderer
member.value: The name of the rendering driver used by the renderer, or if the name is not available

remarks.para: The value of this property can be compared to the name of any pre-defined rendering driver implementing the interface to determine whether the renderer is using that driver.
Names of rendering drivers should all be simple, low-ASCII identifiers, like "opengl", "direct3d12" or "metal". These should never have Unicode characters, and are not meant to be proper names.
You can see for a list of the names of all available rendering drivers in the current environment.

member.summary: Gets the safe area for rendering within the current viewport
member.value: The safe area for rendering within the current viewport, in pixels

remarks.para: Some devices have portions of the screen which are partially obscured or not interactive, possibly due to on-screen controls, curved edges, camera notches, TV overscan, etc. This property provides the area of the current viewport which is safe to have interactible content. You should continue rendering into the rest of the render target, but it should not contain visually important or interactible content.

member.summary: Gets or sets the drawing scales for rendering on the current target
member.value: The drawing scales for rendering on the current target

remarks.para: The drawing coordinates are scaled by the values of this property before they are used by the renderer. This allows for resolution-independent drawing with a single coordinate system.
If this results in scaling or subpixel drawing by the rendering backend, it will be handled using the appropriate quality hints. For best results use integer scaling factors.
Each render target, or (or in the case of a software renderer), has its own drawing scales. This property reflects the drawing scales of the current target.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SupportedTextureFormats
member.summary: Gets the texture formats supported by the renderer
member.value: The texture formats supported by the renderer

remarks.para: For performance reasons, the resulting collection is enumerated lazily, so the actual enumeration of the supported texture formats is deferred until you start enumerating over the collection.

Because of that, the fact that you can enumerate the result just a single time, and the fact that the enumeration itself is quite an expensive operation, you should consider caching the result into a persistent collection.
Note that the enumerator returned by this property can an if the reference to the texture formats is invalid or if it changes during the enumeration.

You can reset the current render target to the default render target by either setting this property to , or by using the method.
Only textures created with this renderer can be used as render targets, and only if they were created with the access mode.

member.summary: Gets or sets the vertical synchronization (VSync) mode or interval for the renderer
member.value: The vertical synchronization (VSync) mode or interval for the renderer

remarks.para: You can set the value of this property to to disable VSync, to enable late swap tearing (adaptive VSync) if supported, or use the method to specify a custom VSync interval. You can specify a custom interval of 1 to synchronize to present of the renderer with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize it with every second vertical refresh, and so on.
When a renderer is newly created, the value of this property defaults to .
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.TryFlush
member.summary: Tries to flush any pending rendering operations on the current target
member.returns: , if the operation was successful; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: You do not need to (and in fact, shouldn't) call this method unless you are planning to call into OpenGL/Direct3D/Metal/whatever directly, in addition to using a .

This method exists for a very-specific case: if you are using SDL's render API, and you plan to make OpenGL/D3D/whatever calls in addition to SDL render API calls. If this applies, you should call this method between calls to SDL's render API and the low-level API you're using in cooperation.

para.em: In all other cases, you can ignore this method!

remarks.para: A call to this method makes SDL flush any pending rendering work it was queueing up to do later in a single batch, and marks any internal cached state as invalid, so it'll prepare all its state again later, from scratch.

This means you do not need to save state in your rendering code to protect the SDL renderer. However, there lots of arbitrary pieces of Direct3D and OpenGL state that can confuse things; you should use your best judgment and be prepared to make changes if specific state needs to be protected.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.returns: , if the pixels were successfully read into a new ; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The resulting contains a copy of the pixels in the area specified by the given clipped to the current .
Note that this method copies the actual pixels on the screen. So if you are using any form of logical presentation, you should use to get the area containing your content.
WARNING: This is a very slow operation, and should not be used frequently. If you're using this on the main rendering target, it should be called after rendering and before .
Please remember to dispose of the resulting when you're done using it.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.TryRenderDebugText(System.Single,System.Single,System.String)
member.summary: Tries to draw debug text to the current target
member.param: The top-left X coordinate where the text should be drawn
The top-left Y coordinate where the text should be drawn
The text to be drawn
member.returns: , if the text was drawn successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method will render text to a . Note that this is a convenience method for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production applications and games.

Among these limitations are:
It accepts Unicode strings, but will only render ASCII characters
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use or to adjust for that.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It doesn't do word-wrapping and doesn't treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the target, it's gone.
For more serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is disposed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by (or ) and determine how the text is blended with the existing content of the target.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.TryRenderDebugText(System.Single,System.Single,System.String,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Object})
member.summary: Tries to draw a debug format string to the current target
member.param: The top-left X coordinate where the text should be drawn
The top-left Y coordinate where the text should be drawn
The C-style printf format string
The arguments corresponding to the format specifiers in
member.returns: , if the text was drawn successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method will render text to a . Note that this is a convenience method for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production applications and games.

Among these limitations are:
It accepts Unicode strings, but will only render ASCII characters
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use or to adjust for that.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It doesn't do word-wrapping and doesn't treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the target, it's gone.
For more serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is disposed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by (or ) and determine how the text is blended with the existing content of the target.

The parameter is interpreted as a C-style printf format string, and the parameter supplies the values for its format specifiers. Supported argument types include all integral types up to 64-bit (including and ), floating point types ( and ), pointer types ( and ), and .
For a detailed explanation of C-style printf format strings and their specifiers, see .
Consider using instead when possible, as it may be more efficient. In many cases, you can use C# string interpolation to construct the message before logging.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.TryRenderPresent
member.summary: Tries to update the screen with any rendering performed since the previous call
member.returns: if the screen was updated successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: SDL's rendering API operates on a backbuffer. E.g., performing a rendering operation such as does not directly draw a line on the screen, but rather updates the backbuffer. That means that you should compose the entire scene in the backbuffer and then present the composed backbuffer to the screen as a complete picture.
When using SDL's rendering API, once per frame, do all drawing intended for the frame, and then call this method once to present the final drawing to the user.

The backbuffer should be considered invalidated after each call to this method; do not assume that previous contents will exist between frames. It is strongly recommended to initialize the backbuffer before starting each new frame's drawing by calling . Even if you plan to overwrite every pixel.

Please note, that in case of rendering to a texture target ( is non-), there is no need to call and in fact, it shouldn't be called. You are only required to change back the rendering target to default via or setting to afterwards, as textures by themselves do not have a concept of backbuffers. Calling while rendering to a texture will fail.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

The height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in

remarks.para: The pixels in the texture are split into a 3⨯3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of the . The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining portion of the .

member.summary: Tries to scalingly copy a portion of a texture to the entirety of the current target using the 9-grid algorithm with tiling for the borders and the center
member.param: The texture to copy
The area of the texture to copy, in a 9-grid manner
The width, in pixels, of the left corners in
The width, in pixels, of the right corners in
The height, in pixels, of the top corners in
The height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in
The scale to use to transform the corners of into the corners of the current target, or 0 for an unscaled copy
The scale to use to transform the borders and the center of into the border and the center of the current target, or 1 for an unscaled copy
member.returns: , if the texture was rendered successfully; otherwise, false (check for more information)

remarks.para: The pixels in the texture are split into a 3⨯3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of the current target. The sides and center are then tiled into place to cover the remaining portion of the current target.
Only textures created with this renderer can be rendered with this method.
Rendering the texture works with sub-pixel precision.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_CreateGPURenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Gpu.GpuDevice.SDL_GPUDevice*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Create a 2D GPU rendering context
member.param: The GPU device to use with the renderer, or NULL to create a device
The window where rendering is displayed, or NULL to create an offscreen renderer
member.returns: Returns a valid rendering context or NULL if there was an error; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The GPU device to use is passed in as a parameter. If this is NULL, then a device will be created normally and can be retrieved using SDL_GetGPURendererDevice().

The window to use is passed in as a parameter. If this is NULL, then this will become an offscreen renderer. In that case, you should call SDL_SetRenderTarget() to setup rendering to a texture, and then call SDL_RenderPresent() normally to complete drawing a frame.

If this function is called with a valid GPU device, it should be called on the thread that created the device. If this function is called with a valid window, it should be called on the thread that created the window.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_CreateRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Create a 2D rendering context for a window
member.param: The window where rendering is displayed
The name of the rendering driver to initialize, or NULL to let SDL choose one
member.returns: Returns a valid rendering context or NULL if there was an error; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If you want a specific renderer, you can specify its name here. A list of available renderers can be obtained by calling SDL_GetRenderDriver() multiple times, with indices from 0 to SDL_GetNumRenderDrivers()-1. If you don't need a specific renderer, specify NULL and SDL will attempt to choose the best option for you, based on what is available on the user's system.
If is a comma-separated list, SDL will try each name, in the order listed, until one succeeds or all of them fail.
By default the rendering size matches the window size in pixels, but you can call SDL_SetRenderLogicalPresentation() to change the content size and scaling options.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_CreateRendererWithProperties(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Create a 2D rendering context for a window, with the specified properties
member.param: The properties to use
member.returns: Returns a valid rendering context or NULL if there was an error; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
These are the supported properties:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_NAME_STRING The name of the rendering driver to use, if a specific one is desired
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_WINDOW_POINTER The window where rendering is displayed, required if this isn't a software renderer using a surface
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_OUTPUT_COLORSPACE_NUMBER
An SDL_Colorspace value describing the colorspace for output to the display, defaults to SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB. The direct3d11, direct3d12, and metal renderers support SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR, which is a linear color space and supports HDR output. If you select SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR, drawing still uses the sRGB colorspace, but values can go beyond 1.0 and float (linear) format textures can be used for HDR content.
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_PRESENT_VSYNC_NUMBER
Non-zero if you want present synchronized with the refresh rate. This property can take any value that is supported by SDL_SetRenderVSync() for the renderer.
With the SDL GPU renderer (since SDL 3.4.0):
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_GPU_DEVICE_POINTER The device to use with the renderer, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_GPU_SHADERS_SPIRV_BOOLEAN The app is able to provide SPIR-V shaders to SDL_GPURenderState, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_GPU_SHADERS_DXIL_BOOLEAN The app is able to provide DXIL shaders to SDL_GPURenderState, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_GPU_SHADERS_MSL_BOOLEAN The app is able to provide MSL shaders to SDL_GPURenderState, optional
With the vulkan renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_INSTANCE_POINTER The VkInstance to use with the renderer, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_SURFACE_NUMBER The VkSurfaceKHR to use with the renderer, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_POINTER The VkPhysicalDevice to use with the renderer, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_DEVICE_POINTER The VkDevice to use with the renderer, optional
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_GRAPHICS_QUEUE_FAMILY_INDEX_NUMBER The queue family index used for rendering
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_CREATE_VULKAN_PRESENT_QUEUE_FAMILY_INDEX_NUMBER The queue family index used for presentation
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_CreateSoftwareRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Create a 2D software rendering context for a surface
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure representing the surface where rendering is done
member.returns: Returns a valid rendering context or NULL if there was an error; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Two other API which can be used to create SDL_Renderer: SDL_CreateRenderer() and SDL_CreateWindowAndRenderer(). These can also create a software renderer, but they are intended to be used with an SDL_Window as the final destination and not an SDL_Surface.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_DestroyRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*)
member.summary: Destroy the rendering context for a window and free all associated textures
member.param: The rendering context

remarks.para: This should be called before destroying the associated window.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_FlushRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*)
member.summary: Force the rendering context to flush any pending commands and state
member.param: The rendering context
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: You do not need to (and in fact, shouldn't) call this function unless you are planning to call into OpenGL/Direct3D/Metal/whatever directly, in addition to using an SDL_Renderer.

This is for a very-specific case: if you are using SDL's render API, and you plan to make OpenGL/D3D/whatever calls in addition to SDL render API calls. If this applies, you should call this function between calls to SDL's render API and the low-level API you're using in cooperation.
In all other cases, you can ignore this function.

This call makes SDL flush any pending rendering work it was queueing up to do later in a single batch, and marks any internal cached state as invalid, so it'll prepare all its state again later, from scratch.

This means you do not need to save state in your rendering code to protect the SDL renderer. However, there lots of arbitrary pieces of Direct3D and OpenGL state that can confuse things; you should use your best judgment and be prepared to make changes if specific state needs to be protected.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_GetCurrentRenderOutputSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.Int32*,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Get the current output size in pixels of a rendering context
member.param: The rendering context
A pointer filled in with the current width
A pointer filled in with the current height
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If a rendering target is active, this will return the size of the rendering target in pixels, otherwise return the value of SDL_GetRenderOutputSize().
Rendering target or not, the output will be adjusted by the current logical presentation state, dictated by SDL_SetRenderLogicalPresentation().
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_GetRendererProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*)
member.summary: Get the properties associated with a renderer
member.param: Tthe rendering context
member.returns: Returns a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_NAME_STRING The name of the rendering driver
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_WINDOW_POINTER The window where rendering is displayed, if any
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_SURFACE_POINTER The surface where rendering is displayed, if this is a software renderer without a window
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VSYNC_NUMBER The current vsync setting
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_MAX_TEXTURE_SIZE_NUMBER The maximum texture width and height
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_TEXTURE_FORMATS_POINTER
A (const SDL_PixelFormat *) array of pixel formats, terminated with SDL_PIXELFORMAT_UNKNOWN, representing the available texture formats for this renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_TEXTURE_WRAPPING_BOOLEAN True if the renderer supports SDL_TEXTURE_ADDRESS_WRAP on non-power-of-two textures
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_OUTPUT_COLORSPACE_NUMBER An SDL_Colorspace value describing the colorspace for output to the display, defaults to SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_HDR_ENABLED_BOOLEAN
True if the output colorspace is SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR and the renderer is showing on a display with HDR enabled. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT
The value of SDR white in the SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR colorspace. When HDR is enabled, this value is automatically multiplied into the color scale. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT
The additional high dynamic range that can be displayed, in terms of the SDR white point. When HDR is not enabled, this will be 1.0. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
With the direct3d renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D9_DEVICE_POINTER The IDirect3DDevice9 associated with the renderer
With the direct3d11 renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D11_DEVICE_POINTER The ID3D11Device associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D11_SWAPCHAIN_POINTER The IDXGISwapChain1 associated with the renderer. This may change when the window is resized.
With the direct3d12 renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D12_DEVICE_POINTER The ID3D12Device associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D12_SWAPCHAIN_POINTER The IDXGISwapChain4 associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_D3D12_COMMAND_QUEUE_POINTER The ID3D12CommandQueue associated with the renderer
With the vulkan renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_INSTANCE_POINTER The VkInstance associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_SURFACE_NUMBER The VkSurfaceKHR associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_PHYSICAL_DEVICE_POINTER The VkPhysicalDevice associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_DEVICE_POINTER The VkDevice associated with the renderer
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_GRAPHICS_QUEUE_FAMILY_INDEX_NUMBER The queue family index used for rendering
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_PRESENT_QUEUE_FAMILY_INDEX_NUMBER The queue family index used for presentation
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_SWAPCHAIN_IMAGE_COUNT_NUMBER The number of swapchain images, or potential frames in flight, used by the Vulkan renderer
With the gpu renderer:
SDL_PROP_RENDERER_GPU_DEVICE_POINTER The SDL_GPUDevice associated with the renderer

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_GetRenderLogicalPresentation(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.Int32*,System.Int32*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererLogicalPresentation*)
member.summary: Get device independent resolution and presentation mode for rendering
member.param: The rendering context
An int filled with the logical presentation width
An int filled with the logical presentation height
A variable filled with the logical presentation mode being used
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_RenderDebugText(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.Single,System.Single,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Draw debug text to an SDL_Renderer
member.param: The renderer which should draw a line of text
The x coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw
The y coordinate where the top-left corner of the text will draw
The string to render
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function will render a string of text to an SDL_Renderer. Note that this is a convenience function for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production apps and games.

Among these limitations:
It accepts UTF-8 strings, but will only renders ASCII characters
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use logical presentation or SDL_SetRenderScale() to adjust it.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It does no word-wrapping and does not treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the window, it's gone.
For serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf, stb_truetype, or other external libraries.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is destroyed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by SDL_SetRenderDrawColor().
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_RenderDebugTextFormat(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.Single,System.Single,System.Byte*)

remarks.para: This function will render a printf()-style format string to a renderer. Note that this is a convenience function for debugging, with severe limitations, and is not intended to be used for production apps and games.
For the full list of limitations and other useful information, see SDL_RenderDebugText.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_RenderPresent(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*)
member.summary: Update the screen with any rendering performed since the previous call
member.param: The rendering context
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: SDL's rendering functions operate on a backbuffer; that is, calling a rendering function such as SDL_RenderLine() does not directly put a line on the screen, but rather updates the backbuffer. As such, you compose your entire scene and present the composed backbuffer to the screen as a complete picture.
Therefore, when using SDL's rendering API, one does all drawing intended for the frame, and then calls this function once per frame to present the final drawing to the user.

The backbuffer should be considered invalidated after each present; do not assume that previous contents will exist between frames. You are strongly encouraged to call SDL_RenderClear() to initialize the backbuffer before starting each new frame's drawing, even if you plan to overwrite every pixel.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_RenderReadPixels(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*)
member.summary: Read pixels from the current rendering target
member.param: The rendering context
An SDL_Rect structure representing the area to read, which will be clipped to the current viewport, or NULL for the entire viewport
member.returns: Returns a new SDL_Surface on success or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The returned surface contains pixels inside the desired area clipped to the current viewport, and should be freed with SDL_DestroySurface().
Note that this returns the actual pixels on the screen, so if you are using logical presentation you should use SDL_GetRenderLogicalPresentationRect() to get the area containing your content.
WARNING: This is a very slow operation, and should not be used frequently. If you're using this on the main rendering target, it should be called after rendering and before SDL_RenderPresent().
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.summary: Perform a scaled copy using the 9-grid algorithm to the current rendering target at subpixel precision
member.param: The renderer which should copy parts of a texture
The source texture
The SDL_Rect structure representing the rectangle to be used for the 9-grid, or NULL to use the entire texture
The width, in pixels, of the left corners in
The width, in pixels, of the right corners in
The height, in pixels, of the top corners in
The height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in
The scale used to transform the corner of into the corner of , or 0.0f for an unscaled copy
A pointer to the destination rectangle, or NULL for the entire rendering target
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The pixels in the texture are split into a 3x3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of the destination rectangle. The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining destination rectangle.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function sets the width and height of the logical rendering output. The renderer will act as if the current render target is always the requested dimensions, scaling to the actual resolution as necessary.
This can be useful for games that expect a fixed size, but would like to scale the output to whatever is available, regardless of how a user resizes a window, or if the display is high DPI.

Logical presentation can be used with both render target textures and the renderer's window; the state is unique to each render target, and this function sets the state for the current render target. It might be useful to draw to a texture that matches the window dimensions with logical presentation enabled, and then draw that texture across the entire window with logical presentation disabled. Be careful not to render both with logical presentation enabled, however, as this could produce double-letterboxing, etc.

You can disable logical coordinates by setting the mode to SDL_LOGICAL_PRESENTATION_DISABLED, and in that case you get the full pixel resolution of the render target; it is safe to toggle logical presentation during the rendering of a frame: perhaps most of the rendering is done to specific dimensions but to make fonts look sharp, the app turns off logical presentation while drawing text, for example.
You can convert coordinates in an event into rendering coordinates using SDL_ConvertEventToRenderCoordinates().
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_SetRenderScale(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Set the drawing scale for rendering on the current target
member.param: The rendering context
Zhe horizontal scaling factor
The vertical scaling factor
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The drawing coordinates are scaled by the x/y scaling factors before they are used by the renderer. This allows resolution independent drawing with a single coordinate system.
If this results in scaling or subpixel drawing by the rendering backend, it will be handled using the appropriate quality hints. For best results use integer scaling factors.
Each render target has its own scale. This function sets the scale for the current render target.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_SetRenderTarget(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*)
member.summary: Set a texture as the current rendering target
member.param: The rendering context
The targeted texture, which must be created with the SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_TARGET flag, or NULL to render to the window instead of a texture
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The default render target is the window for which the renderer was created. To stop rendering to a texture and render to the window again, call this function with a NULL .

Viewport, cliprect, scale, and logical presentation are unique to each render target. Get and set functions for these states apply to the current render target set by this function, and those states persist on each target when the current render target changes.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_SetRenderViewport(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*)
member.summary: Set the drawing area for rendering on the current target
member.param: The rendering context
The SDL_Rect structure representing the drawing area, or NULL to set the viewport to the entire target
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Drawing will clip to this area (separately from any clipping done with SDL_SetRenderClipRect), and the top left of the area will become coordinate (0, 0) for future drawing commands.
The area's width and height must be >= 0.
Each render target has its own viewport. This function sets the viewport for the current render target.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_SetRenderVSync(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererVSync)
member.summary: Toggle VSync of the given renderer
member.param: The renderer to toggle
The vertical refresh sync interval
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: When a renderer is created, vsync defaults to SDL_RENDERER_VSYNC_DISABLED.

The parameter can be 1 to synchronize present with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize present with every second vertical refresh, etc., SDL_RENDERER_VSYNC_ADAPTIVE for late swap tearing (adaptive vsync), or SDL_RENDERER_VSYNC_DISABLED to disable. Not every value is supported by every driver, so you should check the return value to see whether the requested setting is supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

In that case, drawing still uses the sRGB color space, but individual values can go beyond 1.0 and floating point textures can be used for HDR content. This defaults to .

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.SDL_GetRenderMetalCommandEncoder(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*)
member.summary: Get the Metal command encoder for the current frame
member.param: The renderer to query
member.returns: Returns an id<MTLRenderCommandEncoder> on success, or NULL if the renderer isn't a Metal renderer or there was an error

remarks.para: This function returns void *, so SDL doesn't have to include Metal's headers, but it can be safely cast to an id<MTLRenderCommandEncoder>.

This will return NULL if Metal refuses to give SDL a drawable to render to, which might happen if the window is hidden/minimized/offscreen. This doesn't apply to command encoders for render targets, just the window's backbuffer. Check your return values!
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.SDL_AddVulkanRenderSemaphores(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.UInt32,System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Add a set of synchronization semaphores for the current frame
member.param: The rendering context
The VkPipelineStageFlags for the wait
A VkSempahore to wait on before rendering the current frame, or 0 if not needed
A VkSempahore that SDL will signal when rendering for the current frame is complete, or 0 if not needed
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The Vulkan renderer will wait for before submitting rendering commands and signal after rendering commands are complete for this frame.

This should be called each frame that you want semaphore synchronization. The Vulkan renderer may have multiple frames in flight on the GPU, so you should have multiple semaphores that are used for synchronization. Querying SDL_PROP_RENDERER_VULKAN_SWAPCHAIN_IMAGE_COUNT_NUMBER will give you the maximum number of semaphores you'll need.
It is NOT safe to call this function from two threads at once.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.<G>$4C636481E1FED4372F897E46407FA892`1.TryRenderDebugText(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Point{System.Single}@,System.String)
member.summary: Tries to draw debug text to the current target
member.param: The top-left point where the text should be drawn
The text to be drawn
member.returns: , if the text was drawn successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method will render text to a . Note that this is a convenience method for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production applications and games.

Among these limitations are:
It accepts Unicode strings, but will only render ASCII characters
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use or to adjust for that.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It doesn't do word-wrapping and doesn't treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the target, it's gone.
For more serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is disposed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by (or ) and determine how the text is blended with the existing content of the target.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.<G>$4C636481E1FED4372F897E46407FA892`1.TryRenderDebugText(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Point{System.Single}@,System.String,System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Object})
member.summary: Tries to draw a debug format string to the current target
member.param: The top-left point where the text should be drawn
The C-style printf format string
The arguments corresponding to the format specifiers in
member.returns: , if the text was drawn successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method will render text to a . Note that this is a convenience method for debugging, with severe limitations, and not intended to be used for production applications and games.

Among these limitations are:
It accepts Unicode strings, but will only render ASCII characters
It has a single, tiny size (8x8 pixels). You can use or to adjust for that.
It uses a simple, hardcoded bitmap font. It does not allow different font selections and it does not support truetype, for proper scaling.
It doesn't do word-wrapping and doesn't treat newline characters as a line break. If the text goes out of the target, it's gone.
For more serious text rendering, there are several good options, such as SDL_ttf.
On first use, this will create an internal texture for rendering glyphs. This texture will live until the renderer is disposed.
The text is drawn in the color specified by (or ) and determine how the text is blended with the existing content of the target.

The parameter is interpreted as a C-style printf format string, and the parameter supplies the values for its format specifiers. Supported argument types include all integral types up to 64-bit (including and ), floating point types ( and ), pointer types ( and ), and .
For a detailed explanation of C-style printf format strings and their specifiers, see .
Consider using instead when possible, as it may be more efficient. In many cases, you can use C# string interpolation to construct the message before logging.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

remarks.para: The line is drawn with the color specified by (or ) and determines how the line is blended with the existing content of the target.

remarks.para: If the is , SDL will automatically select or create a suitable GPU device for you. The can be later retrieved from the renderer using the property.

If the is , the created renderer will be an off-screen renderer, not associated with any window. In that case, you should set a valid for the renderer and then still call normally to complete drawing a frame (note that that's different from using a target texture with a window-associated renderer, where you shouldn't call when rendering to the target texture).
If this method is called with a non- , it should be called on the thread that created the device. If it is called with a non- , it should be called on the thread that created the window.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.<G>$DD2A532C94276C9EC619509987A58185.MetalCommandEncoder
member.summary: Gets the Metal command encoder for the current frame
member.value: The Metal command encoder for the current frame, as an id<MTLCommandEncoder> handle

remarks.para: The value of this property can be directly cast to an id<MTLCommandEncoder> handle.

The value of this property can be () if Metal refuses to give SDL a drawable to render to, which might happen if the window is hidden, minimized, or off-screen. However, this doesn't apply to command encoders for render targets though, just the window's backbuffer.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.RendererExtensions.<G>$208551F690AB35561A3F9E4DC56DC4BF.TryAddVulkanRenderSemaphores(System.UInt32,System.Int64,System.Int64)
member.summary: Tries to add a set synchronization semaphores for the current frame
member.param: The VkPipelineStageFlags for the wait
A VkSemaphore handle to wait on before rendering the current frame, or 0 if not needed. Must be directly cast to a from a VkSemaphore handle.
A VkSemaphore handle that SDL will signal when rendering for the current frame is complete, or 0 if not needed. Must be directly cast to a from a VkSemaphore handle.
member.returns: , if the semaphores were successfully added for this frame; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The Vulkan renderer will wait for before submitting rendering commands and signal after rendering commands are completed for this frame.

This method should be called each frame that you want semaphore synchronization. The Vulkan renderer may have multiple frames in flight on the GPU, so you should have multiple semaphores that are used for synchronization. The value of the property gives you the maximum number of semaphores you'll need.
It is not safe to call this method from two different threads at once.

member.param: A VkImage handle to associate with the newly created texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture. Must be directly cast to a from a VkImage handle.
A VkImageLayout value for the VkImage. Must be directly cast to a from a VkImageLayout value.

member.exception: is less than (0)
is equal to (-1) or (0)

If you want to disable vertical synchronization, use instead. If you want to enable adaptive vertical synchronization, use instead.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer`1
member.summary: Represents a rendering context (renderer)
member.typeparam: The rendering driver type associated with this renderer

remarks.para: This is used to perform driver-specific 2D rendering operations, most commonly to a or an off-screen render target.
You can create new renderers for a using the or instance methods on a instance.
Additionally, there are some driver-specific methods for creating renderers, such as , , , , , and .

If you create textures using an , please remember to dispose them before disposing the renderer. Do not dispose the associated before disposing the either! Using an after its associated has been disposed can cause undefined behavior, including crashes.
For the most part s are not thread-safe, and most of their properties and methods should only be accessed from the main thread!
s are concrete renderer types, associate with a specific rendering driver. They are used for driver-specific rendering operations with s that were created by them.
If you want to use them in a more general way, you can use them as instances, which serve as abstractions to use them for common rendering operations with instance that were created by them.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer`1.PropertyNames
member.summary: Provides property names for properties

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture
member.summary: Represents a texture, created by an

remarks.para: This is an efficient driver-specific representation of pixel data.
You can create new textures using the , , or instance methods on an instance.
Please remember to dispose s before disposing the that created them! Using an after its associated has been disposed can lead to undefined behavior, including corruption and crashes.

s are not driver-agnostic! Most of the time instance of this abstract class are of the concrete type with a specific rendering driver as the type argument. However, the type exists as an abstraction to use them in common rendering operations with the instance that created them.
To specify a concrete texture type, use with a rendering driver that implements the interface (e.g. Texture<OpenGL>).

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.Access
member.summary: Gets the access mode of the texture
member.value: The access mode of the texture

remarks.para: The value of this property determines whether the texture is lockable and/or can be used as a render target.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.AlphaMod
member.summary: Gets or sets the additional alpha modulation value multiplied into render copy operations
member.value: The additional alpha modulation value multiplied into render copy operations, in the range 0 to 255, where 0 is fully transparent and 255 is fully opaque

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation the source alpha value is modulated by this alpha value according to the following formula:
srcA = srcA * (alpha / 255)
Alpha modulation is not always supported by the renderer.
The value of this property is equivalent to * 255, rounded to the nearest integer.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.AlphaModFloat
member.summary: Gets or sets the additional alpha modulation value multiplied into render copy operations
member.value: The additional alpha modulation value multiplied into render copy operations, in the range 0.0 to 1.0, where 0.0 is fully transparent and 1.0 is fully opaque

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation the source alpha value is modulated by this alpha value according to the following formula:
srcA = srcA * alpha
Alpha modulation is not always supported by the renderer.
The value of this property is equivalent to / 255.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.BlendMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the blend mode used for texture copy operations
member.value: The blend mode used for texture copy operations

remarks.para: This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting this property, the specified blend is or not supported by the renderer (check for more information)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.ColorMod
member.summary: Gets or sets the additional color modulation value multiplied into render copy operations
member.value: The additional color modulation value multiplied into render copy operations

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula:
srcC = srcC * (color / 255)
Color modulation is not always supported by the renderer.
The value of this property is equivalent to (.R * 255, .G * 255, .B * 255), each component rounded to the nearest integer.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.ColorModFloat
member.summary: Gets or sets the additional color modulation value multiplied into render copy operations
member.value: The additional color modulation value multiplied into render copy operations

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula:
srcC = srcC * color
Color modulation is not always supported by the renderer.
The value of this property is equivalent to (.R / 255, .G / 255, .B / 255).
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.ColorSpace
member.summary: Gets the color space of the texture
member.value: The color space of the texture

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.Format
member.summary: Gets the pixel format of the texture
member.value: The pixel format of the texture

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.HdrHeadroom
member.summary: Gets the maximum dynamic range, in terms of the SDR white point
member.value: The maximum dynamic range, in terms of the SDR white point

remarks.para: This property is used for HDR10 and floating point textures.
The value of this property defaults to 1.0 for SDR textures, 4.0 for HDR10 textures, and has no default for floating point textures.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.Height
member.summary: Gets the height of the texture
member.value: The height of the texture, in pixels

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.Palette
member.summary: Gets or sets the palette used by the texture
member.value: The palette used by the texture, or if the texture doesn't use a palette

remarks.para: A palette is only used by textures with an indexed pixel format.
A single can be shared between multiple textures.

member.exception: When setting this property, the specified palette doesn't match the texture's pixel format (check for more information)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SdrWhitePoint
member.summary: Gets the defining value for 100% white
member.value: The defining value for 100% white

remarks.para: This property is used for HDR10 and floating point textures.
The value of this property defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom.
The value defaults to 100 for HDR textures and 1.0 for other textures.

remarks.para: This property should only be accessed from the main thread.
