member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.TryLock(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}@,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager@)
member.summary: Tries to lock an area of the texture for write-only pixel access and set up a pixel memory manager
member.param: The area of the texture to lock for write-only pixel access
The pixel memory manager meant to be used to access the texture's pixels, if this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the texture was successfully locked for write-only pixel access successfully and the was created; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method is meant to be used as a simpler and safer alternative to using and .
This method fails and returns if the texture's is not .

If the was created successfully, you can use its property to write the pixel memory of the locked area, using this texture's . Once you're done accessing the pixel memory, you should dispose the to unlock the texture and apply the changes.

As an optimization, the pixels made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old texture data. This is a write-only operation, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.

Warning: Please note that locking a texture is intended to be write-only; it will not guarantee the previous contents of the texture will be provided. You must fully initialize any area of a texture that you locked before unlocking it (disposing the ), as the pixels might otherwise be uninitialized memory. E.g., if you locked a texture and immediately unlocked it again without writing any pixel data, the texture could end up in a corrupted state, depending on the renderer in use.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.TryUpdate(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}@,Sdl3Sharp.Utilities.ReadOnlyNativeMemory,System.Int32)
member.summary: Tries to update an area of the texture with new pixel data
member.param: The area of the texture to update with the new pixel data
The new pixel data to be copied into the specified area of the texture, in the pixel format of the texture

The pitch used in the given data, in bytes; that is the length between the start of a row of pixels and the start of the next row of pixels (which may be greater than the pixel width of the given data, expressed in bytes)
member.returns: , if the texture was successfully updated with the new pixel data; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The given data must be in the pixel format of the texture.
This is a fairly slow operation, intended for use with static textures that do not change often.

If the texture is intended to be updated often, you should prefer to create the texture as streaming and use one of the locking mechanisms to manipulate the texture's pixels (e.g. ) instead. While this method still works with streaming textures, for optimization reasons you may not get the pixels back if you lock the texture afterward.

Using this method for NV12, NV21, YV12 or IYUV textures is perfectly fine as long as the given data is a contiguous block of NV12/N21 planes or Y and UV planes, respectively, in the proper order. If not, you should use or , respectively, instead.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.UnsafeUnlock
member.summary: Unlocks the texture after write-only pixel access

remarks.para: This method is meant to be used in conjuction with any of the "TryUnsafeLock" or "TryUnsafeLockToSurface" methods (e.g. ), if you want to access the texture's pixels directly in a faster and more efficient way. If you're locking for a simpler and safer way to access the texture's pixels, consider using any of the "TryLock" or "TryLockToSurface" methods (e.g. ) instead.

Warning: Please note that locking a texture is intended to be write-only; it will not guarantee the previous contents of the texture will be provided. You must fully initialize any area of a texture that you locked before unlocking it, as the pixels might otherwise be uninitialized memory. E.g., if you locked a texture and immediately unlocked it again without writing any pixel data, the texture could end up in a corrupted state, depending on the renderer in use.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_CreateTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormat,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TextureAccess,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Create a texture for a rendering context
member.param: The rendering context
One of the enumerated values in SDL_PixelFormat
One of the enumerated values in SDL_TextureAccess
The width of the texture in pixels
The height of the texture in pixels
member.returns: Returns the created texture or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_CreateTextureFromSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Create a texture from an existing surface
member.param: The rendering context
The SDL_Surface structure containing pixel data used to fill the texture
member.returns: Returns the created texture or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The surface is not modified or freed by this function.
The SDL_TextureAccess hint for the created texture is SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_STATIC.
The pixel format of the created texture may be different from the pixel format of the surface, and can be queried using the SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_FORMAT_NUMBER property.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_CreateTextureWithProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer.SDL_Renderer*,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Create a texture for a rendering context with the specified properties
member.param: The rendering context
The properties to use
member.returns: Returns the created texture or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
These are the supported properties:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_COLORSPACE_NUMBER
An SDL_Colorspace value describing the texture colorspace, defaults to SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR for floating point textures, SDL_COLORSPACE_HDR10 for 10-bit textures, SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB for other RGB textures and SDL_COLORSPACE_JPEG for YUV textures
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_FORMAT_NUMBER One of the enumerated values in SDL_PixelFormat, defaults to the best RGBA format for the renderer
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_ACCESS_NUMBER One of the enumerated values in SDL_TextureAccess, defaults to SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_STATIC
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_WIDTH_NUMBER The width of the texture in pixels, required
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_HEIGHT_NUMBER The height of the texture in pixels, required
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for floating point textures.
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This would be equivalent to maxCLL / SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT for HDR10 content. If this is defined, any values outside the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they are clipped.
With the direct3d11 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D11_TEXTURE_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D11_TEXTURE_U_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D11_TEXTURE_V_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
With the direct3d12 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D12_TEXTURE_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D12_TEXTURE_U_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_D3D12_TEXTURE_V_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
With the metal renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_METAL_PIXELBUFFER_POINTER The CVPixelBufferRef associated with the texture, if you want to create a texture from an existing pixel buffer
With the opengl renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_UV_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_U_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_V_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
With the opengles2 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_UV_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_U_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_V_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
With the vulkan renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_VULKAN_TEXTURE_NUMBER The VkImage associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_VULKAN_LAYOUT_NUMBER The VkImageLayout for the VkImage, defaults to VK_IMAGE_LAYOUT_SHADER_READ_ONLY_OPTIMAL
With the GPU renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_GPU_TEXTURE_POINTER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_GPU_TEXTURE_UV_NUMBER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_GPU_TEXTURE_U_NUMBER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_CREATE_GPU_TEXTURE_V_NUMBER the SDL_GPUTexture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture, if you want to wrap an existing texture
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_GetTextureProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*)
member.summary: Get the properties associated with a texture
member.param: The texture to query
member.returns: Returns a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_COLORSPACE_NUMBER An SDL_Colorspace value describing the texture colorspace
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_FORMAT_NUMBER One of the enumerated values in SDL_PixelFormat
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_ACCESS_NUMBER One of the enumerated values in SDL_TextureAccess
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_WIDTH_NUMBER The width of the texture in pixels
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_HEIGHT_NUMBER The height of the texture in pixels
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for other textures.
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point textures, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. If this is defined, any values outside the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they are clipped. This defaults to 1.0 for SDR textures, 4.0 for HDR10 textures, and no default for floating point textures.
With the direct3d11 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D11_TEXTURE_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D11_TEXTURE_U_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D11_TEXTURE_V_POINTER The ID3D11Texture2D associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
With the direct3d12 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D12_TEXTURE_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D12_TEXTURE_U_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_D3D12_TEXTURE_V_POINTER The ID3D12Resource associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
With the vulkan renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_VULKAN_TEXTURE_NUMBER The VkImage associated with the texture
With the opengl renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_UV_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_U_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_V_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEXTURE_TARGET_NUMBER The GLenum for the texture target (GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_RECTANGLE_ARB, etc)
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEX_W_FLOAT The texture coordinate width of the texture (0.0 - 1.0)
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGL_TEX_H_FLOAT The texture coordinate height of the texture (0.0 - 1.0)
With the opengles2 renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_UV_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_U_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_V_NUMBER The GLuint texture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_OPENGLES2_TEXTURE_TARGET_NUMBER The GLenum for the texture target (GL_TEXTURE_2D, GL_TEXTURE_EXTERNAL_OES, etc)
With the gpu renderer:
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_GPU_TEXTURE_POINTER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_GPU_TEXTURE_UV_POINTER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the UV plane of an NV12 texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_GPU_TEXTURE_U_POINTER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the U plane of a YUV texture
SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_GPU_TEXTURE_V_POINTER The SDL_GPUTexture associated with the V plane of a YUV texture

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_GetTextureScaleMode(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.ScaleMode*)
member.summary: Get the scale mode used for texture scale operations
member.param: The texture to query
A pointer filled in with the current scale mode
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_GetTextureSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,System.Single*,System.Single*)
member.summary: Get the size of a texture, as floating point values
member.param: The texture to query
A pointer filled in with the width of the texture in pixels. This argument can be NULL if you don't need this information.
A pointer filled in with the height of the texture in pixels. This argument can be NULL if you don't need this information.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_LockTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,System.Void**,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Lock a portion of the texture for write-only pixel access
member.param: The texture to lock for access, which was created with SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_STREAMING
An SDL_Rect structure representing the area to lock for access; NULL to lock the entire texture
This is filled in with a pointer to the locked pixels, appropriately offset by the locked area
This is filled in with the pitch of the locked pixels; the pitch is the length of one row in bytes
member.returns: Returns true on success or false if the texture is not valid or was not created with SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_STREAMING; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: As an optimization, the pixels made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old texture data. This is a write-only operation, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.
You must use SDL_UnlockTexture() to unlock the pixels and apply any changes.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_LockTextureToSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface**)
member.summary: Lock a portion of the texture for write-only pixel access, and expose it as a SDL surface
member.param: The texture to lock for access, which must be created with SDL_TEXTUREACCESS_STREAMING
An SDL_Rect structure representing the area to lock for access; NULL to lock the entire texture
A pointer to an SDL surface of size . Don't assume any specific pixel content.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Besides providing an SDL_Surface instead of raw pixel data, this function operates like SDL_LockTexture.

As an optimization, the pixels made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old texture data. This is a write-only operation, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.
You must use SDL_UnlockTexture() to unlock the pixels and apply any changes.
The returned surface is freed internally after calling SDL_UnlockTexture() or SDL_DestroyTexture(). The caller should not free it.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureAlphaMod(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,System.Byte)
member.summary: Set an additional alpha value multiplied into render copy operations
member.param: The texture to update
The source alpha value multiplied into copy operations
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation the source alpha value is modulated by this alpha value according to the following formula:
srcA = srcA * (alpha / 255)
Alpha modulation is not always supported by the renderer; it will return false if alpha modulation is not supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureAlphaModFloat(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,System.Single)
member.summary: Set an additional alpha value multiplied into render copy operations
member.param: The texture to update
The source alpha value multiplied into copy operations
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation the source alpha value is modulated by this alpha value according to the following formula:
srcA = srcA * alpha
Alpha modulation is not always supported by the renderer; it will return false if alpha modulation is not supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureBlendMode(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Blending.BlendMode)
member.summary: Set the blend mode for a texture, used by SDL_RenderTexture()
member.param: The texture to update
The SDL_BlendMode to use for texture blending
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the blend mode is not supported, the closest supported mode is chosen and this function returns false.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureColorMod(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Set an additional color value multiplied into render copy operations
member.param: The texture to update
The red color value multiplied into copy operations
The green color value multiplied into copy operations
The blue color value multiplied into copy operations
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula:
srcC = srcC * (color / 255)
Color modulation is not always supported by the renderer; it will return false if color modulation is not supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureColorModFloat(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,System.Single,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Set an additional color value multiplied into render copy operations
member.param: The texture to update
The red color value multiplied into copy operations
The green color value multiplied into copy operations
The blue color value multiplied into copy operations
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
When this texture is rendered, during the copy operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula:
srcC = srcC * color
Color modulation is not always supported by the renderer; it will return false if color modulation is not supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTexturePalette(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*)
member.summary: Set the palette used by a texture
member.param: The texture to update
The SDL_Palette structure to use
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Setting the palette keeps an internal reference to the palette, which can be safely destroyed afterwards.
A single palette can be shared with many textures.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_SetTextureScaleMode(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.ScaleMode)
member.summary: Set the scale mode used for texture scale operations
member.param: The texture to update
The SDL_ScaleMode to use for texture scaling
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The default texture scale mode is SDL_SCALEMODE_LINEAR.
If the scale mode is not supported, the closest supported mode is chosen.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_UnlockTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*)
member.summary: Unlock a texture, uploading the changes to video memory, if needed
member.param: A texture locked by SDL_LockTexture()

remarks.para: Warning: Please note that SDL_LockTexture() is intended to be write-only; it will not guarantee the previous contents of the texture will be provided. You must fully initialize any area of a texture that you lock before unlocking it, as the pixels might otherwise be uninitialized memory.
Which is to say: locking and immediately unlocking a texture can result in corrupted textures, depending on the renderer in use.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_UpdateNVTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Byte*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Update a rectangle within a planar NV12 or NV21 texture with new pixels
member.param: The texture to update
A pointer to the rectangle of pixels to update, or NULL to update the entire texture
The raw pixel data for the Y plane
The number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the Y plane
The raw pixel data for the UV plane
The number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the UV plane
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: You can use SDL_UpdateTexture() as long as your pixel data is a contiguous block of NV12/21 planes in the proper order, but this function is available if your pixel data is not contiguous.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_UpdateTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,System.Void*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Update the given texture rectangle with new pixel data
member.param: The texture to update
An SDL_Rect structure representing the area to update, or NULL to update the entire texture
The raw pixel data in the format of the texture
The number of bytes in a row of pixel data, including padding between lines
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The pixel data must be in the pixel format of the texture, which can be queried using the SDL_PROP_TEXTURE_FORMAT_NUMBER property.
This is a fairly slow function, intended for use with static textures that do not change often.

If the texture is intended to be updated often, it is preferred to create the texture as streaming and use the locking functions referenced below. While this function will work with streaming textures, for optimization reasons you may not get the pixels back if you lock the texture afterward.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_UpdateYUVTexture(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.SDL_Texture*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Byte*,System.Int32,System.Byte*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Update a rectangle within a planar YV12 or IYUV texture with new pixel data
member.param: the texture to update
A pointer to the rectangle of pixels to update, or NULL to update the entire texture
The raw pixel data for the Y plane
The number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the Y plane
The raw pixel data for the U plane
The number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the U plane
The raw pixel data for the V plane
The number of bytes between rows of pixel data for the V plane
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: You can use SDL_UpdateTexture() as long as your pixel data is a contiguous block of Y and U/V planes in the proper order, but this function is available if your pixel data is not contiguous.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames
member.summary: Provides property names for properties

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateColorSpaceNumber
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the color space of the texture to create

remarks.para: The value of the associated property should be a describing the color space that should be used by the newly created texture. This defaults to for floating point textures, for 10-bit textures, for RGB textures and for YUV textures.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateFormatNumber
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the pixel format of the texture to create

remarks.para: The value of the associated property should be any of the pre-defined values describing the pixel format that should be used by the newly created texture. This defaults to the best RGBA format for the renderer that creates the texture.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateAccessNumber
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the access mode of the texture to create

remarks.para: The value of the associated property should be any of the pre-defined values describing the access mode that should be used by the newly created texture. This defaults to .

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateWidthNumber
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the width of the texture to create

remarks.para: The width of the texture to create is given in pixels. This property is required when creating a texture. Although, you don't need to explicitly specify this property if you set the width parameter in a call to the method.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateHeightNumber
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the height of the texture to create

remarks.para: The height of the texture to create is given in pixels. This property is required when creating a texture. Although, you don't need to explicitly specify this property if you set the height parameter in a call to the method.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreatePalettePointer
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the palette to use when creating a with a palettized pixel format

remarks.para: This can be set or changed later using the property of a .

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateSdrWhitePointFloat
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the defining value for 100% diffuse white for HDR10 and floating point s when creating them

remarks.para: For HDR10 and floating point s, the value of the property defines the value of 100% diffuse white that should be used by the newly created texture, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for other textures.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.CreateHdrHeadroomFloat
member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a that holds the maximum dynamic range for HDR10 and floating point s when creating them

remarks.para: For HDR10 and floating point s, the value of the property defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content of the newly created texture, in terms of the SDR white point. This would be equivalent to the maximum content light level (maxCLL) divided by the SDR white point for HDR10 content. If the associated property is defined, any values outside of the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they will be clipped.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.ColorSpaceNumber
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the color space of the texture

remarks.para: The value of the associated property is a describing the color space used by the texture.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.FormatNumber
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the pixel format of the texture

remarks.para: The value of the associated property is any of the pre-defined values.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.AccessNumber
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the access mode of the texture

remarks.para: The value of the associated property is any of the pre-defined values.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.WidthNumber
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the width of the texture

remarks.para: The width of the texture is given in pixels.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.HeightNumber
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the height of the texture

remarks.para: The height of the texture is given in pixels.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.SdrWhitePointFloat
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the defining value for 100% diffuse white for HDR10 and floating point s

remarks.para: For HDR10 and floating point s, the value of the property defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 100 for HDR10 textures and 1.0 for other textures.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture.PropertyNames.HdrHeadroomFloat
member.summary: The name of a read-only property that holds the maximum dynamic range for HDR10 and floating point s

remarks.para: For HDR10 and floating point s, the value of the property defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. If the associated property is defined, any values outside of the range supported by the display will be scaled into the available HDR headroom, otherwise they will be clipped. This defaults to 1.0 for SDR textures, 4.0 for HDR10 textures and has no default for floating point textures.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TextureAddressMode.Wrap

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager
member.summary: Manages and represents the entire pixel memory of a locked or a partial rectangle of it

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.IsPinned
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the is currently locked (pinned)
member.value: if the is currently locked (pinned); otherwise, ( is in that case)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.Memory
member.summary: Gets a representing the pixel memory of the locked
member.value: A representing the pixel memory of the locked , or if the is not locked

remarks.para: The pixel memory data is in the pixel format specified by the property of the .

The pixel memory might be longer than the actual pixel data that's safe to access, due to padding at the end of each row. Especially if the was locked with a rectangle smaller than the full size of the texture.

The pixel memory is laid out in rows, where each row is pixels long, and there are rows. Between the start of each row, there are bytes. The returned by this property starts at the beginning of the first row.
As an optimization, the pixel data made available for editing don't necessarily contain the old (at the time of locking) texture data.
Notice: The pixel memory is write-only, and if you need to keep a copy of the texture data you should do that at the application level.
You must dispose the to unlock the and apply the changes made to the pixel data.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.Pitch
member.summary: Gets the pitch of the pixel memory, in bytes
member.value: The pitch of the pixel memory, in bytes

remarks.para: The pitch is the length of a single row of pixel data in bytes, including any padding bytes at the end of the row. Notice that that's not necessarily equal to RowLength * Texture.Format.BytesPerPixel. Especially if the was locked with a rectangle smaller than the full size of the texture.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.RowCount
member.summary: Gets the number of rows in the pixel memory, in pixels
member.value: The number of rows in the pixel memory, in pixels

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.RowLength
member.summary: Gets the length of each row in the pixel memory, in pixels
member.value: The length of each row in the pixel memory, in pixels

remarks.para: To get the length of each row in bytes, use RowLength * Texture.Format.BytesPerPixel.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.Texture
member.summary: Gets the locked texture
member.value: The locked texture, or if the texture is not locked

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.TexturePixelMemoryManager.Dispose(System.Boolean)
member.summary: Disposes the , unlocking the associated

remarks.para: Calling this method unlocks the associated , if it's still locked, applying any changes made to the pixel data, and making its pixel memory inaccessible until it's locked again.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Texture`1
member.summary: Represents a texture, created by a
member.typeparam: The rendering driver type associated with this texture

remarks.para: This is an efficient driver-specific representation of pixel data.
You can create new textures using the , , or instance methods on a instance.
Additionally, there are some driver-specific methods for creating textures, such as Direct3D 11, Direct3D 12, GPU, Metal, OpenGL, OpenGL ES 2, and Vulkan.
Please remember to dispose s before disposing the that created them! Using an after its associated has been disposed can lead to undefined behavior, including corruption and crashes.
s are concrete texture types, associated with a specific rendering driver. They are used in driver-specific rendering operations with the that created them.
If you want to use them in a more general way, you can use them as instances, which serve as abstractions to use them in common rendering operations with the instance that created them.

member.summary: Creates a new with the specified width, height, pixel format and pre-allocated pixel memory
member.param: The width of the surface
The height of the surface
The pixel format of the surface
The pre-allocated pixel memory
The pitch of the surface, in bytes

remarks.para: No copy is made of the , instead it's referenced directly as the pixel memory of the newly created surface. That also means that must remain valid and alive for the lifetime of the surface. This is not handled automatically for you!
The is the offset in bytes between each vertical pixel row in the memory, e.g. 4 * for a .

member.param: The stream to load the image from
A value indicating whether the stream should be closed before this method returns (even in case of an error)

remarks.para: The format of the data in the stream must be BMP or PNG, otherwise this constructor will fail.
Remember to dispose of the resulting surface when you are done with it. Not doing so will result in a memory leak.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.AlphaMod
member.summary: Gets or sets the alpha modulation value used in blit operations
member.value: The alpha modulation value used in blit operations

remarks.para: When this surface is blitted, during the blit operation the source alpha value is modulated by this alpha value according to the following formula: srcA = srcA * (alpha / 255)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.BlendMode
member.summary: Gets or sets the blend mode used for blit operations
member.value: The blend mode used for blit operations

remarks.para: To copy a surface to another surface without blending with the existing data, the of the source surface should be set to .

member.exception: When setting this property, the specified blend mode is or not supported by the platform (check for more information)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.ClippingRect
member.summary: Gets or sets the clipping rectangle for the surface
member.value: The clipping rectangle for the surface

remarks.para: When this surface is blitted onto, only the area within the clipping rectangle is drawn into. Note that blits are automatically clipped to the edges of the source and destination surfaces.
If the value of this property is equal to the surface's bounds, clipping is effectively disabled.
Alternatively, you can use the methods and to manipulate the clipping rectangle. The latter returns a value indicating whether the set clipping rectangle intersects the surface area.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.ColorKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the color key (transparent pixel value) in the surface
member.value: The color key (transparent pixel value) in the surface, as an encoded pixel value used by the surface, or if color keying is disabled

remarks.para: The color key defines a pixel value that will be treated as transparent in a blit. For example, one can use this to specify that cyan pixels should be considered transparent, and therefore not rendered.
The value of this property uses the encoded pixel value format used by the surface, as generated by .
Alternatively, if you just want to check whether color keying is enabled, you can use the property instead.

member.exception: When setting this property, the specified color key is invalid for the surface's pixel format (check for more information). For example, this can happen when the key is out of range for palettized formats.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.ColorMod
member.summary: Gets or sets the color modulation values used in blit operations
member.value: The color modulation values used in blit operations

remarks.para: When this surface is blitted, during the blit operation each source color channel is modulated by the appropriate color value according to the following formula: srcC = srcC * (color / 255)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.ColorSpace
member.summary: Gets or sets the color space of the surface
member.value: The color space of the surface

remarks.para: The color space defaults to for floating point formats, for 10-bit formats, for other RGB surfaces and for YUV textures.

Setting the color space doesn't change the pixels, only how they are interpreted in color operations. If you want to convert the pixels to a different color space, use an appropriate one of the TryConvert* methods.

remarks.para: It's safe to access only if this property is .

member.value: , if the surface uses run-length encoding; otherwise,

remarks.para: If RLE is enabled, color key and alpha blending blits are much faster, but the surface must be locked before directly accessing the pixels.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.MustLock
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the surface must be locked before accessing it's pixel memory
member.value: , if the surface must be locked before accessing it's pixel memory; otherwise,

remarks.para: This property is meant to be used as part of the - - - - pattern, if you want to access the surface's pixel memory directly in a faster and more efficient way. If you're looking for a simpler and safer way to access the pixel memory, consider using instead.
Not all surfaces require locking. If the value of this property is , then you can read and write to the surface at any time, and the pixel format of the surface will not change.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.Palette
member.summary: Gets or sets the palette used by the surface
member.value: The palette used by the surface, or if the surface doesn't use a palette

remarks.para: A single can be shared between multiple s.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.Pitch
member.summary: Gets the pitch of the surface, in bytes
member.value: The pitch of the surface, in bytes

remarks.para: The pitch is the length of a single row of pixels in bytes. This value may be different from the width of the surface times the number of bytes per pixel for packing and alignment purposes.
Pixels are arranged in memory in rows, with the top row first. Each row occupies an amount of memory given by the pitch (sometimes known as the "row stride").

Within each row, pixels are arranged from left to right until the width is reached. Each pixel occupies a number of bits appropriate for its format, with most formats representing each pixel as one or more whole bytes (in some indexed formats, instead multiple pixels are packed into each byte), and a byte order given by the format. After encoding all pixels, any remaining bytes to reach the pitch are used as padding to reach a desired alignment, and have undefined contents.

When a surface holds YUV format data, the planes are assumed to be contiguous without padding between them, e.g. a 32⨯32 surface in NV12 format with a pitch of 32 would consist of 32⨯32 bytes of Y plane followed by 32⨯16 bytes of UV plane.
When a surface holds MJPG format data, pixels points at the compressed JPEG image and pitch is the length of that data.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.Properties
member.summary: Gets the properties associated with the surface
member.value: The properties associated with the surface, or if the properties could not be retrieved successfully (check for more information)

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.Rotation
member.summary: Gets or sets the rotation angle, in degrees, for the surface
member.value: The rotation angle, in clockwise degrees, for the surface

remarks.para: The value of this property defines the number of degrees the surface's pixel data is meant to be rotated clockwise to make it right-side up.

This is used by the camera API, if a mobile device is oriented differently than what its camera provides (i.e. the camera always provides portrait images but the phone is being held in landscape orientation).
The value defaults to 0.0.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SdrWhitePoint
member.summary: Gets or sets the defining value for 100% white
member.value: The defining value for 100% white

remarks.para: This property is used by HDR10 and floating point s.
The value of the property defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom.
The value defaults to 203 for HDR10 surfaces and 1.0 for floating point surfaces.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TonemapOperator
member.summary: Gets or sets the expression of the tonemap operator to used when compressing from a higher dynamic range to a lower dynamic range
member.value: The expression of the tonemap operator to used when compressing from a higher dynamic range to a lower dynamic range

remarks.para:
The value of this property defines the tone mapping operator used when compressing from a with high dynamic range to another with lower dynamic range. Currently this supports the following values:
"chrome" The same tone mapping that Chrome uses for HDR content
"*=N" where N is a floating point number N is a floating point scale factor applied in linear. E.g. "*=0.5".
"none" Tone mapping is disabled
The value defaults to "chrome".

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.UnsafePixels

member.value: The underlying pixel memory of the surface

remarks.para: It's unsafe to access this property for surfaces that must be locked and are not currently locked.

This property is meant to be used as part of the - - - - pattern, if you want to access the surface's pixel memory directly in a faster and more efficient way. If you're looking for a simpler and safer way to access the pixel memory, consider using instead.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.MapColor(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Maps RGB component values to an opaque encoded pixel value for the surface
member.param: The red component value to encode
The green component value to encode
The blue component value to encode
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of the surface

remarks.para: This method maps the RGB color value to the surface's pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given RGB color value for the pixel format.
If the surface has a , the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has an alpha component, the encoded pixel value's alpha component value will be set to be fully opaque.

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.MapColor(System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Maps RGBA component values to an encoded pixel value for the surface
member.param: The red component value to encode
The green component value to encode
The blue component value to encode
The alpha component value to encode
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of the surface

remarks.para: This method maps the RGBA color value to the surface's pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given RGBA color value for the pixel format.
If the surface has a , the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has no alpha component, the given alpha component value will be ignored (as it will be in indexed formats with a ).

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

member.param: The Color<> to encoded
member.returns: An encoded pixel value in the pixel format of the surface

remarks.para: This method maps the Color<> to the surface's pixel format and returns the encoded pixel value best approximating the given Color<> for the pixel format.
If the surface has a , the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the pixel format has no alpha component, the given alpha component value will be ignored (as it will be in indexed formats with a ).

If the pixel format's bits-per-pixel value (color depth) is less than 32-bits per pixel then the unused upper bits of the returned encoded pixel value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bits per pixel format the returned encoded pixel value can be safely cast (truncated) to an value, and similarly to a value for an 8-bits per pixel formats).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryBlit(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}@,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}@)
member.summary: Tries to perform a fast blit from a specified source surface to this surface with clipping
member.param: The destination rectangle on this surface to blit onto. The width and the height are ignored and taken from instead.
The source surface to blit from
The source rectangle on the source surface to blit
member.returns: , if the blit was successful; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method blits a portion of the surface defined by to this surface at the position defined by while ensuring clipping by . The width and the height of are ignored and taken from instead. If you want to specify width and height for the destination rectangle, consider using instead.
Neither this surface nor the surface must be locked when calling this method!

The blitting semantics for surfaces with and without blending and color keying are defined as follows:
RGBA 🡒 RGB
's BlendMode is Blend
alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha); the 's ColorKey is ignored
's BlendMode is None
copy RGB; if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison
RGB 🡒 RGBA
's BlendMode is Blend
alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha); if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key
's BlendMode is None
copy RGB, set destination alpha to source per-surface alpha value; if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key
RGBA 🡒 RGBA
's BlendMode is Blend
alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha); the 's ColorKey is ignored
's BlendMode is None
copy all of RGBA; if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison
RGB 🡒 RGB
's BlendMode is Blend
alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha); if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key
's BlendMode is None
copy RGB; if the 's is set, only copy pixels that do not match the color key

member.summary: Tries to perform a 9-grid scaled blit from a specified source surface to this surface, which maybe of a different format
member.param: The destination rectangle on this surface to blit onto
The source surface to blit from
The source rectangle on the source surface to blit
The width, in pixels, of the left corners in
The width, in pixels, of the right corners in
The height, in pixels, of the top corners in
The height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in
The scale to use to transform the corners of into the corners of , or 0 for an unscaled blit
The scale mode to use for the blit
member.returns: , if the blit was successful; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The pixels in the source surface are split into a 3⨯3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of the . The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining portion of the .
The region specified by from the surface is used as the 9-grid source and scaled to fit into the region specified by on this surface.

remarks.para: The pixels in the source surface are split into a 3⨯3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of this surface. The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining portion of this surface.
The entire surface is used as the 9-grid source and scaled to fit into the entire area of this surface.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryConvert(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormat,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface@)
member.summary: Tries to create a copy of this surface converted to the specified format
member.param: The pixel format to convert the surface to
The converted surface, if this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the surface was successfully converted; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method is used to optimize images for faster repeat blitting. This is accomplished by converting the original and storing the result as a new surface. The new, optimized surface can then be used as the source for future blits, making them faster.
If you are converting to an indexed surface and want to map colors to a palette, you can use instead.
If the original surface has alternate images, the duplicated surface will have a reference to them as well.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryCreatePalette(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette@)
member.summary: Tries to create a new for this surface
member.param: The newly created palette, if this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the palette was created successfully; otherwise, (e.g. if the surface didn't have an index format, check for more information)

remarks.para: This function creates a palette compatible with the surface. The palette is then returned for you to modify, and the surface will automatically use the new palette in future operations.
Bitmap surfaces (with format or ) will have the palette initialized with 0 as white and 1 as black. Other surfaces will get a palette initialized with white in every entry.
If this method is called for on surface that already has a palette, a new palette will be created to replace it.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryCreateRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer{Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.Software}@)
member.summary: Tries to create a new software renderer for this surface
member.param: The resulting software renderer, if the method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the renderer was created successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method creates a software renderer that renders directly to this surface.
As such, the resulting renderer won't be associated with any window. You can also create software renderers for s using the appropriate methods on a instance.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryCreateRenderer(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Renderer{Sdl3Sharp.Video.Rendering.Drivers.Software}@,System.Nullable{Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ColorSpace},Sdl3Sharp.Properties)
member.summary: Tries to create a new software renderer for this surface
member.param: The resulting software renderer, if the method returns ; otherwise,

The color space to be used by renderer for presenting the output. The Direct3D 11, Direct3D 12, and Metal renderers support , which is a linear color space and supports HDR output. In that case, drawing still uses the sRGB color space, but individual values can go beyond 1.0 and floating point textures can be used for HDR content. If this parameter is (the default), the output color space defaults to .
Additional properties to use when creating the renderer
member.returns: , if the renderer was created successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: This method creates a software renderer that renders directly to this surface.
As such, the resulting renderer won't be associated with any window. You can also create software renderers for s using the appropriate methods on a instance.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.summary: Tries to read a single pixel from the surface at the specified coordinates

remarks.para: This method prioritizes correctness over speed: it is suitable for unit tests, but is not intended to be used in game engines.

Like , this method uses the entire 8-bit (0-255) range when converting color components from pixel formats with less than 8 bits per RGB component (e.g., a completely white pixel in 16-bit format would return (, , ) = (0xff, 0xff, 0xff), not (0xf8, 0xfc, 0xf8)).
If the surface has no alpha component, the lpha component value will be returned as 0xff (fully opaque).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryRotate(System.Single,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface@)
member.summary: Tries to create a copy of this surface rotated clockwise by the specified angle
member.param: The angle to rotate the surface clockwise, in degrees
The rotated surface, if this method returns ; otherwise,
member.returns: , if the surface was rotated successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: The of rotation can be negative for counter-clockwise rotation.

When the rotation isn't a multiple of 90°, the resulting surface is larger than the original, with the background filled in with the , if available, or the color (R = 255, G = 255, B = 255, A = 0) if not.

If surface has the property () set on it, the new copy will have the adjusted value set: if the value of the property is 90 and was 30, the new surface will have a property value of 60 (that is: to be upright vs. gravity, the surface needs to rotate 60° more). However, note that further rotations on the new surface in this example will produce unexpected results, since the image will have resized and padded to accommodate the not-90° angle.

remarks.para: Surfaces with a 24-bit, 32-bit and paletted 8-bit format get saved in the BMP directly. Other RGB formats with 8-bit or higher get converted to a 24-bit surface or, if they have an alpha mask or a , to a 32-bit surface before they are saved.
YUV and paletted 1-bit and 4-bit formats are not supported.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.TryUnsafeLock
member.summary: Tries to set up the surface for direct pixel access
member.returns: , if the surface was locked successfully; otherwise, (check for more information)

remarks.para: It's unsafe to access the property for surfaces that must be locked and are not currently locked. And it's only safe to do so, if this method returned .
Between calls to / , you can write to and read from , using the surface's . Once you are done accessing the surface, you should use to release it.

This method is meant to be used as part of the - - - - pattern, if you want to access the surface's pixel memory directly in a faster and more efficient way. If you're looking for a simpler and safer way to access the pixel memory, consider using instead.
Not all surfaces require locking. If the value of the property is , then you can read and write to the surface at any time, and the pixel format of the surface will not change.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_AddSurfaceAlternateImage(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Add an alternate version of a surface
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to update
A pointer to an alternate SDL_Surface to associate with this surface
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function adds an alternate version of this surface, usually used for content with high DPI representations like cursors or icons. The size, format, and content do not need to match the original surface, and these alternate versions will not be updated when the original surface changes.
This function adds a reference to the alternate version, so you should call SDL_DestroySurface() on the image after this call.
This function can be called on different threads with different surfaces.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_BlitSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*)
member.summary: Performs a fast blit from the source surface to the destination surface with clipping
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to be copied from
The SDL_Rect structure representing the rectangle to be copied, or NULL to copy the entire surface
The SDL_Surface structure that is the blit target

The SDL_Rect structure representing the x and y position in the destination surface, or NULL for (0,0). The width and height are ignored, and are copied from srcrect. If you want a specific width and height, you should use SDL_BlitSurfaceScaled().
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If either or are NULL, the entire surface ( or ) is copied while ensuring clipping to ->clip_rect.
The blit function should not be called on a locked surface.

The blit semantics for surfaces with and without blending and colorkey are defined as follows:
RGBA->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB, set destination alpha to source per-surface alpha value. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.
RGBA->RGBA: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source alpha-channel and per-surface alpha) SDL_SRCCOLORKEY ignored. Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy all of RGBA to the destination. if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the RGB values of the source color key, ignoring alpha in the comparison.
RGB->RGB: Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_BLEND: alpha-blend (using the source per-surface alpha) Source surface blend mode set to SDL_BLENDMODE_NONE: copy RGB. both: if SDL_SRCCOLORKEY set, only copy the pixels that do not match the source color key.
Only one thread should be using the and surfaces at any given time.

member.summary: Perform a scaled blit using the 9-grid algorithm to a destination surface, which may be of a different format.
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to be copied from
The SDL_Rect structure representing the rectangle to be used for the 9-grid, or NULL to use the entire surface
The width, in pixels, of the left corners in
The width, in pixels, of the right corners in
The height, in pixels, of the top corners in
The height, in pixels, of the bottom corners in
The scale used to transform the corner of into the corner of , or 0.0f for an unscaled blit
Scale algorithm to be used
The SDL_Surface structure that is the blit target
The SDL_Rect structure representing the target rectangle in the destination surface, or NULL to fill the entire surface
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The pixels in the source surface are split into a 3x3 grid, using the different corner sizes for each corner, and the sides and center making up the remaining pixels. The corners are then scaled using and fit into the corners of the destination rectangle. The sides and center are then stretched into place to cover the remaining destination rectangle.
Only one thread should be using the and surfaces at any given time.

member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_ConvertSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormat)
member.summary: Copy an existing surface to a new surface of the specified format
member.param: The existing SDL_Surface structure to convert
The new pixel format
member.returns: Returns the new SDL_Surface structure that is created or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function is used to optimize images for faster repeat blitting. This is accomplished by converting the original and storing the result as a new surface. The new, optimized surface can then be used as the source for future blits, making them faster.
If you are converting to an indexed surface and want to map colors to a palette, you can use SDL_ConvertSurfaceAndColorspace() instead.
If the original surface has alternate images, the new surface will have a reference to them as well.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_ConvertSurfaceAndColorspace(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormat,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.Palette.SDL_Palette*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.ColorSpace,System.UInt32)

The new pixel format
An optional palette to use for indexed formats, may be NULL
The new colorspace
An SDL_PropertiesID with additional color properties, or 0
member.returns: Returns the new SDL_Surface structure that is created or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function converts an existing surface to a new format and colorspace and returns the new surface. This will perform any pixel format and colorspace conversion needed.
If the original surface has alternate images, the new surface will have a reference to them as well.

member.summary: Allocate a new surface with a specific pixel format

The height of the surface
The SDL_PixelFormat for the new surface's pixel format

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_CreateSurfaceFrom(System.Int32,System.Int32,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Coloring.PixelFormat,System.Void*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Allocate a new surface with a specific pixel format and existing pixel data
member.param: The width of the surface
The height of the surface
The SDL_PixelFormat for the new surface's pixel format
A pointer to existing pixel data
The number of bytes between each row, including padding
member.returns: Returns the new SDL_Surface structure that is created or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: No copy is made of the pixel data. Pixel data is not managed automatically; you must free the surface before you free the pixel data.
Pitch is the offset in bytes from one row of pixels to the next, e.g. width*4 for SDL_PIXELFORMAT_RGBA8888.
You may pass NULL for pixels and 0 for pitch to create a surface that you will fill in with valid values later.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_CreateSurfacePalette(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Create a palette and associate it with a surface
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to update
member.returns: Returns a new SDL_Palette structure on success or NULL on failure (e.g. if the surface didn't have an index format); call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function creates a palette compatible with the provided surface. The palette is then returned for you to modify, and the surface will automatically use the new palette in future operations. You do not need to destroy the returned palette, it will be freed when the reference count reaches 0, usually when the surface is destroyed.

Bitmap surfaces (with format SDL_PIXELFORMAT_INDEX1LSB or SDL_PIXELFORMAT_INDEX1MSB) will have the palette initialized with 0 as white and 1 as black. Other surfaces will get a palette initialized with white in every entry.
If this function is called for a surface that already has a palette, a new palette will be created to replace it.

remarks.para: This returns all versions of a surface, with the surface being queried as the first element in the returned array.
Freeing the array of surfaces does not affect the surfaces in the array. They are still referenced by the surface being queried and will be cleaned up normally.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_GetSurfacePalette(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_GetSurfaceProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Get the properties associated with a surface.
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to query
member.returns: Returns a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
The following properties are understood by SDL:
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_SDR_WHITE_POINT_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the value of 100% diffuse white, with higher values being displayed in the High Dynamic Range headroom. This defaults to 203 for HDR10 surfaces and 1.0 for floating point surfaces.
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT
For HDR10 and floating point surfaces, this defines the maximum dynamic range used by the content, in terms of the SDR white point. This defaults to 0.0, which disables tone mapping.
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_TONEMAP_OPERATOR_STRING
The tone mapping operator used when compressing from a surface with high dynamic range to another with lower dynamic range. Currently this supports "chrome", which uses the same tone mapping that Chrome uses for HDR content, the form "*=N", where N is a floating point scale factor applied in linear space, and "none", which disables tone mapping. This defaults to "chrome".
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HOTSPOT_X_NUMBER
The hotspot pixel offset from the left edge of the image, if this surface is being used as a cursor.
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_HOTSPOT_Y_NUMBER
The hotspot pixel offset from the top edge of the image, if this surface is being used as a cursor.
SDL_PROP_SURFACE_ROTATION_FLOAT
The number of degrees a surface's data is meant to be rotated clockwise to make the image right-side up. Default 0. This is used by the camera API, if a mobile device is oriented differently than what its camera provides (i.e. - the camera always provides portrait images but the phone is being held in landscape orientation). Since SDL 3.4.0.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_LockSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Set up a surface for directly accessing the pixels
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure to be locked
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Between calls to SDL_LockSurface() / SDL_UnlockSurface(), you can write to and read from ->pixels, using the pixel format stored in ->format. Once you are done accessing the surface, you should use SDL_UnlockSurface() to release it.
Not all surfaces require locking. If SDL_MUSTLOCK() evaluates to 0, then you can read and write to the surface at any time, and the pixel format of the surface will not change.
The locking referred to by this function is making the pixels available for direct access, not thread-safe locking.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_MapSurfaceRGB(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Map an RGB triple to an opaque pixel value for a surface
member.param: The surface to use for the pixel format and palette
The red component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The green component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255
member.returns: Returns a pixel value

remarks.para: This function maps the RGB color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGB color value for the given pixel format.
If the surface has a palette, the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.
If the surface pixel format has an alpha component it will be returned as all 1 bits (fully opaque).

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_MapSurfaceRGBA(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte,System.Byte)
member.summary: Map an RGBA quadruple to a pixel value for a surface
member.param: The surface to use for the pixel format and palette
The red component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The green component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The blue component of the pixel in the range 0-255
The alpha component of the pixel in the range 0-255
member.returns: Returns a pixel value

remarks.para: This function maps the RGBA color value to the specified pixel format and returns the pixel value best approximating the given RGBA color value for the given pixel format.
If the surface pixel format has no alpha component the alpha value will be ignored (as it will be in formats with a palette).
If the surface has a palette, the index of the closest matching color in the palette will be returned.

If the pixel format bpp (color depth) is less than 32-bpp then the unused upper bits of the return value can safely be ignored (e.g., with a 16-bpp format the return value can be assigned to a Uint16, and similarly a Uint8 for an 8-bpp format).

true to convert from sRGB to linear space for the alpha multiplication, false to do multiplication in sRGB space

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_RotateSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,System.Single)
member.summary: Return a copy of a surface rotated clockwise a number of degrees
member.param: The surface to rotate
The rotation angle, in degrees
member.returns: Returns a rotated copy of the surface or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The angle of rotation can be negative for counter-clockwise rotation.
When the rotation isn't a multiple of 90 degrees, the resulting surface is larger than the original, with the background filled in with the colorkey, if available, or RGBA 255/255/255/0 if not.

If has the SDL_PROP_SURFACE_ROTATION_FLOAT property set on it, the new copy will have the adjusted value set: if the rotation property is 90 and was 30, the new surface will have a property value of 60 (that is: to be upright vs gravity, this surface needs to rotate 60 more degrees). However, note that further rotations on the new surface in this example will produce unexpected results, since the image will have resized and padded to accommodate the not-90 degree angle.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_SaveBMP(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Save a surface to a file in BMP format
member.param: The SDL_Surface structure containing the image to be saved
A file to save to
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Surfaces with a 24-bit, 32-bit and paletted 8-bit format get saved in the BMP directly. Other RGB formats with 8-bit or higher get converted to a 24-bit surface or, if they have an alpha mask or a colorkey, to a 32-bit surface before they are saved. YUV and paletted 1-bit and 4-bit formats are not supported.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_SaveBMP_IO(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*,Sdl3Sharp.IO.Stream.SDL_IOStream*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)

A data stream to save to
if true, calls SDL_CloseIO() on before returning, even in the case of an error
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Surfaces with a 24-bit, 32-bit and paletted 8-bit format get saved in the BMP directly. Other RGB formats with 8-bit or higher get converted to a 24-bit surface or, if they have an alpha mask or a colorkey, to a 32-bit surface before they are saved. YUV and paletted 1-bit and 4-bit formats are not supported.

The alpha value multiplied into blit operations

The SDL_Rect structure representing the clipping rectangle, or NULL to disable clipping

true to enable color key, false to disable color key
the transparent pixel
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The color key defines a pixel value that will be treated as transparent in a blit. For example, one can use this to specify that cyan pixels should be considered transparent, and therefore not rendered.
It is a pixel of the format used by the surface, as generated by SDL_MapRGB().

srcC = srcC * (color / 255)
