member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_GetWindowProperties(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Gets the properties associated with a window
member.param: The window to query
member.returns: Returns a valid property ID on success or 0 on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para:
The following read-only properties are provided by SDL:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_SHAPE_POINTER The surface associated with a shaped window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_HDR_ENABLED_BOOLEAN
true if the window has HDR headroom above the SDR white point. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_SDR_WHITE_LEVEL_FLOAT
The value of SDR white in the SDL_COLORSPACE_SRGB_LINEAR colorspace. On Windows this corresponds to the SDR white level in scRGB colorspace, and on Apple platforms this is always 1.0 for EDR content. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_HDR_HEADROOM_FLOAT
The additional high dynamic range that can be displayed, in terms of the SDR white point. When HDR is not enabled, this will be 1.0. This property can change dynamically when SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_HDR_STATE_CHANGED is sent.
On Android:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_ANDROID_WINDOW_POINTER The ANativeWindow associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_ANDROID_SURFACE_POINTER The EGLSurface associated with the window
On iOS:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_UIKIT_WINDOW_POINTER The (__unsafe_unretained) UIWindow associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_UIKIT_METAL_VIEW_TAG_NUMBER The NSInteger tag associated with metal views on the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_UIKIT_OPENGL_FRAMEBUFFER_NUMBER The OpenGL view's framebuffer object. It must be bound when rendering to the screen using OpenGL.
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_UIKIT_OPENGL_RENDERBUFFER_NUMBER The OpenGL view's renderbuffer object. It must be bound when SDL_GL_SwapWindow is called.
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_UIKIT_OPENGL_RESOLVE_FRAMEBUFFER_NUMBER The OpenGL view's resolve framebuffer, when MSAA is used
On KMS/DRM:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_KMSDRM_DEVICE_INDEX_NUMBER The device index associated with the window (e.g. the X in /dev/dri/cardX)
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_KMSDRM_DRM_FD_NUMBER The DRM FD associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_KMSDRM_GBM_DEVICE_POINTER The GBM device associated with the window
On macOS:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_COCOA_WINDOW_POINTER The (__unsafe_unretained) NSWindow associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_COCOA_METAL_VIEW_TAG_NUMBER The NSInteger tag associated with metal views on the window
On OpenVR:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_OPENVR_OVERLAY_ID_NUMBER The OpenVR Overlay Handle ID for the associated overlay window
On QNX:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_QNX_WINDOW_POINTER The screen_window_t associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_QNX_SURFACE_POINTER The EGLSurface associated with the window
On Vivante:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_VIVANTE_DISPLAY_POINTER The EGLNativeDisplayType associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_VIVANTE_WINDOW_POINTER The EGLNativeWindowType associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_VIVANTE_SURFACE_POINTER The EGLSurface associated with the window
On Windows:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WIN32_HWND_POINTER The HWND associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WIN32_HDC_POINTER The HDC associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WIN32_INSTANCE_POINTER The HINSTANCE associated with the window
On Wayland:
Note: The xdg_* window objects do not internally persist across window show/hide calls. They will be null if the window is hidden and must be queried each time it is shown.
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_DISPLAY_POINTER The wl_display associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_SURFACE_POINTER The wl_surface associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_VIEWPORT_POINTER The wp_viewport associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_EGL_WINDOW_POINTER The wl_egl_window associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_XDG_SURFACE_POINTER The xdg_surface associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_XDG_TOPLEVEL_POINTER The xdg_toplevel role associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_XDG_TOPLEVEL_EXPORT_HANDLE_STRING The export handle associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_XDG_POPUP_POINTER The xdg_popup role associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_WAYLAND_XDG_POSITIONER_POINTER The xdg_positioner associated with the window, in popup mode
On X11:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_X11_DISPLAY_POINTER The X11 Display associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_X11_SCREEN_NUMBER The screen number associated with the window
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_X11_WINDOW_NUMBER The X11 Window associated with the window
On Emscripten:
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_EMSCRIPTEN_CANVAS_ID_STRING The id the canvas element will have
SDL_PROP_WINDOW_EMSCRIPTEN_KEYBOARD_ELEMENT_STRING The keyboard element that associates keyboard events to this window
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_GetWindows(System.Int32*)
member.summary: Gets a list of valid windows
member.param: A pointer filled in with the number of windows returned, may be NULL
member.returns: Returns a NULL terminated array of SDL_Window pointers or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information. This is a single allocation that should be freed with SDL_free() when it is no longer needed.

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_GetWindowSafeArea(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*)
member.summary: Gets the safe area for this window
member.param: The window to query
A pointer filled in with the client area that is safe for interactive content
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Some devices have portions of the screen which are partially obscured or not interactive, possibly due to on-screen controls, curved edges, camera notches, TV overscan, etc. This function provides the area of the window which is safe to have interactable content. You should continue rendering into the rest of the window, but it should not contain visually important or interactable content.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_GetWindowSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32*,System.Int32*)
member.summary: Gets the size of a window's client area
member.param: The window to query the width and height from
A pointer filled in with the width of the window, may be NULL
A pointer filled in with the height of the window, may be NULL
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The window pixel size may differ from its window coordinate size if the window is on a high pixel density display. Use SDL_GetWindowSizeInPixels() or SDL_GetRenderOutputSize() to get the real client area size in pixels.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.summary: Get the size of a window's client area, in pixels
member.param: The window from which the drawable size should be queried
A pointer to variable for storing the width in pixels, may be NULL

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_HideWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Hides a window
member.param: The window to hide
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_MaximizeWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Requests that the window be made as large as possible
member.param: The window to maximize
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Non-resizable windows can't be maximized. The window must have the SDL_WINDOW_RESIZABLE flag set, or this will have no effect.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_MAXIMIZED event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.

When maximizing a window, whether the constraints set via SDL_SetWindowMaximumSize() are honored depends on the policy of the window manager. Win32 and macOS enforce the constraints when maximizing, while X11 and Wayland window managers may vary.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_MinimizeWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Requests that the window be minimized to an iconic representation
member.param: The window to minimize
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the window is in a fullscreen state, this request has no direct effect. It may alter the state the window is returned to when leaving fullscreen.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_MINIMIZED event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_RaiseWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Requests that a window be raised above other windows and gain the input focus
member.param: The window to raise
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The result of this request is subject to desktop window manager policy, particularly if raising the requested window would result in stealing focus from another application. If the window is successfully raised and gains input focus, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_FOCUS_GAINED event will be emitted, and the window will have the SDL_WINDOW_INPUT_FOCUS flag set.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_RestoreWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Requests that the size and position of a minimized or maximized window be restored
member.param: The window to restore
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the window is in a fullscreen state, this request has no direct effect. It may alter the state the window is returned to when leaving fullscreen.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the window state changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESTORED event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, the windowing system can deny the state change.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowAlwaysOnTop(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets the window to always be above the others
member.param: The window of which to change the always on top state
true to set the window always on top, false to disable
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This will add or remove the window's SDL_WINDOW_ALWAYS_ON_TOP flag. This will bring the window to the front and keep the window above the rest.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowAspectRatio(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Requests that the aspect ratio of a window's client area be set
member.param: The window to change
The minimum aspect ratio of the window, or 0.0f for no limit
The maximum aspect ratio of the window, or 0.0f for no limit
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The aspect ratio is the ratio of width divided by height, e.g. 2560x1600 would be 1.6. Larger aspect ratios are wider and smaller aspect ratios are narrower.
If, at the time of this request, the window in a fixed-size state, such as maximized or fullscreen, the request will be deferred until the window exits this state and becomes resizable again.

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new window state may not have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window size changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED event will be emitted with the new window dimensions. Note that the new dimensions may not match the exact aspect ratio requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the window size in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the size of the content area to remain within the usable desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowBordered(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets the border state of a window
member.param: The window of which to change the border state
false to remove border, true to add border
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This will add or remove the window's SDL_WINDOW_BORDERLESS flag and add or remove the border from the actual window. This is a no-op if the window's border already matches the requested state.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowFocusable(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Set whether the window may have input focus
member.param: The window to set focusable state
true to allow input focus, false to not allow input focus
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowFullscreen(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Requests that the window's fullscreen state be changed
member.param: The window to change
true for fullscreen mode, false for windowed mode
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: By default a window in fullscreen state uses borderless fullscreen desktop mode, but a specific exclusive display mode can be set using SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode().

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new fullscreen state may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window state changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_ENTER_FULLSCREEN or SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_LEAVE_FULLSCREEN event will be emitted. Note that, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.DisplayMode.SDL_DisplayMode*)
member.summary: Sets the display mode to use when a window is visible and fullscreen
member.param: The window to affect

A pointer to the display mode to use, which can be NULL for borderless fullscreen desktop mode, or one of the fullscreen modes returned by SDL_GetFullscreenDisplayModes() to set an exclusive fullscreen mode
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This only affects the display mode used when the window is fullscreen. To change the window size when the window is not fullscreen, use SDL_SetWindowSize().

If the window is currently in the fullscreen state, this request is asynchronous on some windowing systems and the new mode dimensions may not be applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.
When the new mode takes effect, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED and/or an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_PIXEL_SIZE_CHANGED event will be emitted with the new mode dimensions.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowHitTest(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,,System.Void*)
member.summary: Provides a callback that decides if a window region has special properties
member.param: The window to set hit-testing on
The function to call when doing a hit-test
An app-defined void pointer passed to
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Normally windows are dragged and resized by decorations provided by the system window manager (a title bar, borders, etc), but for some apps, it makes sense to drag them from somewhere else inside the window itself; for example, one might have a borderless window that wants to be draggable from any part, or simulate its own title bar, etc.

This function lets the app provide a callback that designates pieces of a given window as special. This callback is run during event processing if we need to tell the OS to treat a region of the window specially; the use of this callback is known as "hit testing."

Mouse input may not be delivered to your application if it is within a special area; the OS will often apply that input to moving the window or resizing the window and not deliver it to the application.
Specifying NULL for a callback disables hit-testing. Hit-testing is disabled by default.
Platforms that don't support this functionality will return false unconditionally, even if you're attempting to disable hit-testing.

Your callback may fire at any time, and its firing does not indicate any specific behavior (for example, on Windows, this certainly might fire when the OS is deciding whether to drag your window, but it fires for lots of other reasons, too, some unrelated to anything you probably care about and when the mouse isn't actually at the location it is testing). Since this can fire at any time, you should try to keep your callback efficient, devoid of allocations, etc.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowIcon(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Sets the icon for a window
member.param: The window to change
An SDL_Surface structure containing the icon for the window
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If this function is passed a surface with alternate representations added using SDL_AddSurfaceAlternateImage(), the surface will be interpreted as the content to be used for 100% display scale, and the alternate representations will be used for high DPI situations. For example, if the original surface is 32x32, then on a 2x macOS display or 200% display scale on Windows, a 64x64 version of the image will be used, if available. If a matching version of the image isn't available, the closest larger size image will be downscaled to the appropriate size and be used instead, if available. Otherwise, the closest smaller image will be upscaled and be used instead.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowKeyboardGrab(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets a window's keyboard grab mode
member.param: The window for which the keyboard grab mode should be set
This is true to grab keyboard, and false to release
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Keyboard grab enables capture of system keyboard shortcuts like Alt+Tab or the Meta/Super key. Note that not all system keyboard shortcuts can be captured by applications (one example is Ctrl+Alt+Del on Windows).
This is primarily intended for specialized applications such as VNC clients or VM frontends. Normal games should not use keyboard grab.

When keyboard grab is enabled, SDL will continue to handle Alt+Tab when the window is full-screen to ensure the user is not trapped in your application. If you have a custom keyboard shortcut to exit fullscreen mode, you may suppress this behavior with SDL_HINT_ALLOW_ALT_TAB_WHILE_GRABBED.
If the caller enables a grab while another window is currently grabbed, the other window loses its grab in favor of the caller's window.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowMaximumSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the maximum size of a window's client area
member.param: The window to change
The maximum width of the window, or 0 for no limit
The maximum height of the window, or 0 for no limit.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowMinimumSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the minimum size of a window's client area
member.param: The window to change
The minimum width of the window, or 0 for no limit
The minimum height of the window, or 0 for no limit.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowModal(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Toggles the state of the window as modal
member.param: The window on which to set the modal state
true to toggle modal status on, false to toggle it off
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: To enable modal status on a window, the window must currently be the child window of a parent, or toggling modal status on will fail.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowMouseGrab(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets a window's mouse grab mode
member.param: The window for which the mouse grab mode should be set
This is true to grab mouse, and false to release
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Mouse grab confines the mouse cursor to the window.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowMouseRect(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*)
member.summary: Confines the cursor to the specified area of a window
member.param: The window that will be associated with the barrier
A rectangle area in window-relative coordinates. If NULL the barrier for the specified window will be destroyed.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: Note that this does NOT grab the cursor, it only defines the area a cursor is restricted to when the window has mouse focus.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowOpacity(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets the opacity for a window
member.param: The window which will be made transparent or opaque
The opacity value (0.0f - transparent, 1.0f - opaque)
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: The parameter will be clamped internally between 0.0f (transparent) and 1.0f (opaque).
This function also returns false if setting the opacity isn't supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowParent(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Sets the window as a child of a parent window
member.param: The window that should become the child of a parent
The new parent window for the child window
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the window is already the child of an existing window, it will be reparented to the new owner. Setting the parent window to NULL unparents the window and removes child window status.

If a parent window is hidden or destroyed, the operation will be recursively applied to child windows. Child windows hidden with the parent that did not have their hidden status explicitly set will be restored when the parent is shown.
Attempting to set the parent of a window that is currently in the modal state will fail. Use SDL_SetWindowModal() to cancel the modal status before attempting to change the parent.
Popup windows cannot change parents and attempts to do so will fail.
Setting a parent window that is currently the sibling or descendent of the child window results in undefined behavior.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowPosition(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Requests that the window's position be set
member.param: The window to reposition
The x coordinate of the window, or SDL_WINDOWPOS_CENTERED or SDL_WINDOWPOS_UNDEFINED
The y coordinate of the window, or SDL_WINDOWPOS_CENTERED or SDL_WINDOWPOS_UNDEFINED
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the window is in an exclusive fullscreen or maximized state, this request has no effect.

This can be used to reposition fullscreen-desktop windows onto a different display, however, as exclusive fullscreen windows are locked to a specific display, they can only be repositioned programmatically via SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode().

On some windowing systems this request is asynchronous and the new coordinates may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window position changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_MOVED event will be emitted with the window's new coordinates. Note that the new coordinates may not match the exact coordinates requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the position of the window in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the position so the window is always within desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowProgressState(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.ProgressState)
member.summary: Sets the state of the progress bar for the given window’s taskbar icon.
member.param: The window whose progress state is to be modified
The progress state. SDL_PROGRESS_STATE_NONE stops displaying the progress bar.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowProgressValue(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Single)
member.summary: Sets the value of the progress bar for the given window’s taskbar icon.
member.param: The window whose progress value is to be modified
The progress value in the range of [0.0f - 1.0f]. If the value is outside the valid range, it gets clamped.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowResizable(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets the user-resizable state of a window.
member.param: The window of which to change the resizable state
true to allow resizing, false to disallow
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This will add or remove the window's SDL_WINDOW_RESIZABLE flag and allow/disallow user resizing of the window. This is a no-op if the window's resizable state already matches the requested state.
You can't change the resizable state of a fullscreen window.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowShape(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Surface.SDL_Surface*)
member.summary: Sets the shape of a transparent window
member.param: The window
The surface representing the shape of the window, or NULL to remove any current shape
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This sets the alpha channel of a transparent window and any fully transparent areas are also transparent to mouse clicks. If you are using something besides the SDL render API, then you are responsible for drawing the alpha channel of the window to match the shape alpha channel to get consistent cross-platform results.

The shape is copied inside this function, so you can free it afterwards. If your shape surface changes, you should call SDL_SetWindowShape() again to update the window. This is an expensive operation, so should be done sparingly.
The window must have been created with the SDL_WINDOW_TRANSPARENT flag.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowSize(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Requests that the size of a window's client area be set
member.param: The window to change
The width of the window, must be > 0
Rhe height of the window, must be > 0
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: If the window is in a fullscreen or maximized state, this request has no effect.
To change the exclusive fullscreen mode of a window, use SDL_SetWindowFullscreenMode().

On some windowing systems, this request is asynchronous and the new window size may not have have been applied immediately upon the return of this function. If an immediate change is required, call SDL_SyncWindow() to block until the changes have taken effect.

When the window size changes, an SDL_EVENT_WINDOW_RESIZED event will be emitted with the new window dimensions. Note that the new dimensions may not match the exact size requested, as some windowing systems can restrict the window size in certain scenarios (e.g. constraining the size of the content area to remain within the usable desktop bounds). Additionally, as this is just a request, it can be denied by the windowing system.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SetWindowTitle(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Byte*)
member.summary: Sets the title of a window
member.param: The window to change
The desired window title in UTF-8 format
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This string is expected to be in UTF-8 encoding.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.summary: Shows a window

member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_ShowWindowSystemMenu(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Displays the system-level window menu
member.param: The window for which the menu will be displayed
The x coordinate of the menu, relative to the origin (top-left) of the client area
The y coordinate of the menu, relative to the origin (top-left) of the client area.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This default window menu is provided by the system and on some platforms provides functionality for setting or changing privileged state on the window, such as moving it between workspaces or displays, or toggling the always-on-top property.
On platforms or desktops where this is unsupported, this function does nothing.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_SyncWindow(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Blocks until any pending window state is finalized
member.param: The window for which to wait for the pending state to be applied
member.returns: Returns true on success or false if the operation timed out before the window was in the requested state

remarks.para: On asynchronous windowing systems, this acts as a synchronization barrier for pending window state. It will attempt to wait until any pending window state has been applied and is guaranteed to return within finite time. Note that for how long it can potentially block depends on the underlying window system, as window state changes may involve somewhat lengthy animations that must complete before the window is in its final requested state.
On windowing systems where changes are immediate, this does nothing.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.summary: Returns whether the window has a surface associated with it
member.param: The window to query
member.returns: Returns true if there is a surface associated with the window, or false otherwise

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods and properties for and various driver-specific implementations of

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowExtensions.SDL_SetWindowFillDocument(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Internal.Interop.CBool)
member.summary: Sets the window to fill the current document space (Emscripten only)
member.param: The window of which to change the fill-document state
true to set the window to fill the document, false to disable
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This will add or remove the window's SDL_WINDOW_FILL_DOCUMENT flag.
Currently this flag only applies to the Emscripten target.

When enabled, the canvas element fills the entire document. Resize events will be generated as the browser window is resized, as that will adjust the canvas size as well. The canvas will cover anything else on the page, including any controls provided by Emscripten in its generated HTML file (in fact, any elements on the page that aren't the canvas will be moved into a hidden div element).

Often times this is desirable for a browser-based game, but it means several things that we expect of an SDL window on other platforms might not work as expected, such as minimum window sizes and aspect ratios.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.summary: The name of a property used when creating a Window<Emscripten> that holds the identifier to use to override the binding element for keyboard inputs for the canvas element used for the window

remarks.para:
The value of the associated property can be one of the following:
"#window" The javascript window object. This is the default.
"#document" The javascript document object
"#screen" The javascript window.screen object
"#canvas" The WebGL canvas element
"#none" Don't bind anything at all
Any other string without a leading # character Bind to the element on the page with that ID

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowExtensions.<G>$AEFFE458B33F2A75B39704E7F48C8724.IsFillDocument
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the window should be fill the current document space
member.value: A value indicating whether the window should be fill the current document space

remarks.para: When fill-document mode is enabled, the canvas element fills the entire document. Resize events will be generated as the browser window is resized, as that will adjust the canvas size as well. The canvas will cover anything else on the page, including any controls provided by Emscripten in its generated HTML file (in fact, any elements on the page that aren't the canvas will be moved into a hidden div element).

Often times this is desirable for a browser-based game, but it means several things that are usually expected to work for windows on other platforms might not work as expected, such as minimum window sizes and aspect ratios.
The value of this property is reflected as the flag in the property of the window.
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information)

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowPosition
member.summary: Represents a window position, which can be either a definite window coordinate value, a centered position, or an undefined position

remarks.para: If the represents a centered position or an undefined position, it can be optionally associated with a specific where the centered or undefined position is applicable. Otherwise, if the isn't associated with any specific , then the centered or undefined position is applicable to the primary display.

You can use the , , and methods to determine whether a represents a definite window coordinate value, a centered position, or an undefined position, and to extract the associated value or , if any, accordingly.

member.value: A representing a centered position on the primary display

remarks.para: If you want to specify a centered position on a specific display, you can use the method instead.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowPosition.CenteredOn(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Display)
member.summary: Gets a representing a centered position on the specified display
member.param: The display on which to center the window coordinate
member.returns: A representing a centered position on the specified display

remarks.para: If you want to specify a centered position on the primary display, you can use the property instead.

s used by this method are technically limited to having an of 65535 or less. However, in practice, this shouldn't be an issue since display ids very rarely exceed this value. Note that this method doesn't fail if given a display with an id larger than 65535, it instead always just takes the lower 16 bits of the display id.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowPosition.Undefined
member.summary: Gets a representing an undefined position on the primary display
member.value: A representing an undefined position on the primary display

remarks.para: This can be used to represent a "don't know" or "don't care" position, which is always going to be treated as if it were on the primary display. If you want to specify an undefined position on a specific display, you can use the method instead.

member.returns: A representing an undefined position on the specified display

s used by this method are technically limited to having an of 65535 or less. However, in practice, this shouldn't be an issue since display ids very rarely exceed this value. Note that this method doesn't fail if given a display with an id larger than 65535, it instead always just takes the lower 16 bits of the display id.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowPosition.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new representing the specified definite window coordinate
member.param: The definite window coordinate value

remarks.para: This constructor can be used to create a with a specific, definite window coordinate for its value.

s passed to specify the window coordinate are technically limited to not be in the range from 536805376 (0x1FFF0000) to 805306367 (0x2FFFFFFF) (inclusive). However, in practice, this shouldn't be an issue since window coordinate values very rarely are within this range. Note that this constructor doesn't fail if a given value is within this range, it instead just accepts the given value as is. This means that, given such a value, the resulting may represent false or invalid window positions.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface
member.summary: Represents the surface of a window

remarks.para: s (coming from the property of a ) can be used as an alternative to using a on the window.
Do not attempt to use a together with a on the same ! They are meant to be used mutually exclusively for the same .
For the most part s are not thread-safe, and most of their properties and methods should only be accessed from the main thread!

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.IsValid
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether the surface is still valid
member.value: A value indicating whether the surface is still valid

remarks.para:
A can become invalid:
If it is disposed
If the associated is resized
If the associated is disposed/destroyed
In any of these cases, the property of the associated will reference a new instance that is valid and can be used instead of the invalid one.
Do not attempt to use an invalid ! You are responsible for ensuring that you only use valid instances.

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.VSync
member.summary: Gets or sets the vertical synchronization (VSync) mode or interval for the surface of a window
member.value: The vertical synchronization (VSync) mode or interval for the surface of a window

remarks.para: You can set the value of this property to to disable VSync, to enable late swap tearing (adaptive VSync) if supported, or use the method to specify a custom VSync interval. You can specify a custom interval of 1 to synchronize the surface with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize it with every second vertical refresh, and so on.
When a window surface is newly created, the value of this property defaults to .
This property should only be accessed from the main thread.

member.exception: When setting or getting this property, SDL failed with an error (check for more information)

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.TryUpdate
member.summary: Tries to copy the entirety of the window surface to the screen
member.returns: , if the window surface was successfully copied to the screen; otherwise, (call for more information about the failure)

remarks.para: This method tries to copy the present window surface's content to the screen, in order to display it as the associated 's visible content. To reflect any changes made to the surface, you need to call this method to update the window's content on the screen.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.TryUpdateRects(System.ReadOnlySpan{Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}})
member.summary: Tries to copy the specified areas of the window surface to the screen
member.param: The list of areas of the window surface copy
member.returns: , if at least the specified areas of the window surface were successfully copied to the screen; otherwise, (call for more information about the failure)

remarks.para: This method tries to copy the present window surface's content to the screen, in order to display it as the associated 's visible content. To reflect any changes made to the surface, you need to call this method to update the window's content on the screen.

Note that this method will update at least the specified areas, but this is only intended as an optimization; in practice, this might update more of the screen (or even the entirety of all of the screen), depending on the method used by SDL to send pixels to the system.
This method should only be called from the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_DestroyWindowSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Destroys the surface associated with the window
member.param: The window to update
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_GetWindowSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Gets the SDL surface associated with the window
member.param: The window to query
member.returns: Returns the surface associated with the window, or NULL on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information.

remarks.para: A new surface will be created with the optimal format for the window, if necessary. This surface will be freed when the window is destroyed. Do not free this surface.
This surface will be invalidated if the window is resized. After resizing a window this function must be called again to return a valid surface.
You may not combine this with 3D or the rendering API on this window.
This function is affected by SDL_HINT_FRAMEBUFFER_ACCELERATION.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_GetWindowSurfaceVSync(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurfaceVSync*)
member.summary: Gets VSync for the window surface
member.param: The window to query
An int filled with the current vertical refresh sync interval. See SDL_SetWindowSurfaceVSync() for the meaning of the value.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_SetWindowSurfaceVSync(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurfaceVSync)
member.summary: Toggles VSync for the window surface
member.param: The window
The vertical refresh sync interval.
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: When a window surface is created, vsync defaults to SDL_WINDOW_SURFACE_VSYNC_DISABLED.

The parameter can be 1 to synchronize present with every vertical refresh, 2 to synchronize present with every second vertical refresh, etc., SDL_WINDOW_SURFACE_VSYNC_ADAPTIVE for late swap tearing (adaptive vsync), or SDL_WINDOW_SURFACE_VSYNC_DISABLED to disable. Not every value is supported by every driver, so you should check the return value to see whether the requested setting is supported.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_UpdateWindowSurface(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*)
member.summary: Copies the window surface to the screen
member.param: The window to update
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This is the function you use to reflect any changes to the surface on the screen.
This function is equivalent to the SDL 1.2 API SDL_Flip().
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: M:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.WindowSurface.SDL_UpdateWindowSurfaceRects(Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window.SDL_Window*,Sdl3Sharp.Video.Drawing.Rect{System.Int32}*,System.Int32)
member.summary: Copies areas of the window surface to the screen
member.param: The window to update
An array of SDL_Rect structures representing areas of the surface to copy, in pixels
The number of rectangles
member.returns: Returns true on success or false on failure; call SDL_GetError() for more information

remarks.para: This is the function you use to reflect changes to portions of the surface on the screen.
This function is equivalent to the SDL 1.2 API SDL_UpdateRects().

Note that this function will update at least the rectangles specified, but this is only intended as an optimization; in practice, this might update more of the screen (or all of the screen!), depending on what method SDL uses to send pixels to the system.
This function should only be called on the main thread.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.Video.Windowing.Window`1
member.summary: Represents a window

remarks.para: A window is used to display content on the screen and receive input from the user.

To display content in a window, you can either use a , constructed from any of the TryCreateRenderer* methods (e.g. or from the methods, or use the property to get a you can draw on. Using either one mutually excludes the other.
If you use a in conjunction with a , please make sure that you don't use it after the window has been disposed. The recommended approach is to dispose the renderer before the window.
Windows can be either top-level or be children of other windows. To make a window a child of another window, you just need to set its property.
Special rules apply for child windows: they will be hidden with their parent and will be destroyed when their parent gets destroyed.

You can create new using the method, but be aware that if the currently active windowing driver doesn't match the specified one, this method will fail. Alternatively, you can create new popup windows using the method. This method will create the new window as a child window of the window the method was called on.

Additionally, there are some driver-specific methods for creating windows, such as Wayland, Windows, Cocoa, and some more. Similar rules apply to these methods as well: if the currently active windowing driver doesn't match the specified one, these method will fail.
For the most part s are not thread-safe, and most of their properties and methods should only be accessed from the main thread!
s are concrete window types, associated with a specific windowing driver.
If you want to use them in a more general way, you can use them as instances, which serve as common abstractions.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.FormattedConstantAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the attributed static partial method or property returns a compile-time formatted constant string
member.param: The format string, using the same syntax as
The arguments to substitute into the format string placeholders

remarks.para: The source generator calls (or an overload with a culture-specific if is set) at compile time and generates an implementation that returns the result as a literal constant.

The target must be a static partial method or a static partial property with only a get accessor. Supported return types are:
string or string?Returns a string literal or
ReadOnlySpan<char>Returns a string literal or []
ReadOnlySpan<byte>Returns a UTF-8 string literal or []

example.code:
[FormattedConstant("Hello, {0}!", "World")] static partial string GetGreeting(); // Returns "Hello, World!"
[FormattedConstant("{0:N2}", 1234.5, Culture = FormatCulture.Invariant)] static partial ReadOnlySpan<byte> GetFormattedNumber(); // Returns "1,234.50"u8
[FormattedConstant("{0}", nameof(MyClass))] static partial ReadOnlySpan<char> GetClassName(); // Returns "MyClass"

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportSymbolKind
member.summary: Controls the kind of access to an imported native symbol

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportSymbolKind.Auto
member.summary: Automatically chooses between Getter and Setter based off the target method's declaration signature
member.remarks: Reference is not considered when using Auto

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportSymbolKind.Getter
member.summary: Provides getter access to the imported native symbol
member.remarks: If the target method returns by ref or ref readonly, then the imported native symbol is considered to be of a by-ref type to underlying type itself. This is in contrast to how Reference is handled.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportSymbolKind.Setter
member.summary: Provides setter access to the imported native symbol
member.remarks: If the target method accepts a ref or ref readonly/in parameter, then the imported native symbol is considered to be of a by-ref type to underlying type itself. NOTE: When using such a setter, the provided reference is temporarily fixed and if the provided reference is managed, its address might unnoticeably change later on. This is in contrast to how Reference is handled.

member.name: F:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportSymbolKind.Reference
member.summary: Provides reference access to the imported native symbol
member.remarks: The target methods needs to return by ref or ref readonly and the imported native symbol is considered to be of the underlying type of the by-ref return. This is in contrast to how Getter and Setter are handled.

member.param: The libraryName part of the result of the last call to GetLibraryNameAndSearchPath and the name of the native library that should have been imported. This value might be , or an empty or whitespace-only string, in which case might be and the error to handle is an invalid library name.
The searchPath part of the result of the last call to GetLibraryNameAndSearchPath and the search path of the native library that should have been imported. This value might be .

member.returns: if the error handling is considered successful and another attempt to import the native library is made by firstly calling GetLibraryNameAndSearchPath again; otherwise , in which case no more attempts to import the native library are made and all subsequent symbol imports from this native library are skipped

member.name: P:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportFunctionAttribute`1.CallConvs
member.summary: Gets or initializes a collection of types indicating calling conventions for the imported native function
member.value: A collection of types indicating calling conventions for the imported native function. If omitted or empty, the the default platform calling convention is used instead.

member.name: T:Sdl3Sharp.SourceGeneration.NativeImportFunctionAttribute`2
member.summary: Indicates that the attributed target method serves as an entry point to an conditionally imported native function
member.typeparam: The type that represent the native library that should get imported
The type that represent the condition that determines if a native symbol should get imported or not
member.remarks: NOTE: when a native symbol is not imported (the given condition evaluates to ), calling the attributed target method results in at least undefined and erroneous behavior. No further indicating is given if a native symbol is not imported.
